Hope you're well. Quick follow-up on Invoice 9211 (due 2027-12-05). We want to make sure you have the easiest route to settle: ACH, wire, or virtual card (if treasury prefers). If wire, I can loop in our finance team with the bank stub and target cutoff windows.

If there are any PO or approval hangups, can you share the expected payment date so we can align downstream entitlements? If you prefer, I can move this to an AP-only thread and include our accounts contact.

Thanks — appreciate the quick confirmation.

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ethan@redwood.com
(415) 555-0192

Attachment: none


From: Sara Liao <sara.liao@medicahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: MedicaHealth Billing <payments@medicahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2027 09:05:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Cashflow commitment check-in — Invoice 9211

Hi Ethan —

Thanks for the ping. We're targeting payment early next week, but Treasury asked for a few clarifications before they schedule the wire:

- Can you confirm Redwood's wire instructions (SWIFT + ABA or IBAN) so they can set up the outbound transfer?
- Our AP team flagged VAT handling: do you have an EU VAT invoice variant or evidence of tax treatment we can attach? We're reconciling across entities.
- PO attached (PO_8793.pdf) — is the invoice mapping OK for reconciliation?

If we can get the bank stub and a VAT note, they should be able to push the wire on 2027-12-03 (company cutoffs permitting). If a virtual card is faster, we can explore that too.

Sara
Sara Liao
Senior AP Manager, MedicaHealth
sara.liao@medicahealth.com
Attachment: PO_8793.pdf (PDF, 34KB)


From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Liao <sara.liao@medicahealth.com>, MedicaHealth Billing <payments@medicahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2027 09:20:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: Cashflow commitment check-in — Invoice 9211

Laura / Ben — forwarding Sara's note from MedicaHealth. Can you confirm the bank wire stub (ABA/SWIFT) and whether we can produce a VAT statement or EU-friendly invoice PDF? PO is attached on their side.

If Treasury needs a billing contact for a quick phone auth, I'm happy to jump on the call — they mentioned a potential 12/03 wire.

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Sara Liao <sara.liao@medicahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2027 09:05:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Cashflow commitment check-in — Invoice 9211
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: MedicaHealth Billing <payments@medicahealth.com>

[see original message and attachment PO_8793.pdf]

----------

Ethan

Attachment: none


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liao <sara.liao@medicahealth.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2027 10:00:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Cashflow commitment check-in — Invoice 9211 (wire details + VAT note)

Hi Sara —

Thanks for the heads-up. Bank details and a copy of Invoice 9211 are attached. Quick points:

- For US wires: ABA 021000021, Account 412345678 (Redwood Inference, Inc.)
- For international wires: Bank of Westbridge, SWIFT BWSTUS33, IBAN: US00BWST000412345678
- ACH is also accepted (Account: 412345678, Routing: 021000021) — please note ACH settlement is typically T+1.
- Cutoff for same-day credit (wire) is 17:00 ET for most US banks; SWIFT settlement depends on intermediary banks.

VAT/tax: Redwood is a US-headquartered entity and does not issue EU VAT-charged invoices. We can provide a tax-exempt statement and a billing PDF that shows net amounts and our US tax id for your reconciliation. If MedicaHealth needs a specific VAT form, let us know the required fields and we'll generate a tailored invoice PDF.

I've attached: bank_details.pdf (bank stub with ABA/SWIFT) and invoice_9211.pdf (billing PDF).

If Treasury wants to route via virtual card, we can provide the virtual-card payment link (one-time charge) — that clears faster but may have a small processing fee.

Ben Carter
Payments Ops Lead, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
Attachment: bank_details.pdf (PDF, 8KB); invoice_9211.pdf (PDF, 42KB)


From: Sara Liao <sara.liao@medicahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: MedicaHealth Billing <payments@medicahealth.com>

Subject: Re: Cashflow commitment check-in — Invoice 9211

Ben / Ethan —

Perfect, thanks for the stub and the clarification on VAT. Treasury confirmed they will schedule an outgoing wire on 2027-12-03 and will include invoice_9211.pdf for reconciliation. We'll send a remittance email once the transfer is initiated.

If anything changes on our end I will update you, otherwise expect confirmation by Friday morning. Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Sara

Senior AP Manager, MedicaHealth
Attachment: none

source.subject: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@cloudwave.com>, Mike Ellis <mike.ellis@cloudwave.com>, Rebecca Lin <rebecca.lin@cloudwave.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)

Hi Sara / Mike / Rebecca,

Thanks again for the productive call this morning. As discussed, Cloudwave is planning a Q1 expansion and we'd like to align the renewal paperwork so the additional capacity can onboard the week of Jan 8. Attached are the current MSA (v3) and a draft order form that reflects the +40% seat/throughput uplift and the proposed 18-month term extension.

High-level asks:
- Confirm the proposed term (18 months) and whether you want a pro-rated billing start date for the new capacity.
- Please confirm the data residency note in Section 5.3 — we show US-east for the new pool but can map to EU for specific workloads if needed.
- If you have legal redlines, send them back in-track (we'll consolidate).

Next steps from our side: Ben will handle pricing specifics and Laura (legal) will review any redlines and propose clean language. If agreeable, we'll send a DocuSign package with the redlined MSA and order form.

Attachments: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.docx (redline), OrderForm_Cloudwave_Q1_2028.pdf (draft), ChangeLog_redlines.txt

Thanks — looking forward to closing this so your team can hit the ramp target.

Cheers,
Ethan Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0132
Redwood Inference

From: Sara Patel <sara@cloudwave.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Ellis <mike.ellis@cloudwave.com>, Rebecca Lin <rebecca.lin@cloudwave.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 13:02:18 -0800
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)

On Wed, Nov 10, 2027 at 9:14 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sara / Mike / Rebecca,
> 
> Thanks again for the productive call this morning...

Thanks, Ethan. We reviewed internally. A few immediate items from Cloudwave legal/procurement:

1) MSA Section 7 (Indemnity) — our legal team wants to narrow the liability carveouts; can you provide the last mutually-agreed language you referenced on the call?
2) Data residency — we need a firm commitment for the obligations around EU customer data; can you confirm SLA language or propose a clause?
3) Order form pricing — we'll need a clearer breakdown on how the +40% was calculated (nodes vs. token units) for our SOW.

We can send detailed redlines but wanted to flag these three items first. If Laura can propose suggested language for (1) and (2) that would speed things up. Also please include the PO process timeline — procurement will need the finance contact for invoicing.

Much appreciated — want to keep this on a short clock.

Sara Patel
Head of Data Platforms | Cloudwave Inc.
sara@cloudwave.com | +1 617-555-2279

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 13:18:02 -0800
Subject: Fwd: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)

Laura / Ben — forwarding Cloudwave note. Highlights they want firm language for data residency (EU flows) and a tightened indemnity. Ben, can you attach the pricing breakout you used for the +40% uplift (nodes vs token-based) and confirm the start-of-billing options (pro-rate vs fixed start date)? Laura — can you propose two alternate indemnity clauses: (A) narrow carveouts that we can sign off quickly and (B) a more conservative lawyer-friendly clause if they push back? Also Laura, please propose a short EU-data-residency addendum we can attach to the MSA (one paragraph + SLA reference).

I'll ack back to Cloudwave with an internal ETA. Ideally we get this wrapped in 48-72 hours.

—Ethan

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Nov 2027 10:05:44 -0800
Subject: RE: Fwd: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)

Ethan / Ben — thanks for the context. I've attached a redlined MSA (Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.docx) that contains:

- Indemnity: tightened language in Section 7.1 with an explicit negligence carveout and a mutual cap tied to the last 12 months' fees (alternative clause included in the attachment, labelled '7.1_alt').
- Data residency addendum: drafted a short addendum (Addendum A) that commits to EU-hosted KV store for specified datasets and includes a service-level reference to our EU-region availability and incident response times.
- Minor cosmetic fixes and a short changelog (ChangeLog_redlines.txt) summarizing edits.

Notes / negotiation posture:
- Start with the narrower indemnity (7.1) — it's acceptable commercially and removes overly broad obligations. If Cloudwave insists on expanded indemnity, push for added fee-based cap (we can only accept with Exec sign-off).
- For the EU residency item, the addendum commits to logical segregation but not full data isolation on the same tenancy; if they require physical tenant separation that becomes an engineering/procurement conversation (cost-impact).

Ben — I left placeholders in the OrderForm for the pricing line-item breakdown. If you can update that, I'll produce a clean version for DocuSign.

Attachments: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.docx, ChangeLog_redlines.txt

Laura Bennett
Head of Legal Ops | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0199

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@cloudwave.com>, Mike Ellis <mike.ellis@cloudwave.com>, Rebecca Lin <rebecca.lin@cloudwave.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2027 17:42:11 -0800
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form: Renewal & Q1 Capacity Expansion (Cloudwave)

Hi Sara / Mike / Rebecca — quick update and next actions.

1) Legal redlines: Attached is the redlined MSA (Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.docx) and a short ChangeLog summarizing the edits. Laura tightened indemnity language (see Section 7.1) and added Addendum A for EU-hosted datasets.
2) Pricing breakdown: Ben has populated the Order Form with a nodes + token unit split; summary below: base subscription (existing) + 40% uplift = 16 additional inference nodes (estimated token units prorated). Ben, please confirm if you want the pro-rated billing start (we suggested Dec 15 start for onboarding).
3) DocuSign: I've packaged the redlined MSA + order form for signature — link here: https://www.docusign.com/recipient/embedded?envelopeId=0a1b2c3d4e5f

Requested actions from Cloudwave:
- Please review Section 7.1 and Addendum A; if the narrow indemnity is acceptable we can proceed without Exec escalation.
- Confirm billing start preference (pro-rate Dec 15 vs fixed Jan 1).
- If legal wants to send tracked redlines, please put them in one document so Laura can consolidate.

If we get confirmation on (1) and (2) by EOD Monday (Nov 15), we can have signed docs and an onboarding date in the first week of January.

Thanks — I'm available for a short call if you want to walk through the redline.

Best,
Ethan Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0132

Attachments: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.docx, OrderForm_Cloudwave_Q1_2028.pdf, ChangeLog_redlines.txt

source.subject: Urgent: Evidence collection & handling for yesterday's latency spike
messages:
From: Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@pioneerfinancial.com
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Rachel —

We experienced repeated 2–3s tail latencies on /v1/generate between 08:52–09:05 PT this morning which caused several timeouts on our end. Can you please provide: a) runtime logs for the 08:45–09:15 window, b) any error-rate graphs or exporter metrics, and c) confirmation about what data is captured in the logs (are there any raw prompts/PII?). Our legal team may need an evidence package; please advise on retention and chain-of-custody.

We need this by EOD today if possible.

—Derek
Ops Lead, Pioneer Financial
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 09:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Thanks Derek — looping in infra and security to assemble an evidence bundle and confirm what will be shared. Initial asks: preserve raw runtime logs from 08:40–09:20 PT and mark them for legal hold; we'll prepare a redacted export plus an audit manifest (hashes + processing steps). Neha, Marcus, please advise on retention, redaction approach, and whether we can produce a signed checksum/manifest. Ethan, please snapshot exporter metrics (error_rate, p95 latency, queue_depth) for that window.

Action: I can host a 30-min sync at 16:00 PT to review the deliverable and approval gating. Derek, is that time okay?

Rachel Kim
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 11:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Quick update on infra side: we've pulled the node-level runtime traces and the aggregate request logs for the region serving Pioneer. Plan:
- Create raw bundle (runtime-logs-20271019.tar.gz) and store in secure staging with strict ACLs.
- Generate an evidence_manifest-20271019.csv listing files, SHA256 checksums, and extraction commands.
- Produce a redacted bundle (runtime-logs-20271019.tar.gz.redacted) that removes any fields matching our PII patterns and prompt bodies per Neha's redaction rules.
- Keep raw bundle immutable with write-once retention for 30 days under legal hold; provide redacted bundle for delivery.

Risks / questions: if Pioneer requires raw prompts verbatim for debugging, we need a legal sign-off (customer request + internal legal approval) due to privacy. Neha — confirm the ruleset and whether SOC 2 logging commitments impose any minimum retention or redaction standard here.

Attachments referenced: redaction_rules.yml (draft)

Marcus
Director, Inference Ops
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 12:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Thanks — confirming security checklist and proposed handling: 
1) Evidence staging: raw bundle placed in s3://redwood-evidence-retention/pioneer/2027-10-19/ raw/ (WORM, restricted to sec+legal+ops).
2) Redaction: run redaction_rules.yml through sanitized-exporter; strip prompt_text, user identifiers, and 4-digit account tokens. Keep request/response ids for traceability.
3) Manifest: evidence_manifest-20271019.csv will include file paths, SHA256, export timestamp, operator id, and redaction_signature. We'll sign the manifest with our custody key (HSM-backed).
4) Retention: raw bundle retained 30 days by default; redacted export available for 365 days unless customer asks for longer. If Pioneer requests raw data for legal investigation we need a signed Data Access Request + Legal approval; then we will create a controlled disclosure with MF authentication and additional audit logging.
5) Chain-of-custody: record each transfer step in the manifest; create a PDF chain_of_custody-20271019 (attached) when handing off to customer.

I'm attaching the redaction_rules.yml and a sample manifest template. Marcus/Ethan: please wire up the exporter and provide the first draft bundles to sec@redwood for review before delivery.

Neha Kapoor
Head of Security Compliance
Redwood Inference
From: Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Thanks, Rachel/Neha/Marcus — that plan sounds good. A couple of clarifications:
- We do not want raw prompts unless absolutely necessary for a regulatory investigation; redacted logs are fine for now.
- For the chain of custody PDF, can you include timestamps for when files were moved and which operator performed the export? That helps our SOC/compliance team.
- Please confirm delivery mechanism: secure shared Drive link or SFTP? SFTP with two-person access control preferred.

We can join the 16:00 PT call.

—Derek
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
To: Derek Hall <derek.hall@pioneerfinancial.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 Oct 2027 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service degradation this morning — request for logs and timeline

Team — final status and next steps after today's sync: 
- Deliverables prepared: runtime-logs-20271019.tar.gz.redacted, evidence_manifest-20271019.csv, redaction_rules.yml, chain_of_custody-20271019.pdf. All files staged in secure Drive (link below) and SFTP will be enabled on request.
- Access controls: two-person SFTP user required; temporary credentials will expire in 72 hours.
- Retention: raw bundle retained 30 days (WORM); redacted bundle retained 365 days.
- Signoffs: Neha will sign manifest with custody key; Marcus will attest extraction steps.
- Follow-up: legal will be copied if Pioneer requests raw prompts.

Derek — I've placed the redacted bundle and manifest at https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-evidence-pioneer-2027 (access to the folder is listed in the manifest). If you want SFTP access instead, reply-all with the two Pioneer contacts who will need accounts and we'll provision.

Thanks — Rachel
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Request for goodwill billing adjustment due to multi-region deployment delay
messages:
From: Sofia Li <sofia@atlashealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Atlas Health Billing <billing@atlashealth.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Hi Ethan / Monica,

Quick note from our side — our multi-region failover cutover that was scheduled for 9/10 had to be paused because the private deployment package we tested with your team didn’t include the VPC peering support we rely on for automated route failover. That feature was supposed to land in the 9/1 runtime release. We incurred overtime and tooling costs to coordinate a manual fallback; our SREs estimate ~120 engineering hours and an immediate impact on our traffic shaping.

Atlas Billing asked me to reach out about whether Redwood would consider a billing adjustment or one-time credit given the missed timeline and operational lift on our side. We value the platform and want this to be a constructive resolution — happy to take a short call if helpful.

Attachments: deployment_timeline.xlsx (our cutover plan), failure_log_snippet.txt

Thanks,
Sofia Li
Director, Platform Engineering
Atlas Health
From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
To: Sofia Li <sofia@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Sofia — really appreciate the level setting and the timeline. Sorry Atlas ran into extra lift here. I’m looping in Karthik from Product to confirm what happened on our release side and Monica for account coordination.

Next steps I propose:
- Karthik to confirm scope gap and expected ship date for VPC peering.
- Monica to estimate a goodwill credit range that’s reasonable given contract and ARR.
- If needed, we’ll route an exec exception for anything outside standard T&Cs.

If you’re available I can do a 20-min call tomorrow to walk through the timeline and ensure we’ve captured the customer impact for any adjustment.

—Ethan
Ethan Park
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 09:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Thanks for the ping. Short explanation from Product/Platform: the 9/1 runtime drop included the orchestration hooks but not the customer-specific VPC peering automation for private deployments — that capability was scoped for the next sprint after we validated some cross-account IAM permutations. We missed communicating the dependency clearly to the deployment team; that’s on us.

What I can confirm:
- Target ETA for VPC peering automation: 2027-10-14 (next maintenance window).
- Workaround (manual peering + runbook) implemented by your SREs is consistent with what we recommended, but we agree it adds manual ops time.

For commercial/credit perspective, product recommends a one-time concession tied to the measurable runway impact. Suggested options:
- Option A: Credit equal to two weeks of average monthly spend (pro-rated) — ~US$18k based on current usage.
- Option B: Credit capped at US$30k plus a free enablement session (2 SRE days) to automate fallbacks.

Either way I’m happy to document the root cause and commit to the 10/14 delivery.

–Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Director, Product — Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 Sep 2027 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Looping Laura from Finance — we need guidance on the credit band we can offer without an exec exception. Karthik’s options above look reasonable; Atlas is asking for something that offsets their SRE hours + incremental risk.

Quick context: Atlas is a mid-enterprise account, current ARR ~$600k, paid annual. They’ve been a good reference and are expanding internally across two product lines. We’d like to keep the relationship healthy without setting a precedent that’s hard to scale.

Ask for Finance: what’s the max one-off credit we can approve at my level? If over that, we’ll need the exec exception form filled and an approval thread.

Thanks — Monica
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 30 Sep 2027 13:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Thanks for the notes. Finance position and required next steps below.

Summary/assessment:
- We acknowledge the product gap and the customer impact.
- Atlas is strategic (ARR ~600k) and the ask is constrained to a one-time goodwill adjustment tied to an implementation miss.

Approval routing:
- At my level Finance can approve up to US$15,000 as a commercial goodwill credit.
- For anything between US$15,000 and US$35,000 we require a written exec exception (COO or VP Sales sign-off).
- >US$35,000 requires CFO approval and legal review for precedent language.

Recommendation: consider Option B (cap US$30k + enablement) and route the exec exception form. I’ve attached the executive_exception_form.pdf that the COO will need to sign; the form includes the business rationale and a short risk checklist.

Next actions (action owners):
- Ethan: get the exec exception form signed by your sales AE leader and the COO (if they agree to $30k).
- Monica: prepare a short customer impact note for the execs (2 bullets + timeline).
- Karthik: provide the root-cause blurb to include in the form (1 paragraph).

I’ll hold off processing credit until signed form is returned. If Atlas needs an interim gesture, Finance can apply a temporary invoice hold while we finalize.

Laura Bennett
Director, Revenue Operations & Finance
Redwood Inference
--- End of thread ---

—Ethan

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Production multi-region go-live delay + billing question

Thanks for the ping. Short explanation from Product/Platform: the 9/1 runtime drop included the orchestration hooks but not the customer-specific VPC peering automation for private deployments — that capability was scoped for the next sprint after we validated some cross-account IAM permutations. We missed communicating the dependency clearly to the deployment team; that’s on us.

What I can confirm:
- Target ETA for VPC peering automation: 2027-10-14 (next maintenance window).
- Workaround (manual peering + runbook) implemented by your SREs is consistent with what we recommended, but we agree it adds manual ops time.

For commercial/credit perspective, product recommends a one-time concession tied to the measurable runway impact. Suggested options:

–Karthik

messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Morris <alicia@blueorchid.io>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-09-08T15:22:00-07:00
Subject: Blue Orchid commercial path & approval mapping (post-call)

Hi Alicia — thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

Call highlights:
- Primary use case: customer support automation for fraud investigation triage (pilot 3 months)
- Expected ramp: 50k tokens/day peak in month 2, sustained thereafter
- Key constraints: must pass vendor security review and procurement needs a PO or marketplace purchase option

Commercial / procurement notes (from our side):
1) Pricing: we'll share a tailored quote (pilot + scale path) — see attached SOW_draft_v1.pdf for baseline scope and estimated pricing bands.
2) Payment path: you mentioned procurement prefers PO; we typically accept PO (net-30) or credit card for initial months. If Blue Orchid prefers marketplace, we can check marketplace listing timelines.
3) Approvals: you referenced Tom Kline (Dir, Finance) as the budget approver and Evan Ross (VP, Procurement) as final signatory. Can you confirm?
4) Required docs: procurement asked for SOC 2 attestation, vendor risk form, and data retention details — we can send SOC 2 report and a short risk packet.

Proposed next actions:
- Alicia: confirm PO template + whether Tom will join a short intro with our Sales Ops (Marissa) — target this week.
- Blue Orchid procurement: if PO, please share the PO template and signatory process.
- Redwood: I will circulate a compact commercial path map and a one-page decision matrix for PO vs card vs marketplace.

Attaching: SOW_draft_v1.pdf (scope + provisional pricing).

Thanks — speak soon.

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0143

---
Attachment: SOW_draft_v1.pdf (pdf)
From: Alicia Morris <alicia@blueorchid.io>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Kline <tom.k@blueorchid.io>, procurement@blueorchid.io
Date: 2027-09-09T09:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Blue Orchid commercial path & approval mapping (post-call)

Thanks Ethan — appreciate the clear recap. Quick answers: 

- Confirming Tom Kline is our economic buyer/finance sponsor and will own budget authority for the pilot. He can join a short call next Tue (9/14) at 10am PT.
- Our procurement leads prefer a PO for mid-term purchases; initial pilot (low spend) we may put on a corporate card to accelerate kick-off but procurement will require a PO for the production contract.
- Attached is our in-house PO template (BlueOrchid_PO_template.docx). Evan Ross (VP, Procurement) is the signatory for contracts over $50k and will require a quick legal review.
- Security asks: yes, please send SOC 2 + vendor risk pack; our InfoSec will run it through a normal 5-7 business day review.

I'll loop Tom on scheduling and procurement will send any additional templates.

Alicia Morris
Head of Product Operations | Blue Orchid
alicia@blueorchid.io | 212-555-0199

---
Attachment: BlueOrchid_PO_template.docx (docx)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-09-10T11:13:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Blue Orchid commercial path & approval mapping (post-call)

Ethan / Ben — quick internal note with recommended play: 

1) Confirm the two-track approach Alicia suggested:
   - Track A (fast): credit card for pilot (low friction) + attach MSA addendum for trial terms
   - Track B (prod): PO -> Net-30 
2) Coordinate SOC2 + vendor risk packet to Alicia's team before Friday so InfoSec has items to triage. I can pull the one-pager with retention and encryption details.
3) Economics / signing flow (proposed mapping):
   - Economic buyer / budget owner: Tom Kline (Blue Orchid)
   - Finance sponsor / approver: Tom Kline (same)
   - Contract signatory: Evan Ross (VP Procurement) — requires VP signature for >$50k
   - Legal review: Blue Orchid legal (Alicia to confirm contact)

I'll draft a short approval-lattice (one page) that calls out decision owners and the PO vs card tradeoffs. Ethan, want me to drop that into the HubSpot deal and schedule the finance intro?

Marissa Cole
Sales Ops | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

---
Attachment: approval-checklist.pdf (pdf)
From: procurement@blueorchid.io
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alicia Morris <alicia@blueorchid.io>, Tom Kline <tom.k@blueorchid.io>, Evan Ross <evan.ross@blueorchid.io>
Date: 2027-09-11T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Blue Orchid vendor onboarding: PO terms and signatory path

Ethan — procurement here. A few concrete items we need to proceed with a PO: 

1) Please confirm standard payment terms (we typically expect Net-30; we can accept Net-45 if negotiated).
2) Provide a redline-friendly MSA or confirm we can use your standard MSA plus our PO (we prefer minimal redlines for vendor onboarding).
3) Our approver flow: budget owner (Tom Kline) -> procurement review (Procurement Team) -> VP signature (Evan Ross) -> final legal sign-off if redlines occur.
4) Please provide: SOC 2 report, vendor risk questionnaire, insurance certificate. You can upload to the Drive link Alicia shared or reply with attachments.

Attachment: BlueOrchid_PO_template.docx (received)

Please confirm today so we can slot you for the 9/20 onboarding batch.

Procurement Team
Blue Orchid
procurement@blueorchid.io
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Morris <alicia@blueorchid.io>, procurement@blueorchid.io
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Tom Kline <tom.k@blueorchid.io>
Date: 2027-09-12T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Blue Orchid vendor onboarding: PO terms and signatory path

Thanks, all — quick confirmation and next steps from Redwood: 

1) Payment terms: Redwood accepts Net-30 by default for PO-backed contracts; we can discuss Net-45 if needed.
2) Docs: attaching SOC2_attestation.pdf and Vendor_Risk_Packet.pdf to this thread; I'll also upload to the Drive link.
3) Signatory mapping (as we have it captured):
   - Economic buyer / budget owner: Tom Kline (Blue Orchid)
   - Finance sponsor: Tom Kline
   - Procurement signatory: Evan Ross (VP, Procurement) for >$50k
   - Redwood signatory: Ben Carter (VP, Commercial) or Ethan Park for PO under $25k
4) Next meetings: I've scheduled the finance intro with Tom + Marissa for Tue 9/14 10am PT (calendar invite sent). Also proposing a short procurement intake meeting for Fri 9/17 at 11am PT to walk through the PO template.

Action items: 
- Redwood (Ben/Marissa): confirm which MSA variant procurement prefers and prepare a short redline-friendly MSA to send.
- Blue Orchid (procurement): confirm if the PO template requires any contract clauses beyond your standard (we'll redline as needed).
- Alicia / Tom: confirm attendees for the 9/14 finance intro.

Attaching: SOC2_attestation.pdf (pdf); Vendor_Risk_Packet.pdf (pdf)

Looking forward — will drop the one-page approval-lattice into HubSpot after this message.

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai

source.subject: [CLAR-3245] Dedicated GPU pool oversubscription — immediate customer escalation
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
To: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:05:00 +0000
Subject: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245)

Team —

Quick summary: between 01:30–01:58 UTC we observed sustained queue depth growth on the dedicated pool backing ClarityMetrics (pool-id: dp-6b2). Token processing latency has spiked from baseline 85ms/token to 610ms/token for seq_len>1024 requests; error rate (5xx) climbed from 0.2% to ~6.5%. The autoscaler attempted a scale but could not allocate additional reserved capacity due to a hard limit on the pool (reserved_ceiling=48 GPUs).

Key artifacts attached: pool-metrics-20270908.csv (per-node CPU/GPU/queue stats), claritymetrics-impact-log.txt (sampled user errors).

Immediate impact: ClarityMetrics is hitting their reserved throughput commit and we're seeing tail latency on inference calls. We have a Jira (INF-5987) capturing live traces.

Ask:
- Marissa / David: please notify the account (we need to set expectations)
- Alex: can you validate whether we can temporarily route low-priority routes for CLAR to shared capacity (best-effort) and enable granular per-route QoS?
- Sean / Vivek: recommend exec visibility if we need cross-tenant bursting or manual capacity reallocation.

I'll keep sampling and will post an ETA for mitigation within 30 minutes.

-Ethan
SRE, Redwood Inference

---
From: Evan Brooks <evan@claritymetrics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: ClarityMetrics SRE <sre@claritymetrics.com>, procurement@claritymetrics.com
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:18:30 +0000
Subject: Re: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245)

Marissa / David —

We started seeing application errors at ~01:40 UTC and some customers are reporting timeouts. This is directly impacting our credit-card fraud score updates (batch window is slipping). We are on a 24/7 production SLA and the errors are causing financial risk. Please advise immediate mitigation and when we can expect normal throughput.

Attached: examples of failed inference payloads (claritymetrics-impact-log.txt).

We need clarity on:
- Is this isolated to our dedicated pool or is it a regional capacity problem?
- Will you be able to burst us onto other capacity in the next hour?
- If not, we will need temporary credits/penalty discussion while this is unresolved.

Please escalate — we will be tracking on our side as well.

—Evan
Head of Engineering, ClarityMetrics

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:25:10 +0000
Subject: Exec escalation: ClarityMetrics dedicated-pool saturation (CLAR-3245)

Vivek/Rishi —

Heads-up: ClarityMetrics (strategic account, annual committed spend) is experiencing a dedicated GPU pool saturation event that is causing both latency and 5xx spikes. They're outside their burst window and are seeing production impact (Head of Eng just emailed). Ethan has collected traces (INF-5987).

Preliminary mitigation options:
1) Authorize cross-tenant bursting: manually permit CLAR to use 8 GPUs from the shared burst pool for 2 hours.
2) Enable degraded-mode routing: shift non-critical routes to a cached-embedding path to reduce token volume.
3) Fast lane: increase their dedicated quota (requires operations lead + billing signoff).

I recommend we authorize option 1 immediately to stop the bleeding while engineering works option 2. We need an explicit yes/no from Vivek within 15 minutes so I can confirm to the customer and avoid contract impact.

David/Sean/Alex — please execute the temporary config if approved.

-Marissa
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

---
From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:37:45 +0000
Subject: RE: Exec escalation: ClarityMetrics dedicated-pool saturation (CLAR-3245)

Short tech note / action plan:

1) Isolation: confirmed saturation is localized to dp-6b2 (one placement group). We are seeing uneven KV-cache pressure and a hot-shard effect on model shards 3/7 which indicates a routing imbalance.

2) Immediate mitigations implemented (02:36 UTC):
   - Enabled per-route sampling to drop low-priority long-context calls (applied via rate-limit policy).
   - Provisioned best-effort burst: temporarily routed CLAR's non-SLA traffic to the shared burst pool (8 GPUs) — best-effort, no SLA guarantee.
   - Activated model-variant fallback for heavy seq_len requests (small quantized variant) to reduce GPU memory pressure.

3) Next steps (ETA timelines):
   - 02:55 UTC: monitor error rate and tail latency (30-min stabilization window).
   - 03:30 UTC: if error rate >1% persist, request manual reallocation of 6 GPUs from non-critical reserved pools (requires billing/ops approval).

Notes: routing the traffic to shared pool will reduce 95th percentile latency but may increase cost (we'll track token-level deltas). We need Marissa to confirm customer-facing messaging and Vivek for approval to reallocate if the burst doesn't stabilize.

-Alex
Senior Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

---
From: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan@claritymetrics.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: ClarityMetrics SRE <sre@claritymetrics.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 09 Sep 2027 18:22:00 +0000
Subject: RE: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245) — mitigation and next steps

Evan —

Thanks for the quick flag. Short update on where we landed and next steps:

What we did (timeline):
- 02:36 UTC: routing changes + shared-burst routing (8 GPUs) + model-variant fallback (Alex).
- 02:50–03:20 UTC: error rate reduced from ~6.5% to 0.8% and tail latency fell to ~150ms/token for the majority of requests.
- 04:10 UTC: initiated policies to permanently rebalance model shard routing for CLAR's pool (elastic config changes scheduled overnight).

Why it happened: a combination of an unusually bursty job pattern from CLAR's long-context analytics job and uneven placement on dp-6b2 produced a hot-shard. Our autoscaler respected the reserved ceiling which prevented an automatic expansion that would have preserved latency.

What we're doing next:
- Short term: we have a scheduled reallocation (INF-5987) to rebalance shard placement. We'll also propose a dedicated headroom buffer for CLAR (configurable via Reserved capacity options).
- Medium term: product will expose per-route headroom controls and improved shard balancing logic to eliminate hot-shard sensitivity.

Compensation/contract: Marissa is coordinating commercial outreach; we'll follow our SLA process. For now, we committed to a 2% credit on the affected billing window while we finish the postmortem.

Attachments shared: pool-metrics-20270908.csv, requested-burst-approval.pdf. HubSpot deal updated: https://redwood.hubspot.com/deal/CLAR-3245.

Evan — can you confirm whether the fallthrough incidents impacted reconciliation batch 01 (yes/no) so we can include concrete failure counts in the postmortem?

Appreciate the patience and we'll follow up with a full timeline + RCA by EOD tomorrow.

-David
Technical Account Lead, Redwood Inference

On Wed, 08 Sep 2027 at 02:18, Evan Brooks <evan@claritymetrics.com> wrote:
> Marissa / David —
>
> We started seeing application errors at ~01:40 UTC and some customers are reporting timeouts. This is directly impacting our credit-card fraud score updates (batch window is slipping). We are on a 24/7 production SLA and the errors are causing financial risk. Please advise immediate mitigation and when we can expect normal throughput.
>
> —Evan

> Marissa / David —
>
> We started seeing application errors at ~01:40 UTC and some customers are reporting timeouts. This is directly impacting our credit-card fraud score updates (batch window is slipping). We are on a 24/7 production SLA and the errors are causing financial risk. Please advise immediate mitigation and when we can expect normal throughput.
>
> —Evan

source.subject: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)
messages:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>, Tom Rios <tom.rios@acmeai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Hi Julia / Tom —

Thanks again for the renewal signal during last week’s exec sync. To make sure we land the 12‑month renewal with the +120 GPU uplift (net 240 reserved GPUs) without interrupting production, I sketched a sequencing plan that covers: procurement sign‑off, SLA amendment for throughput guarantees, a staged capacity provisioning window, and a lightweight canary followed by stepped traffic migration.

High‑level asks from you to proceed:
- Procurement approval window (expected sign date)
- Preferred canary weekday/time and blackout windows for batch jobs
- Any hard SLOs for tail latency or per‑minute TPS we must meet on day‑1 of expanded pool

Attached: RDW‑SLA‑Amendment‑AcmeAI.docx (SLA language + throughput targets) and MA‑Appendix‑GPU‑Uplift.pdf (pricing appendix and burst policy). I’ll loop in pricing and infra below to align on timeline.

Proposed timeline (working):
1) Contract addendum signed — target 8/27
2) Provision new reserved pool (40% of uplift) — 8/30–9/1 (canary)
3) Traffic ramp to 20% / 50% / 100% — 9/2–9/6, with rollback gates at each step
4) Final optimization & billing alignment — 9/10

Karthik / Marissa / Ethan — please weigh in on commercial, success criteria, and runbook items below.

Thanks,
Rachel
Rachel Kim
Head of Renewals, Redwood Inference
rachel@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-SLA-Amendment-AcmeAI.docx, MA-Appendix-GPU-Uplift.pdf

From: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Rios <tom.rios@acmeai.com>, AcmeAI Procurement <procurement@acmeai.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Rachel — thanks for the clear plan. AcmeAI remains committed to the uplift, but a few constraints from our side:
- Procurement prefers contracts to be in the queue by 8/26 so we can sign by 8/31.
- We have a full nightly batch window from 01:00–04:00 PT on weekends — please avoid any irreversible schema or data migration then.
- Hard SLOs: p95 latency for generation pipelines must be <220ms; sustained throughput target for the expanded pool is ~18k tokens/sec.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide a prorated invoice that reflects only the days the new pool is in production in September? Also looped Tom (infra) to confirm blackout windows.

Julia Park
VP Product, AcmeAI
julia@acmeai.com

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, AcmeAI Procurement <procurement@acmeai.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2027 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Julia / Team — appreciate the constraints. Quick commercial notes:
- Pricing: uplift quote attached (pricing-uplift-quote-8421.xlsx). The committed monthly fee covers 200 of the 240 GPUs; burst pricing applies to additional 40 GPUs at a reduced per‑GPU rate (see appendix).
- Billing: we can prorate the committed fee to start on the first full calendar month after sign. For September we can issue a one‑line prorated invoice for the days the new pool serves live traffic.
- Term: renew for 12 months with an option to re‑evaluate quantization profiles after month 6.

Next steps from commercial: Procure to send redlines against RDW‑SLA‑Amendment by EOD 8/25 so DocuSign can be prepared.

—Karthik
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai

Attachment: pricing-uplift-quote-8421.xlsx

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Thanks everyone — a couple technical success criteria and operational asks from CS: 
- Success criteria for canary: 48h of stable p50/p95 within target, no more than 2 customer‑facing error spikes >1% in that window.
- Test set: please provide a representative prompt set (we can anonymize) and two heavy batch jobs you run nightly so we can run parallel load tests.
- Traffic shifting recommendation: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% with at least 12 hours between steps for telemetry evaluation. If any metric crosses rollback thresholds, we step back immediately and interrogate.

I'll lead the runbook coordination and own the production validation checklist. Ethan — can you confirm whether KV cache warmup between pools will require any client change or if we can route traffic to the new pool without rekeying?

Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.ai

Attachment: migration-runbook-v1.md

From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2027 13:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Marissa / all — infra notes and runbook excerpt below (more detail in migration-runbook-v1.md attached to Marissa’s message).

Runbook excerpt (canary window):
- Provision pool A (new uplift) as read‑only to control plane and allocate 80 GPUs initially for canary.
- Route 3% traffic via routing policy tag "acmeai-uplift-canary" to pool A. Maintain full routing to current pool B for everything else.
- KV cache warmup: run a background prefix prefill against the KV warmup API for the top 500 recent keys — no client change needed. Client should continue to present the same model version; routing is handled at the RDW gateway.
- Metrics guardrails (automated): p95 gen latency > 250ms OR error rate > 1.5% for 30m triggers automated rollback to pool B.
- Manual rollback: on pager, provisioned lead must acknowledge and then we run a controlled rollback script.

Edge notes: the new pool will be the same quantized variant but with an alternate kernel config for throughput. Expect initial cold start latency ~+30ms for the first 5 minutes until KV warms.

—Ethan
Infra Reliability Lead, Redwood Inference
ethan@redwood.ai

From: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>, Tom Rios <tom.rios@acmeai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, AcmeAI Procurement <procurement@acmeai.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Aug 2027 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Julia / Team — consolidated timeline and action items (please confirm by EOD 8/25 so we can prep DocuSign):

Action owners & timeline:
- Friday 8/26: Procurement to send any redlines to RDW (AcmeAI Procurement)
- 8/27–8/28: RDW Legal & Commercial finalize addendum, create DocuSign envelope (Karthik)
- 8/30: Provision first tranche (80 GPUs) for canary; CS and Infra to execute warmup (Marissa / Ethan)
- 9/1–9/3: Canary observation window (3% -> 5% traffic)
- 9/4–9/6: Stepped ramp (20% -> 50% -> 100%) subject to guardrails
- 9/10: Invoice alignment & finalize metrics baseline for month 1 billing

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/rdw-acmeai/8421
Attachments: MA-Appendix-GPU-Uplift.pdf, RDW-SLA-Amendment-AcmeAI.docx

Please reply with a one‑line signoff or any blocking change. If we don’t hear back by EOD 8/25 we’ll assume the 8/30 provisioning window is acceptable and proceed to prepare the pool.

Thanks,
Rachel

On Tue, Aug 24, 2027 at 13:42 Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Marissa / all — infra notes and runbook excerpt below (more detail in migration-runbook-v1.md attached to Marissa’s message).
>

> - Route 3% traffic via routing policy tag "acmeai-uplift-canary" to pool A. Maintain full routing to current pool B for everything else.
> - KV cache warmup: run a background prefix prefill against the KV warmup API for the top 500 recent keys — no client change needed.
> - Metrics guardrails (automated): p95 gen latency > 250ms OR error rate > 1.5% for 30m triggers automated rollback to pool B.
>
> —Ethan

From: AcmeAI Procurement <procurement@acmeai.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 25 Aug 2027 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Rachel / Karthik — procurement reviewed the RDW‑SLA‑Amendment and sent minor redlines (pricing appendix and invoicing cadence). We’ll initiate DocuSign today; estimated sign by 8/31. Please confirm:
- We agree to the prorated invoice approach for September.
- We request a single invoice for the committed fee and a separate line for any burst usage over the committed pool.

Tom Rios (Infra) will coordinate final blackout windows — Tom, please confirm earliest.

Procurement Team
AcmeAI

- Friday 8/26: Procurement to send any redlines to RDW (AcmeAI Procurement)
- 8/27–8/28: RDW Legal & Commercial finalize addendum, create DocuSign envelope (Karthik)
- 8/30: Provision first tranche (80 GPUs) for canary; CS and Infra to execute warmup (Marissa / Ethan)
- 9/1–9/3: Canary observation window (3% -> 5% traffic)
- 9/4–9/6: Stepped ramp (20% -> 50% -> 100%) subject to guardrails
- 9/10: Invoice alignment & finalize metrics baseline for month 1 billing

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/rdw-acmeai/8421
Attachments: MA-Appendix-GPU-Uplift.pdf, RDW-SLA-Amendment-AcmeAI.docx

Please reply with a one‑line signoff or any blocking change. If we don’t hear back by EOD 8/25 we’ll assume the 8/30 provisioning window is acceptable and proceed to prepare the pool.

Thanks,
Rachel

On Tue, Aug 24, 2027 at 13:42 Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Marissa / all — infra notes and runbook excerpt below (more detail in migration-runbook-v1.md attached to Marissa’s message).
>
> Runbook excerpt (canary window):
> - Provision pool A (new uplift) as read‑only to control plane and allocate 80 GPUs initially for canary.
> - Route 3% traffic via routing policy tag "acmeai-uplift-canary" to pool A. Maintain full routing to current pool B for everything else.
> - KV cache warmup: run a background prefix prefill against the KV warmup API for the top 500 recent keys — no client change needed.
> - Metrics guardrails (automated): p95 gen latency > 250ms OR error rate > 1.5% for 30m triggers automated rollback to pool B.
>
> —Ethan

From: AcmeAI Procurement <procurement@acmeai.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julia Park <julia@acmeai.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 25 Aug 2027 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: sequencing plan for 120->240 GPU reserved pool (AcmeAI)

Rachel / Karthik — procurement reviewed the RDW‑SLA‑Amendment and sent minor redlines (pricing appendix and invoicing cadence). We’ll initiate DocuSign today; estimated sign by 8/31. Please confirm:
- We agree to the prorated invoice approach for September.
- We request a single invoice for the committed fee and a separate line for any burst usage over the committed pool.

Tom Rios (Infra) will coordinate final blackout windows — Tom, please confirm earliest.

Procurement Team
AcmeAI
procurement@acmeai.com

Attachments: procurement-redlines-RDW-SLA.pdf

source.subject: Follow-up: integration checklist & timeline for embedding pilot
messages:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: integration checklist & timeline for embedding pilot

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for a productive session today. Quick recap from our side and a proposed plan to get the embedding pilot moving: 

Key pain points you emphasized:
- Search relevance suffers for long-form clinical notes; keyword-based retrieval returns noisy results and degrades clinician trust.
- Latency spikes during end-of-day batch queries impacting the UI.
- Data residency / auditability requirements for PHI-adjacent metadata.

Current stack you described:
- Postgres primary store (RDS) + S3 for blob storage.
- Primary app in Python (FastAPI) with a small Go microservice for ingestion.
- Using an in-house vector store built on top of FAISS for experiments.

Success criteria for the pilot (what we heard) — please confirm or adjust:
1) 15–20% improvement in top-3 relevance on clinician evaluation set (you’ll share 250 labeled queries).
2) Median retrieval latency under 120ms at 99th percentile for single-query path.
3) No external exposure of raw PHI — all embeddings stored in your VPC and logs retained for 90 days.

Proposed integration & onboarding plan (owners & timeline):
- Week 0 (this week): Access requests + NDA amendment for data handling (BrightHealth legal) — owner: Alex.
- Week 1: SDK runbook & compatibility check — Marissa to deliver quick-start SDK examples in Python and Go; Alex to confirm which service account will be used.
- Week 2–3: Ingest subset (250 queries + 5k documents) to a dedicated test namespace in your VPC (private deployment). Redwood on-call to assist with ingestion; owner: Ethan Park.
- Week 4: End-to-end demo with clinician relevance panel and latency benchmarking — owners: Alex + Marissa.
- Week 5: Pilot evaluation review and decision point (scale or iterate).

Technical notes / compatibility flags:
- SDKs: we recommend starting with the Python SDK for FastAPI (we’ll include a companion Go example for your ingestion microservice).
- API: Our hosted inference supports streaming; for your privacy requirements we suggest Private deployment with a pinned model version.
- Quantization: We can run a 4-bit quantized variant during the pilot to reduce latency; we’ll flag quality regressions if any.

Attachments: I’ve attached a concise SDK compatibility matrix and a one-page pilot milestones brief.

Next steps (action items):
- Alex: confirm the 250 labeled queries and the service account we should provision (by Wed).
- Alex/legal: confirm NDA amendment scope for PHI-adjacent metadata (by Fri).
- Marissa: share SDK examples + ingestion checklist (today).
- Ethan: schedule a 90-minute engineering pairing session for Week 1.

If that looks right I’ll schedule the Week 1 pairing and share the Drive folder. Thanks again — excited to get this moving.

Best,

Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0198

Attachment: rdw-sdk-compatibility-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet stub, 14KB)
Attachment: pilot-milestones-brief.pdf (pdf stub, 28KB)
From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: integration checklist & timeline for embedding pilot

Hi Marissa —

Thanks for the clear write-up — this aligns well with what we discussed. A couple of clarifications from our side: 

1) The 250 labeled queries: we can get those to you by next Tuesday (30 Aug). They’re de-identified but still treated as PHI-adjacent for audit purposes.
2) Service account: we’ll use an internal read-only service account for retrieval traffic (I’ll open the access ticket today and loop in our infra lead).
3) Latency target: 120ms p99 is aggressive for our current infra; can Redwood confirm whether that includes network hop to our VPC or is strictly inference time? If it’s strictly inference time that seems feasible.

We’re comfortable starting with the Python SDK; the Go example is helpful for our ingestion pipeline later. I reviewed the compatibility matrix — looks good.

I can take ownership of Week 0 access requests and the NDA amendment. We’ll aim to have that ready by Friday EOD.

Also: are there any minimal monitoring hooks (trace IDs, token-level cost) we should plan to expose to our observability stack during the pilot? If so, please list them in the SDK README.

Happy to sync briefly tomorrow (15 minutes) if you want to confirm owners.

Best,
Alex
Director, Search & NLP | BrightHealth
alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai

Attachment: —
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Follow-up: integration checklist & timeline for embedding pilot

Looping you both in — Alex confirmed timelines and took ownership of Week 0. Two quick asks before I schedule the Week 1 pairing: 

1) Logan — can we approve a short sandbox instance in the us-west-private cluster for BrightHealth (single-tenant, 4xA10G equivalent) for Weeks 2–4? I’ll include the cost estimate in the VP-runbook.
2) Ethan — please draft the pairing agenda (30–45 minutes) focusing on ingestion checklist, service account permissions, and monitoring hooks. Include code snippets for tracing headers and token cost metrics.

I’ll update the Drive folder and send the calendar invite once I hear back.

Thanks,
Marissa
Quoted-From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai>
On Mon, 23 Aug 2027 at 09:18, Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai> wrote:
> Hi Marissa —
> 
> Thanks for the clear write-up — this aligns well with what we discussed. A couple of clarifications from our side: 
> 
> 1) The 250 labeled queries: we can get those to you by next Tuesday (30 Aug). They’re de-identified but still treated as PHI-adjacent for audit purposes.
> 2) Service account: we’ll use an internal read-only service account for retrieval traffic (I’ll open the access ticket today and loop in our infra lead).
> 3) Latency target: 120ms p99 is aggressive for our current infra; can Redwood confirm whether that includes network hop to our VPC or is strictly inference time? If it’s strictly inference time that seems feasible.
> 
> We’re comfortable starting with the Python SDK; the Go example is helpful for our ingestion pipeline later. I reviewed the compatibility matrix — looks good.
> 
> I can take ownership of Week 0 access requests and the NDA amendment. We’ll aim to have that ready by Friday EOD.
> 
> Also: are there any minimal monitoring hooks (trace IDs, token-level cost) we should plan to expose to our observability stack during the pilot? If so, please list them in the SDK README.
> 
> Happy to sync briefly tomorrow (15 minutes) if you want to confirm owners.
> 
> Best,
> Alex
> Director, Search & NLP | BrightHealth
> alex.johnson@brighthealth.ai

source.subject: Week 0 hands-on orientation & load-test workshop
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Shah <samir@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Week 0 hands-on orientation & load-test workshop

Hi Samir —

Thanks again for agreeing to a hands-on POC with Redwood. To get us rolling, can you confirm which of the two options below works best for your team? We want to make sure engineering + security can join. Both sessions are planned for 90 minutes in this phase.

Option A: Thu Aug 26, 10:00–11:30 CET (02:00–03:30 PDT)
Option B: Tue Aug 31, 14:00–15:30 CET (06:00–07:30 PDT)

Proposed working session agenda (high level) — please see attached PDF for more detail:  
- 0–10m: Introductions & mutual goals
- 10–30m: POC scope and success metrics (routing, latency targets, cost target)
- 30–60m: Hands-on prompt review & live routing walkthrough (we’ll pull in your prompt examples)
- 60–80m: Load-testing plan and smoke-run (short synthetic test)
- 80–90m: Next steps & responsibilities / mutual action plan

Pre-reads and ask:  
- Please review attached POC_Agenda_v1.pdf (short slides + checklist).
- Share 3–5 representative prompts and a sample routing scenario you want us to exercise.
- If you want an internal security review during the session, include the security contact (we can reserve extra time).

If neither option works, propose 2 windows and we’ll adapt. Once you confirm, I’ll circulate a calendar invite and the Zoom link, and Laura will lead the hands-on portion.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: POC_Agenda_v1.pdf (PDF)]

From: Samir Shah <samir@acmecorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marta Kowalska <marta.k@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 11:05:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Week 0 hands-on orientation & load-test workshop

Ben — thanks for the agenda and the options. Both look good but we have a conflict with Option A (all-hands). Option B on Aug 31 is much better for our EU engineering team. Can we shift to 14:00–15:30 CET on Aug 31?

Also: please include our engineering lead (Marta Kowalska) and our security reviewer, Niels Petersen (niels.p@acmecorp.com). We’d like the security reviewer to sit in on the routing/load-test discussion — is that fine?

Finally, do you need us to provision any ephemeral load drivers or will Redwood run synthetic traffic from your side?

Best,
Samir
Head of Product
Acme Corp

(Please note I’m in CET; times above are CET.)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Kowalska <marta.k@acmecorp.com>, Samir Shah <samir@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Week 0 hands-on orientation & load-test workshop

Samir / Marta —

Aug 31 at 14:00 CET works for us. We’ll plan a 90-minute hands-on session. Quick notes for internal coordination (looping in Ethan as ops lead):

- Load testing: Redwood can run the synthetic smoke test from our infra (we’ll generate synthetic prompts and ramp to the agreed concurrency). If Acme wants to run their own drivers, we can accept traffic to a pre-signed endpoint; please share your source IP ranges if so.
- Laura will run the prompt walkthrough and live routing demo. Neha will monitor latency and tracer logs during the test. Ethan — can you confirm we have a small ephemeral load driver ready or should we rely on Redwood’s runner? Also confirm any quotas on the Dedicated pool for that window.
- I updated the agenda slightly and attached POC_Agenda_v1.1.pdf with a 10-minute buffer at the end for quick follow-ups. I also attached loadtest-checklist.md (runbook) for reference.

Next steps:
- Samir/Marta: confirm final attendee list and any sample prompts by EOD next Monday (Aug 23).
- Ethan: confirm load-runner availability and any infra notes.
- I’ll publish the calendar invite with the Zoom link when Samir confirms attendees.

Thanks,
Ben

[Attachment: POC_Agenda_v1.1.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: loadtest-checklist.md (MARKDOWN)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Week 0 hands-on orientation & load-test workshop

All — I’ll lead the hands-on prompt review and the routing demo. A few quick asks: 

- Ben: can you add a small section in the agenda for the prompt evaluation rubric (expected outputs, tolerance for hallucination, and fallback rules)? I’ll use that to grade the before/after prompt sets.
- Samir / Marta: please send 3 representative prompts plus desired responses (examples) — that will make the session effective.
- Neha: I’ll pair with you for the tracer/latency dashboards during the smoke test. Can you make sure the dashboard has the token-level breakdown and the KV cache hit rate visible?

I’ll prepare a 7-slide deck with the rubric and a short checklist and drop it in the Drive folder tonight.

—Laura
Sr. Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Option B: Tue Aug 31, 14:00–15:30 CET (06:00–07:30 PDT)

- 10–30m: POC scope and success metrics (routing, latency targets, cost target)
- 30–60m: Hands-on prompt review & live routing walkthrough (we’ll pull in your prompt examples)
- 60–80m: Load-testing plan and smoke-run (short synthetic test)
- 80–90m: Next steps & responsibilities / mutual action plan

Pre-reads and ask:  
- Please review attached POC_Agenda_v1.pdf (short slides + checklist).
- Share 3–5 representative prompts and a sample routing scenario you want us to exercise.
- If you want an internal security review during the session, include the security contact (we can reserve extra time).

If neither option works, propose 2 windows and we’ll adapt. Once you confirm, I’ll circulate a calendar invite and the Zoom link, and Laura will lead the hands-on portion.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Private deployment architecture session — scheduling and constraints
messages:
From: Samir Khan <samir.k@bluewavehealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BlueWave Health Security <security@bluewavehealth.com>
Date: Sun, 1 Aug 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — availability

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the initial call last week. Our infra and security teams want a thorough architecture review before we move to procurement — specifically a technical deep-dive that covers VPC networking, on-prem deployment topology, and air-gapped (disk/network) requirements. Our CISO will likely attend and wants to see connectivity diagrams + KMS/HSM integration options.

We have two constraints: 1) any session must finish before 5:00 PM PT, and 2) our security lead is only available mornings (PT) on Tue/Wed/Thu next week. Can you propose a few 90-minute windows (we prefer 60–90 minutes) and share a short pre-read or checklist we can review beforehand?

Also, please loop in the person who will own the ops/implementation questions.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Khan
Head of Engineering — BlueWave Health
samir.k@bluewavehealth.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

---
Attachment: enactment_request_stub.txt (requested preflight list)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.k@bluewavehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Aug 2027 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — availability

Hi Samir — great to hear from you. I copied our solutions and ops leads so they can drive the technical portion.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue 2027-08-03 09:00–10:30
- Wed 2027-08-04 08:30–10:00
- Thu 2027-08-05 10:00–11:30 (can end by 11:30 if needed)

Agenda (short):
- 10m introductions & goals
- 25m networking + VPC peering / routing patterns
- 25m on-prem topology + air-gap considerations (HSM/KMS integration)
- 20m ops runbook, RBAC, audit logs, SLOs
- 10m Q&A / next steps

I attached a short pre-read and a precall checklist — Mina will lead the technical walkthrough and Ethan will cover operational runbook items. Ben will stay on to help with next steps and procurement notes. If none of those slots work I can open additional morning blocks.

Marissa Cole
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: Redwood_Private_Deploy_Preread.pdf
Attachment: Private_Deploy_Precall_Checklist.xlsx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Samir Khan <samir.k@bluewavehealth.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Aug 2027 08:52:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — availability

Samir — quick note from my side: our CISO counterpart sometimes prefers an earlier window (07:30–09:00 PT) for internal reviews; if BlueWave can do Wed 08:30 that should be fine.

If Wed 08:30–10:00 is acceptable, I’ll pencil a calendar invite and add a short mutual action plan (milestones + decision points) so procurement has what it needs.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Samir Khan <samir.k@bluewavehealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BlueWave Health Security <security@bluewavehealth.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Aug 2027 06:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — availability

Thanks everyone. Small change on our end — the CISO got called into an early vendor review on Wed morning and can’t make 08:30. We can do an alternate block Wed 07:30–09:00 PT, or Thu 10:00–11:30 (but Thu would push into our operations handoff window). Which would you prefer?

Apologies for the shuffle — we want the CISO present for the network/KMS portion.

Samir

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.k@bluewavehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Aug 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — scheduling and constraints

Samir — thanks for the update. We’ll take the Wed 07:30–09:00 PT slot to ensure the CISO can attend. I’ve attached the pre-read and checklist again and created a calendar event placeholder (link below).

Action items before the call:
- BlueWave: share any network diagrams or existing on-prem topology PDFs (security@bluewavehealth.com)
- Redwood: Mina to prepare HSM/KMS integration patterns + example diagrams; Ethan to prepare a short ops runbook template
- Both: confirm attendee list (esp. CISO + security lead)

Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcd1234
Pre-read: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1A2B3C4D

Looking forward — see you Wednesday 07:30 PT.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com
Phone: +1 (650) 555-0199

---
Attachments: Redwood_Private_Deploy_Preread.pdf, Private_Deploy_Precall_Checklist.xlsx

Date: Sun, 1 Aug 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — availability

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the initial call last week. Our infra and security teams want a thorough architecture review before we move to procurement — specifically a technical deep-dive that covers VPC networking, on-prem deployment topology, and air-gapped (disk/network) requirements. Our CISO will likely attend and wants to see connectivity diagrams + KMS/HSM integration options.

We have two constraints: 1) any session must finish before 5:00 PM PT, and 2) our security lead is only available mornings (PT) on Tue/Wed/Thu next week. Can you propose a few 90-minute windows (we prefer 60–90 minutes) and share a short pre-read or checklist we can review beforehand?

Also, please loop in the person who will own the ops/implementation questions.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Khan
Head of Engineering — BlueWave Health
samir.k@bluewavehealth.com
+1 (415) 555-0123
