source.subject: ARR verification - last-mile handoff
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: ARR verification - last-mile handoff

Team —

Board packet freeze is in 5 business days. We need a last-mile pass over the ARR rollforward and booking-level stage hygiene so finance can certify numbers for the investor deck. Please do the following by EOD today: 

- Ben: attach a cleaned pipeline snapshot (HubSpot export) with hubspot id, opportunity name, stage, commit level, ACV, and close date. Highlight any deals >$50k that moved stages in the last 14 days.
- Vanessa: provide a short note (2–3 bullets) on the three largest mid-funnel deals we currently treat as "commit" for the board. Explicitly call out any gating items.
- Laura: run a quick rollforward sanity check vs. QTD recognized revenue and note any timing differences that could affect ARR.

I've attached the latest ARR rollforward and the template for the snapshot request. Use the Drive folder for final artifacts. If you think any of the 4 deals in tab "questionables" should be excluded, mark them in the comments column and tag me.

Attachments referenced: [Attachment: ARR_rollforward_2027Q3_v2.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)], [Attachment: pipeline_snapshot_template.csv (text/csv)]

Ethan
Ethan Park
Head of FP&A | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 11:12:18 -0700
Subject: Re: ARR verification - last-mile handoff

Ethan — attaching the export. Two quick notes up front:

1) Pipeline export attached as CSV (pipeline_snapshot_20270728.csv). I've flagged opportunities HS-987654 and HS-987659 in the comments column — both have ambiguous commit language from the AE ("verbal yes pending legal" and "PO incoming"). Both are >$75k ARR.
2) There were three stage changes in the last week that bumped us up ~70k ARR in the rollforward. Those are in the sheet "recent_changes".

Questions for you: do we treat "PO incoming" as commit for the board? Historically we have only included deals with signed SOW or signed PO. If we include HS-987659 we should note fallback and probability in the narrative.

Attachments: [Attachment: pipeline_snapshot_20270728.csv (text/csv)]

—Ben
Ben Carter
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 13:05:47 -0700
Subject: Re: ARR verification - last-mile handoff

On Wed, Jul 28, 2027 at 11:12 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Ethan — attaching the export. Two quick notes up front:
>
> 1) Pipeline export attached as CSV (pipeline_snapshot_20270728.csv). I've flagged opportunities HS-987654 and HS-987659 in the comments column — both have ambiguous commit language from the AE ("verbal yes pending legal" and "PO incoming"). Both are >$75k ARR.
> 2) There were three stage changes in the last week that bumped us up ~70k ARR in the rollforward. Those are in the sheet "recent_changes".

Thanks Ben — good catch. My read:

- HS-987654 (verbal yes pending legal): do NOT include in the board commit. Legal is still confirming terms and we treat verbal commitments as 50% probability in ARR math; we'll disclose as "at-risk" in the appendix.
- HS-987659 (PO incoming): include in the headline ARR only if finance can show a dated PO/commit that is signed and dated within the quarter. If we only have email confirmation ("PO coming"), we tag as contingency and exclude from the committed corridor.

Action items I will take now:
- Draft two-sentence board note for the appendix calling out at-risk items and the fallback plan.
- Pull the three largest commit deals' recent cadence and list gating items (ops/legal/contracting).

I'll drop the draft note in the Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-ARR-rollforward (see file: board_appendix_at-risk_notes.docx)

—Vanessa
Vanessa Ortiz
VP Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference

From: Samantha Cole <samantha@harborvc.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: finance_ops@harborvc.com
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 16:30:05 -0400
Subject: Quick question on ARR rollforward for board packet

Ethan/Vanessa — thanks for the heads-up. Two quick clarifications from the investor side as we prep the board materials: 

1) For PO-driven deals, we need to see either a signed PO dated this quarter or an explicit accounting memo from the buyer that establishes effective date (email + PO number is fine).
2) Please confirm whether the $70k bump referenced above is reflected in the public-facing ARR number or only in the internal rollforward. If it's in the public number, we'll need a short footnote in the packet.

Can you share the rollforward and the specific rows that changed? Appreciate a quick reply — pack build starts tomorrow morning on our side.

Samantha Cole
Investor, Harbor VC

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha@harborvc.com>, finance_ops@harborvc.com
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 17:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on ARR rollforward for board packet

Samantha — thanks for the quick read. Short answers below and attached artifacts.

1) PO policy: agreed — for headline public ARR we will only include contracts with signed PO or SOW dated in the quarter. For internal rollforward we will show email confirmations in the "at-risk" appendix (separate tab).
2) The +$70k is currently in the internal rollforward (worksheet: recent_changes) but NOT in the public-facing ARR number. We'll keep the public number steady and include a single footnote in the appendix explaining the at-risk deals and the PO policy.

Attached: [Attachment: ARR_rollforward_2027Q3_v2.xlsx], [Attachment: stage_comments_20270728.pdf]
Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-ARR-rollforward
HubSpot reference: HS-987654, HS-987659

I'll follow up tomorrow morning with the final audited rollforward after Laura's timing check.

Ethan
Ethan Park
Head of FP&A | Redwood Inference

2) The +$70k is currently in the internal rollforward (worksheet: recent_changes) but NOT in the public-facing ARR number. We'll keep the public number steady and include a single footnote in the appendix explaining the at-risk deals and the PO policy.

Attached: [Attachment: ARR_rollforward_2027Q3_v2.xlsx], [Attachment: stage_comments_20270728.pdf]
Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-ARR-rollforward
HubSpot reference: HS-987654, HS-987659

I'll follow up tomorrow morning with the final audited rollforward after Laura's timing check.

Ethan
Ethan Park
Head of FP&A | Redwood Inference

source.subject: PO memo / entity zip mapping for fiscal cutover & temporary net30 exception
messages:
From: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@cumulus-health.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap-team@cumulus-health.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Upcoming fiscal cutover — PO memo & invoice lane questions

Hi Ethan,

We are preparing for a fiscal entity cutover at the end of August and wanted to confirm a couple of AP constraints that will affect how we issue POs to Redwood for Q3 consumption. Our procurement system will require: 1) a memo field that contains the receiving entity ZIP code (5 digits) and a short cost center code, and 2) a unique PO line identifier in the PO header (PO-Line-ID).

A few clarifying questions:
- Can Redwood accept the PO-Line-ID in the PO memo rather than a dedicated header field?
- We typically run a weekly sweep (Friday) for invoices during cutovers. Can Redwood produce a consolidated weekly invoice that includes a per-PO breakdown, and will you accept net30 for the first two billing runs while we stabilize our GL mappings?

I've attached our standard PO template and the AP requirements doc for reference (PO_Template_v3.xlsx, Cumulus_AP_Requirements.docx).

Thanks — looking for a quick confirmation so we can update our procurement team.

Best,
Sofia Ramos
Sr. Procurement Manager
Cumulus Health Systems
attachments: PO_Template_v3.xlsx (spreadsheet), Cumulus_AP_Requirements.docx (doc)

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@cumulus-health.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Jul 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming fiscal cutover — PO memo & invoice lane questions

Hi Sofia — thanks for the detail and the docs. Short answers up front: yes, we can accept the PO-Line-ID and the entity ZIP in the PO memo, and we can run a weekly consolidated invoice for the cutover period. We can also make a short-term net30 exception for the first two runs, subject to the credit handoff described below.

Operational notes / options:
- PO memo mapping: we will map the memo string to two fields in our billing system (entity_zip, cost_center) using a simple regex. Please format the memo as: ZIP|COSTCENTER|PO-LINE-ID (e.g., 94107|IT-ENG|PL-12345) so the parsing is deterministic.
- Invoice cadence: we can produce a consolidated weekly invoice (Friday sweep) that lists each PO and line item. Invoice will still include the Redwood invoice number and a per-PO subtotal.
- Net terms: we can do net30 for the first two weekly runs, provided there is a pre-authorized credit holdback equal to 15% of the invoice amount that we will release after the entity mapping is validated on your side. This is a temporary operational control to mitigate risk during the cutover.

I've attached a short mapping guide (Redwood_PO_Mapping_guide.pdf) that shows how we ingest memo strings and what fields we print on the invoice. Can you confirm your procurement system can adopt the ZIP|COSTCENTER|PO-LINE-ID memo pattern? If so, we'll schedule a short handoff call with your AP team to run a mock invoice.

Thanks,
Ethan Park
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
attachments: Redwood_PO_Mapping_guide.pdf (pdf)

From: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@cumulus-health.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap-team@cumulus-health.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Jul 2027 08:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming fiscal cutover — PO memo & invoice lane questions

Ethan — that format works for us. We've updated the template to include the memo pattern and are returning an updated PO template (attached: PO_Template_v3.xlsx).

Couple of follow-ups:
- Please confirm the exact field name you will print on the invoice for the PO-Line-ID — our ERP team wants to map it to a GL dimension named "PO_LINE_REF".
- For the credit holdback, can you issue an automated credit note referencing the original invoice number when you release the holdback? Our auditors want a clear linkage.

Forwarding our AP lead's availability for a quick mock on Thurs 7/29 at 10:00 AM PT.

On Mon, Jul 26, 2027 at 10:03 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Hi Sofia — thanks for the detail and the docs. Short answers up front: yes, we can accept the PO-Line-ID and the entity ZIP in the PO memo, and we can run a weekly consolidated invoice for the cutover period. We can also make a short-term net30 exception for the first two runs, subject to the credit handoff described below.
> 
> Operational notes / options:
> - PO memo mapping: we will map the memo string to two fields in our billing system (entity_zip, cost_center) using a simple regex. Please format the memo as: ZIP|COSTCENTER|PO-LINE-ID (e.g., 94107|IT-ENG|PL-12345) so the parsing is deterministic.
> - Invoice cadence: we can produce a consolidated weekly invoice (Friday sweep) that lists each PO and line item. Invoice will still include the Redwood invoice number and a per-PO subtotal.
> - Net terms: we can do net30 for the first two weekly runs, provided there is a pre-authorized credit holdback equal to 15% of the invoice amount that we will release after the entity mapping is validated on your side. This is a temporary operational control to mitigate risk during the cutover.

Best,
Sofia Ramos
attachments: PO_Template_v3.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: FW: Upcoming fiscal cutover — PO memo & invoice lane questions

Thanks for the thread. Quick internal decision and action items from Finance: we approve the temporary net30 exception for the first two weekly invoices (Deal DL-9821) under the following conditions: 
1) Credit holdback of 15% to be recorded on our invoice as "CUTOVER_HOLDBACK" and released via automated credit note once Cumulus confirms successful GL mapping (we will produce the credit note referencing the original invoice number).
2) AP must populate the memo exactly as: ZIP|COSTCENTER|PO-LINE-ID. No deviations — otherwise the invoice will be queued for manual review and may hit the monthly billing lane.
3) Tag all invoices for the period with billing run label: Q3CUTOVER so engineering can route them to the weekly consolidation job.

Action items:
- Ethan: implement memo parsing rule and add invoice field label "PO_LINE_REF" for printing (ETA: Thu 7/29 EOD).
- Vivek: join mock call with Cumulus AP on Thurs 7/29 10:00 AM PT and confirm procurement will send the updated PO template.
- Finance (Marissa): I will prepare the credit-note workflow and confirm the automated reversal steps are in our billing system.

I'll loop back once the credit-note process is validated. If any of you foresee downstream risks (reconciliation, accounting lag, or tax implications) call me directly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Manager, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
attachments: none

> Ethan — that format works for us. We've updated the template to include the memo pattern and are returning an updated PO template (attached: PO_Template_v3.xlsx).
> 
> Couple of follow-ups:
> - Please confirm the exact field name you will print on the invoice for the PO-Line-ID — our ERP team wants to map it to a GL dimension named "PO_LINE_REF".
> - For the credit holdback, can you issue an automated credit note referencing the original invoice number when you release the holdback? Our auditors want a clear linkage.
> 
> Forwarding our AP lead's availability for a quick mock on Thurs 7/29 at 10:00 AM PT.

source.subject: Re: Demo sequencing and slot consolidation after regional outage
messages:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Weber <alex.weber@nimbusanalytics.com>; Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 17 Jul 2027 14:10:00 -0000
Subject: Nimbus Analytics — initial demo & preflight checklist

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for the time on the intro call. Per our conversation, we'd like to schedule a 90-minute technical walkthrough + a short procurement sync to cover licensing/terms. Proposed slots: Monday 19 Jul 10:00–11:30 PDT or Tuesday 20 Jul 13:00–14:30 PDT.

Attached are a short agenda and a preflight checklist so your team can confirm network/SSO access ahead of time.

High level plan:
- 90 min technical demo (AE+SE present) — architecture, routing, quantization profile suggestions
- 15 min procurement follow-up (Marissa + Carla)

Quick ask: please confirm attendees (security, infra, procurement) so we can tailor the deep-dive.

Thanks,
Soojin
Soojin Lee | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: NIM-demo-agenda.pdf
Attachment: NIM-preflight-checklist.xlsx

From: Alex Weber <alex.weber@nimbusanalytics.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Carla Ruiz <procurement@nimbusanalytics.com>; security@NimbusAnalytics.com
Date: Sun, 18 Jul 2027 08:03:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Nimbus Analytics — initial demo & preflight checklist

Hi Soojin —

Quick update: we hit a regional DC incident over the weekend (our west coast cluster). A subset of the infra team is tied up with incident response through Monday. That impacts a few of the folks we planned to have on the call — including our lead infra SE (J. Morales).

Also, procurement has asked that any demo that surfaces cost estimates include a separate procurement-only lane so they can validate billing terms before engineering invests time.

Can we do two things?
1) Split the engagement: short technical session with a smaller engineer group + later procurement-only go/no-go slot.
2) If possible, move the longer technical session to Tuesday afternoon when our infra rotation clears.

Cc'ing Carla and security to confirm availability.

—Alex
Alex Weber | Staff Engineer, Nimbus Analytics
alex.weber@nimbusanalytics.com

From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Weber <alex.weber@nimbusanalytics.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Jul 2027 12:27:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Nimbus Analytics — initial demo & preflight checklist

Thanks for the heads-up, Alex — sorry to hear about the outage. Totally makes sense to split the sessions. Proposed updated plan:

A) Technical sync (short) — 30 minutes, run Mon 19 Jul 16:00–16:30 PDT: overview + blocker checklist (no deep benchmarks). For that we'll only need Alex + 1 infra SE and our SE.

B) Full technical deep-dive — Tue 20 Jul 13:00–14:30 PDT (90 min) with engineering, SEs, and optional product folks.

C) Procurement-only checkpoint — Tue 20 Jul 15:00–15:30 PDT (Marissa + Carla + procurement). We can provide a short cost worksheet before that slot.

Note: Ethan is traveling Mon–Thu morning (SE on field enablement). If the 90-min deep-dive requires a hands-on demo, Ethan suggests Marcus Lin can step in as backup SE for Tue afternoon. Laura can be available to help with escalation if scheduling slips.

If this works, I'll hold provisional invites and share a pared-down agenda for the Monday quick sync.

Thanks,
Soojin

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Jul 2027 06:05:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Nimbus Analytics — initial demo & preflight checklist

On travel — I can cover the pared-down Monday slot remotely (only 30m) but will be in transit; for the full Tue deep-dive, Marcus can take lead on the live model walk and repro steps. Marcus, are you good to own Tue 13:00–14:30 PDT if confirmed?

Ethan

From: Alex Weber <alex.weber@nimbusanalytics.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Carla Ruiz <procurement@nimbusanalytics.com>; security@NimbusAnalytics.com; Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Jul 2027 09:42:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Nimbus Analytics — initial demo & preflight checklist

source.subject: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Lowe <jordan@healthsync.ai>, Elsa Rivera <elsa@medmorph.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-28T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)

Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet both of you. Short recap below so we have alignment and clear next steps. Please flag anything I missed.

Quick TL;DR
- Primary objective: remove dependency on proprietary generation API (current OpenAI/Anthropic mix) while preserving or improving quality and reducing unit cost.
- Success criteria agreed: <=150ms p95 end-to-end latency (prod-like), embedding parity for semantic search (>=95% of current similarity baseline), and ~3–4x cost reduction on gen tokens for steady-state traffic.

What we heard (pain / driver summary)
- Vendor lock-in: procurement wants multi-vendor or self-host option to avoid single-provider pricing shocks.
- Cost pressure: current token spend ~1.2B tokens/mo (generation) + ~500k embeddings/mo; past quarter cost growth ~34%.
- Quality & safety: cannot regress on hallucination / safety postures — Anthropic used for higher-assurance prompts.
- Data controls & residency: EU data-at-rest requirement for certain patient flows; KMS-managed key material required.

Current stack (from the call)
- Generation: OpenAI (davinci-like) for product features, Anthropic for safety-critical assistant flows.
- Embeddings: OpenAI embeddings for search and rerank.
- Orchestration: home-grown router that abstracts model endpoints; ~120 concurrency peak during morning sync windows.

Initial model shortlist we discussed (Redwood POV)
- Llama 3 (quantized 70B variants) — good instruction-following and broad community benchmarks.
- Mistral Large / Mix — strong quality-per-parameter, promising for cost.
- Falcon-40B instruct variants — latency and open licensing fit some internal ops.
Notes: quantization + curated fine-tuning / instruction-tuning will be required to hit parity. We also mentioned the option of a hybrid routing policy (open model for bulk generation, Anthropic fallback for safety-critical paths).

Proposed pilot (two-week technical pilot + evaluation)
1) Redwood runs a controlled benchmark using a HealthSync sample prompt set (you shared a small sample on the call). We will report quality (ROUGE / embedding-sim), latency (p50/p95), and cost projections.
2) Deploy a dedicated pilot instance (isolated pool) we can spin up in your preferred cloud region; KMS integration and EU data routing configured.
3) Safety baseline: run your highest-risk prompts through Anthropic + candidate models to map divergence and failure modes.
4) Deliverables: benchmark report, recommended model + quantization profile, pilot pricing estimate, and short runbook for cutover.

Immediate asks from you
- Share a ~100–200 prompt sample and a typical embedding query set (CSV okay).
- Confirm whether EU-only data residency applies to the pilot or just production.
- Security contact for questionnaire coordination (we can pre-fill and return).

Next steps / proposed timeline
- Today:E1 — Ben to send pilot pricing estimate + Redwood pilot checklist (attached).
- By Tue 07/02: HealthSync to share sample prompts + security contact.
- Week of 07/05: Technical deep-dive with Redwood infra team (Grace + Ethan) — 60–90m.
- Two-week pilot starts within 3 business days after sample + access are provided.

Attachments: healthsync_sample_prompts.csv (placeholder), redwood_pilot_pricing_estimate.xlsx (pricing stub).

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142
[Attachment: redwood_pilot_pricing_estimate.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

From: Jordan Lowe <jordan@healthsync.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Elsa Rivera <elsa@medmorph.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-28T15:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)

Thanks Ben — nice write-up and aligned on the ask. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Embeddings baseline: we'll send a set of ~2k representative queries and your team should report cosine similarity distribution vs our OpenAI baseline. We care most about nearest-neighbor ranking stability for our clinical note search.

2) Data residency: EU-only for PHI-adjacent flows; non-PHI can be routed to US. For the pilot we prefer EU routing for any sample data we send. KMS integration is a hard requirement for production (we will provide key rotation policy).

3) Security: attaching our security requirements matrix — can you flag gaps quickly? We need SOC2 + reasoning about ephemeral logging while the pilot runs.

4) Commercial: procurement asked for a simple SOW + pricing band for 0–2B tokens and an option for a dedicated reserved pool if the pilot looks good.

We'll share the prompt CSV and the security contact (Elsa, looping you) by Tue 07/02 as discussed.

Jordan
Jordan Lowe
Head of Integrations | HealthSync
jordan@healthsync.ai | +1 (617) 555-0123
[Attachment: healthsync_security_requirements.pdf (pdf)]

On Jun 28, 2027, at 15:02, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet both of you. Short recap below so we have alignment and clear next steps. Please flag anything I missed.
> ...

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-28T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)

Ben — thanks for the recap. I'll own the technical deep-dive and the benchmark run. Quick notes from my side: 

- I'll ask Ethan to pre-run a small compatibility pass (embedding parity + instruction-following) against a 100-sample subset once HealthSync drops the CSV. That will let us validate toolchain latency assumptions before the full benchmark.
- For EU KMS routing: ops can provision a pilot region with customer-managed keys; I'll put together the steps and a short checklist for the security team.
- Priyom, can you draft a concise SOW that covers pilot scope + reserved-pool option? I included the template link here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-healthsync-pilot

Will sync up with you both tomorrow AM to align on who owns what before the deep-dive.

Grace
Grace O'Connor
Platform Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com
(Attachments referenced above are stubs; actual files stored in the HealthSync pilot Drive folder.)

1) Redwood runs a controlled benchmark using a HealthSync sample prompt set (you shared a small sample on the call). We will report quality (ROUGE / embedding-sim), latency (p50/p95), and cost projections.
2) Deploy a dedicated pilot instance (isolated pool) we can spin up in your preferred cloud region; KMS integration and EU data routing configured.
3) Safety baseline: run your highest-risk prompts through Anthropic + candidate models to map divergence and failure modes.
4) Deliverables: benchmark report, recommended model + quantization profile, pilot pricing estimate, and short runbook for cutover.

Immediate asks from you
- Share a ~100–200 prompt sample and a typical embedding query set (CSV okay).
- Confirm whether EU-only data residency applies to the pilot or just production.
- Security contact for questionnaire coordination (we can pre-fill and return).

Next steps / proposed timeline
- Today:E1 — Ben to send pilot pricing estimate + Redwood pilot checklist (attached).
- By Tue 07/02: HealthSync to share sample prompts + security contact.
- Week of 07/05: Technical deep-dive with Redwood infra team (Grace + Ethan) — 60–90m.
- Two-week pilot starts within 3 business days after sample + access are provided.

Attachments: healthsync_sample_prompts.csv (placeholder), redwood_pilot_pricing_estimate.xlsx (pricing stub).

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142
[Attachment: redwood_pilot_pricing_estimate.xlsx (spreadsheet)]


From: Jordan Lowe <jordan@healthsync.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Elsa Rivera <elsa@medmorph.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-28T15:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)

Thanks Ben — nice write-up and aligned on the ask. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Embeddings baseline: we'll send a set of ~2k representative queries and your team should report cosine similarity distribution vs our OpenAI baseline. We care most about nearest-neighbor ranking stability for our clinical note search.

2) Data residency: EU-only for PHI-adjacent flows; non-PHI can be routed to US. For the pilot we prefer EU routing for any sample data we send. KMS integration is a hard requirement for production (we will provide key rotation policy).

3) Security: attaching our security requirements matrix — can you flag gaps quickly? We need SOC2 + reasoning about ephemeral logging while the pilot runs.

4) Commercial: procurement asked for a simple SOW + pricing band for 0–2B tokens and an option for a dedicated reserved pool if the pilot looks good.

We'll share the prompt CSV and the security contact (Elsa, looping you) by Tue 07/02 as discussed.

Jordan
Jordan Lowe
Head of Integrations | HealthSync
jordan@healthsync.ai | +1 (617) 555-0123
[Attachment: healthsync_security_requirements.pdf (pdf)]

On Jun 28, 2027, at 15:02, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet both of you. Short recap below so we have alignment and clear next steps. Please flag anything I missed.
> ...


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-28T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: HealthSync migration goals & next steps (proprietary API -> open models)

Ben — thanks for the recap. I'll own the technical deep-dive and the benchmark run. Quick notes from my side: 

- I'll ask Ethan to pre-run a small compatibility pass (embedding parity + instruction-following) against a 100-sample subset once HealthSync drops the CSV. That will let us validate toolchain latency assumptions before the full benchmark.
- For EU KMS routing: ops can provision a pilot region with customer-managed keys; I'll put together the steps and a short checklist for the security team.
- Priyom, can you draft a concise SOW that covers pilot scope + reserved-pool option? I included the template link here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-healthsync-pilot

Will sync up with you both tomorrow AM to align on who owns what before the deep-dive.

Grace
Grace O'Connor
Platform Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com


(Attachments referenced above are stubs; actual files stored in the HealthSync pilot Drive folder.)

source.subject: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Rogers <ben.rogers@ventureapps.com>, Alice Chen <alice.chen@ventureapps.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics

Hi Ben / Alice — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can lock the run in: 

Summary of goals:
- Verify production throughput and tail latency under a customer-driven Locust profile (read-heavy, many streaming responses).
- Confirm SLOs for the integration: p50 150ms, p95 500ms, p99 900ms for 512-token responses (non-streaming) and sustained streaming throughput of ~400 tokens/sec for streaming endpoints.
- Validate concurrency model: support up to 120 concurrent user sessions with a 30m warmup.

Proposed test details (first pass):
- Test driver: Locust (we attached locust-scenarios.yaml).
- Target sustained request rate: 600 requests/minute (burst to 900/min during ramp).
- Concurrency: target 120 users, ramp 0->120 over 15 minutes, steady for 60 minutes, 30-minute cool-down.
- Streaming: we will exercise the chunked response path for the streaming endpoints; please let us know if you need SSE vs. chunked HTTP framing.

Logistics asks from VentureApps (so we can provision):
- IP allowlist for test runner(s) (we will provide our public egress IPs if you prefer us to be allowed; otherwise provide your allowlist) — see required format in ip-allowlist.json (attached).
- Test accounts / test API keys: we can create short-lived keys scoped to a dedicated test pool; please confirm where to send keys.
- Test window: we proposed Wed 2027-06-23 10:00 - 12:00 PT as primary, Thu 2027-06-24 09:00 - 11:00 PT as backup.

Action items for Redwood:
- Provide dedicated test pool name and routing details (DNS + model pinning).
- Create short-lived test API keys and share securely.
- Stand up a lightweight fallback policy (smaller model variant) if throughput or latency breach occurs.

Attachments: locust-scenarios.yaml (scenario fragments), mutual-action-plan.xlsx (MVP checklist).

Next steps: Ben/Alice — confirm the test window and whether you prefer Redwood to egress from our IPs or to open your allowlist. Once confirmed we will provision the pool and share keys.

Cheers,
Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Rogers <ben.rogers@ventureapps.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Chen <alice.chen@ventureapps.com>, VentureApps QA <qa@ventureapps.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics

Ethan — thanks for the clear write-up. A couple quick clarifications before we lock times: 

1) Warmup behavior: do you want us to run a cache-warm script against the same endpoints before the Locust ramp, or do you rely on a steady ramp inside Locust? We want to avoid cold-cache artifacts affecting p95/p99.

2) Streaming framing: VentureApps uses SSE for streaming in production; can you exercise SSE in the scenario or should we adapt to chunked HTTP?

3) IP allowlist: we can open outbound IPs on our firewall. Please send your egress CIDR blocks and any hostname/DNS we should whitelist.

We prefer the Wed window but want the warmup question answered so we can schedule our SREs.

Ben
Director of Engineering | VentureApps

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Rogers <ben.rogers@ventureapps.com>, Alice Chen <alice.chen@ventureapps.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jun 2027 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics

Hey Ben — great questions. Short answers and a couple of concrete notes: 

1) Warmup: we recommend a two-step approach — a short cache-warm job (5-10 minutes) executed from your QA runners hitting a /warmup endpoint we can provision, followed by the Locust ramp. That prevents synthetic tail spikes from cold caches. We'll include the warmup snippet in ip-allowlist.json for runner guidance.

2) SSE: we can exercise SSE — the attached locust-scenarios.yaml contains an SSE task that matches VentureApps' streaming framing. If you prefer we will push an alternate chunked example.

3) IPs: attached ip-allowlist.json shows our preferred egress ranges and hostname entries (these are the minimal set we need). If your firewall team prefers single-hostnames over CIDRs we can provide DNS names.

Provisioning notes:
- Test pool name: test_pool_RDW_2147 (will be created by Marissa).
- We'll issue short-lived API keys with 2-hour TTL scoped to the test pool and streaming endpoints only; I will send those via the secure channel once you confirm the window.
- DocuSign: we need a signed test AUP before running (link in related_links).

Proposed schedule (final):
- Wed 2027-06-23 09:30 PT: QA warmup (5-10m)
- Wed 2027-06-23 10:00 PT: Locust ramp start -> steady run 10:15 - 11:15 PT
- Wed 2027-06-23 11:15 PT: cool-down 11:45 PT; debrief 11:45 - 12:00 PT

If that looks good I'll coordinate with Marissa to create the pool and with Soojin to ensure we have the right model pinning configuration.

Thanks,
Ethan

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Ben Rogers <ben.rogers@ventureapps.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jun 2027 10:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics

Looping in operations notes and what I will do on the Redwood side: 

Operational items I will complete by EOD Tue 2027-06-22:
- Create dedicated test pool: name test_pool_RDW_2147; pin to model family 'redwood-large-v2' for parity.
- Configure routing policy with a canary fallback to 'redwood-medium-v2' at 20% if latency > p95 target for >5 minutes.
- Provision DNS endpoint: test-pool-rdw-2147.redwood.net (use this for IP allowlist if you prefer host-based allow).
- Generate short-lived keys and upload to secure key share; Ethan will notify when they are ready.
- Reserve two on-call SREs for the window.

Notes: Please ensure your warmup hits the same request paths and payload sizes you plan to use in Locust. If you plan to drive large-batch streaming (long-form responses), include a smaller subset of heavier sessions during warmup so caches heat appropriately.

I'll also add the mutual-action-plan.xlsx to the test folder and mark owners against each step.

-Marissa
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Rogers <ben.rogers@ventureapps.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Chen <alice.chen@ventureapps.com>, VentureApps QA <qa@ventureapps.com>
Date: Wed, 23 Jun 2027 15:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Kickoff: customer-led Locust run - SLOs & logistics

Thanks Ethan / Marissa — that all sounds good. We confirm the Wed 2027-06-23 09:30 PT warmup + 10:00 PT ramp schedule. Our SREs will run the warmup script and then hand over to the Locust team.

Items for our side: 
- We will whitelist test-pool-rdw-2147.redwood.net and the CIDR ranges in ip-allowlist.json.
- QA contact for the run: qa@ventureapps.com (they will own Locust exec and log uploads).
- We have signed the test AUP and pushed the DocuSign envelope (see related link).

Please go ahead and provision the pool and send the test keys when ready. We'll upload any sample outputs to the shared drive folder after the run.

See you Wednesday.

Ben

source.subject: Recap & SOC 2 packet — next steps and any blockers
messages:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@chartersoft.com>

source.subject: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>, DataWave Procurement <procurement@datawave.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-06T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

Olivia / procurement team —

Thanks again for the time on Friday. We’re excited to support DataWave’s PoC. To help speed procurement, I put together a concise proposal for the private offer pricing and how we’d handle partner/reseller economics. High-level asks:

- Confirm whether you need list-price passback or netback-invoice (this changes routing).
- Confirm desired reseller uplift target (you mentioned ~10% on the call).
- Agree which SKUs will be reserved-capacity vs metered for the pilot (affects discounting).

Attached is a quick workback (pricing_workback.xlsx) showing two options:
A) Anchored discount: deeper list discount to DW, 12% reseller uplift pass-through (simpler invoicing)
B) Surgical splits: modest list discount + explicit partner netback line (9% uplift, invoiced to partner) — lower blended cost to DW but needs passback mapping.

If you can confirm runway (3 months vs 6 months) and whether your reseller will accept a netback line on invoices, we’ll finalize numbers and get DocuSign-ready.

Next steps I propose: once you pick A or B I’ll run the channel passback sim with finance and loop in Ben for tax/invoice routing.

Thanks,
Ethan
Ethan Park
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192
Redwood Inference

Attachments: pricing_workback.xlsx (spreadsheet, 42 KB)
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: DataWave Procurement <procurement@datawave.ai>
Date: 2027-06-06T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

Hi Ethan —

Thanks for the note and the workbook. A couple quick clarifications from procurement:

1) We prefer netback shown on the invoice (so partner sees a line showing their fee) rather than list-price passback — this helps our finance team reconcile.
2) The reseller uplift we discussed is a hard requirement for our partner: they need at least 10% netback retained after tax/withholding.
3) The PoC will be a 3-month engagement initially; if it meets the KPIs we’ll extend to 6 months with reserved capacity.

Can you confirm whether option B (surgical splits) can be set up so the partner’s 10% is protected even with a modest list discount to DataWave? Also, we’ll need an evidence doc that shows how you route reseller netbacks for marketplace private offers (audit purpose).

If that works, we’ll sign the PO once the DocuSign has the final numbers.

Regards,
Olivia
Olivia Chen
Head of Cloud Partnerships | DataWave
olivia.chen@datawave.ai

--- quoted text ---
On Jun 6, 2027, at 10:12 AM, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Olivia / procurement team —
> 
> Thanks again for the time on Friday. We’re excited to support DataWave’s PoC....
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>
Date: 2027-06-07T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

Irene / Ben / Laura —

Looping you in. Procurement says they prefer netback-on-invoice and the reseller needs a protected 10% uplift. PoC is 3 months, then reserve potential. Can we do a configuration where:

- Public list discount to DataWave is ~8% (keeps gateway simple)
- Partner netback is a separate invoice line guaranteeing 10% of base revenue (pre-tax), with Redwood absorbing any marketplace fee delta up to 1.5% (so partner netback is protected)

Irene — can you do a quick simulation and confirm whether finance is comfortable with Redwood taking up to 1.5% delta? Also procurement asked for audit evidence showing how we route these netbacks on AWS/GCP private offers. I attached the initial workbook.

Thanks — please reply with the simplest yes/no and any red flags.

Ethan

Attachments: pricing_workback.xlsx

--- forwarded message ---
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-06T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

(see above)
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>
Date: 2027-06-08T11:46:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

Ethan / team —

Quick analysis: option B (surgical splits + explicit partner netback line) is feasible for AWS and GCP private offers. Key notes:

- We can model the partner netback as a separate invoice line tied to the private-offer SKU; that meets DW’s reconciliation requirement.
- To protect a 10% partner uplift, Redwood can guarantee up to 1.25% delta vs marketplace fee (not 1.5%). Finance flags any guarantee above ~1.5% as a write-down risk for PoC scale; 1.25% is within expected tolerances for a 3-month pilot.
- Tax/withholding: we’ll put language in the SOW that partner netback is gross and partner is responsible for local taxes; if withholding applies to Redwood payments we’ll flow through the grossed-up amount to preserve 10%.
- Evidence: attaching our reseller routing policy and a short example invoice that shows the netback line.

Proposed final numbers (3-month PoC):
- List price to DW: $14,500 / month (8% discount applied)
- Partner netback: 10% of base ($1,450 / month) — delivered as separate invoice line to partner. Redwood guarantees shortfall up to 1.25% of base (~$181/month) for duration of PoC.

If that sounds good I can draft the SOW language and we’ll hand off to legal for a short amendment.

Irene Choi
Director, Deal Ops | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.ai

Attachments: evidence_reseller_policy.pdf (PDF, 128 KB); example_invoice_netback.pdf (PDF, 45 KB)

--- quoted text ---
On Jun 7, 2027, at 9:22 AM, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Irene / Ben / Laura —
> 
> Looping you in. Procurement says they prefer netback-on-invoice...
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-09T08:14:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

All — quick thumbs up from finance ops. A couple operational points:

- We’ll route the partner netback to the partner’s payee ID on the invoice; tax gross-up language goes into the SOW as Irene suggested.
- For the guarantee cap, finance accepts 1.25% delta for a 3-month PoC.
- Please note: if usage spikes > 2x forecast we’ll need to re-open terms for the remainder of the month. Add a clause that overage triggers re-pricing notice.

If that works for DW, Ethan — send the revised workbook and I’ll prep the DocuSign package.

Ben Carter
Manager, Financial Operations | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

--- quoted text ---
On Jun 8, 2027, at 11:46 AM, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Ethan / team —
> 
> Quick analysis: option B (surgical splits + explicit partner netback line) is feasible...
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@datawave.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-09T15:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing: surgical splits and partner netbacks for DataWave PoC

Ethan / team —

Thanks — that set of answers works for us. DataWave accepts option B (surgical splits) with the 1.25% Redwood guarantee for the 3-month PoC. Please include the overage re-pricing clause Ben mentioned. We’ll route the PO once the DocuSign is live.

I’ve shared the DocuSign link below for signature. We expect our procurement team to finish approvals within 24 hours.

DocuSign: https://redwood.app/docusign/DW-2027-091

Olivia Chen
Head of Cloud Partnerships | DataWave

--- quoted text ---
On Jun 9, 2027, at 8:14 AM, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> All — quick thumbs up from finance ops. A couple operational points:...

source.subject: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 23 May 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Hi Sanjay / Elena —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and the concrete options we discussed so the team can align:

Summary of pain and goals
- Real-time triage assistant for your nurse intake flow; target p95 latency under concurrent load.
- Current baseline: internal tests show 200–300ms p95 at low concurrency but spikes to 700ms at peak due to KV cache churn and short lived sessions.
- Primary business constraint: minimize per-request cost while keeping response SLAs for 99% of patient-facing requests.

Technical constraints you mentioned
- Typical request size: 150–400 tokens (prompt + context)
- Peak concurrent RPS (expected during morning windows): ~80 concurrent inference sessions
- Safety requirement: model outputs must be auditable; retention of prompts for 90 days in encrypted storage

Options we reviewed (tradeoffs)
1) Hosted small-model routing (7B quantized) — Pros: lowest infra cost, easy scale; Cons: slightly higher hallucination risk vs 13B, but can add quality checks and prompt engineering. Estimated cost: ~ $0.75 per 1k requests (rough).
2) Dedicated capacity with mixed model routing — primary: 13B int8 for higher quality at p95 ~150-200ms with KV cache; fallback: 7B quantized when burst capacity needed. Pros: consistent latency; Cons: higher monthly commitment.
3) Hybrid private VPC for PHI-sensitive endpoints + hosted for non-PHI routable inference — Pros: compliance; Cons: operational overhead.

Immediate performance notes from our runtime team
- KV / prefix caching reduces repeated-token compute by ~30–45% for your typical templates.
- Continuous batching helps throughput but increases tail latency; our routing policy can cap batch windows to preserve p95.
- Quantization to INT4 reduces memory footprint and cost but we recommend a quick eval suite — quality delta for your few-shot prompts is model-dependent.

Next steps (agreed on call)
- Redwood: produce a discreet latency profile CSV from our synthetic run matching your traffic pattern (+ attached).
- Verdant: share a sanitized sample prompt set (5–10 examples) and expected acceptance criteria for hallucination/precision.
- Both: align on a 4-week POC scope — objective: demonstrate p95 <= 200ms at 80 concurrent sessions with quality thresholds.

I'll attach the latency profile and a draft mutual action plan for the POC. Please flag anything I missed.

Thanks,
Ethan Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: verdant_latency_profile.csv (synthetic run, sequence-length buckets)
Attachment: mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx

From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 23 May 2027 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Ethan — thanks for the concise write-up. Couple clarifications from our side:

- The 80 concurrent figure is conservative; we may see short spikes to 120 for 2–3 minutes on weekday mornings. Important that fallback behavior is predictable.
- For the sanitized prompt set we'll redact PHI and deliver 12 representative prompts by Tuesday COB.
- Cost sensitivity: we prefer minimizing variable per-token spend with a willingness to commit to reserved capacity if it reduces unit cost by ~25% over hosted.

Also, question on model choices — you mentioned INT4 quantization. If quality loss is >2% on our precision metric, we can't accept it for the primary path. Can you add quantization evaluation to the POC?

I'll review the attachments and come back with any edits to the MAP.

— Sanjay
Director, ML Systems — Verdant Health
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Thanks Ethan & Sanjay — adding a few operational items so the POC runbook is clear:

- Instrumentation: we will expose per-token and end-to-end latency, KV cache hit ratio, and p50/p95/p99 for both model variants. We'll provide trace IDs for any request that exceeds p95.
- Rollout plan for the POC: week 0: baseline profiling (no caching), week 1: caching enabled + small model routing, week 2: dedicated upsize test (if reserved commit agreed), week 3: production-mirroring and acceptance.
- Security/compliance: Redwood will store logs in a customer-dedicated bucket with SSE-KMS. We'll sign an addendum for 90-day prompt retention.

Notes on quantization eval (per Sanjay's ask):
- We'll run side-by-side eval across INT8/INT4 on your sample prompts and return per-example precision and hallucination flags. Acceptance threshold set to <=2% degradation on precision for primary path.

Action items (who/when)
- Verdant (Sanjay) — deliver sanitized prompt set by Tue EOD (24 May).
- Redwood (Ethan/Grace) — run quant/eval and synthetic concurrency run; deliver CSV and MAP by Thu (27 May).
- Mutual decision call on reserved capacity economics — Fri 28 May.

Grace O'Connor
Customer Success, Redwood Inference

On Sun, May 23, 2027 at 3:12 PM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sanjay / Elena —
> 
> Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and the concrete options we discussed so the team can align:
> ...
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Sanjay / Elena —

Perfect — appreciate the fast turnaround. Per Grace's timeline, we'll deliver the evaluation outputs and a recommended commit sizing for a Dedicated pool (if cost math favors commit). Attached:
- verdant_latency_profile.csv — synthetic run with buckets (50/150/350 token scenarios) showing median and tail under 80/120 concurrency.
- mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx — proposed milestones, acceptance criteria, and expected deliverables.
- poc_infra_sketch.png — lightweight topology sketch (routing, cache placement, VPC egress notes).

Quick excerpt of recommended starting config for POC
- Primary model: 13B int8 (p95 140–180ms w/ KV cache; quality baseline)
- Fallback: 7B int4 quantized (burst capacity, accept slightly higher hallucination risk; used only when dedicated pool saturates)
- Autoscale policy: maintain p95 target by temporarily routing <10% of traffic to fallback rather than increasing batch window.

If this looks right I'll lock the MAP and schedule Redwood infra to run the week-0 baseline profile as Grace outlined. Also adding a link to a short pricing sketch so procurement has numbers: https://drive.redwood.ai/pricing/VD-9231-sketch

Thanks — looking forward to the POC.

Ethan Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: verdant_latency_profile.csv, mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx, poc_infra_sketch.png

- mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx — proposed milestones, acceptance criteria, and expected deliverables.
- poc_infra_sketch.png — lightweight topology sketch (routing, cache placement, VPC egress notes).

Quick excerpt of recommended starting config for POC
- Primary model: 13B int8 (p95 140–180ms w/ KV cache; quality baseline)
- Fallback: 7B int4 quantized (burst capacity, accept slightly higher hallucination risk; used only when dedicated pool saturates)
- Autoscale policy: maintain p95 target by temporarily routing <10% of traffic to fallback rather than increasing batch window.

If this looks right I'll lock the MAP and schedule Redwood infra to run the week-0 baseline profile as Grace outlined. Also adding a link to a short pricing sketch so procurement has numbers: https://drive.redwood.ai/pricing/VD-9231-sketch

Thanks — looking forward to the POC.

Ethan Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: verdant_latency_profile.csv, mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx, poc_infra_sketch.png
text:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 23 May 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Hi Sanjay / Elena —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and the concrete options we discussed so the team can align:

Summary of pain and goals
- Real-time triage assistant for your nurse intake flow; target p95 latency under concurrent load.
- Current baseline: internal tests show 200–300ms p95 at low concurrency but spikes to 700ms at peak due to KV cache churn and short lived sessions.
- Primary business constraint: minimize per-request cost while keeping response SLAs for 99% of patient-facing requests.

Technical constraints you mentioned
- Typical request size: 150–400 tokens (prompt + context)
- Peak concurrent RPS (expected during morning windows): ~80 concurrent inference sessions
- Safety requirement: model outputs must be auditable; retention of prompts for 90 days in encrypted storage

Options we reviewed (tradeoffs)
1) Hosted small-model routing (7B quantized) — Pros: lowest infra cost, easy scale; Cons: slightly higher hallucination risk vs 13B, but can add quality checks and prompt engineering. Estimated cost: ~ $0.75 per 1k requests (rough).
2) Dedicated capacity with mixed model routing — primary: 13B int8 for higher quality at p95 ~150-200ms with KV cache; fallback: 7B quantized when burst capacity needed. Pros: consistent latency; Cons: higher monthly commitment.
3) Hybrid private VPC for PHI-sensitive endpoints + hosted for non-PHI routable inference — Pros: compliance; Cons: operational overhead.

Immediate performance notes from our runtime team
- KV / prefix caching reduces repeated-token compute by ~30–45% for your typical templates.
- Continuous batching helps throughput but increases tail latency; our routing policy can cap batch windows to preserve p95.
- Quantization to INT4 reduces memory footprint and cost but we recommend a quick eval suite — quality delta for your few-shot prompts is model-dependent.

Next steps (agreed on call)
- Redwood: produce a discreet latency profile CSV from our synthetic run matching your traffic pattern (+ attached).
- Verdant: share a sanitized sample prompt set (5–10 examples) and expected acceptance criteria for hallucination/precision.
- Both: align on a 4-week POC scope — objective: demonstrate p95 <= 200ms at 80 concurrent sessions with quality thresholds.

I'll attach the latency profile and a draft mutual action plan for the POC. Please flag anything I missed.

Thanks,
Ethan Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: verdant_latency_profile.csv (synthetic run, sequence-length buckets)
Attachment: mutual_action_plan_VD-9231.xlsx



From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 23 May 2027 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Ethan — thanks for the concise write-up. Couple clarifications from our side:

- The 80 concurrent figure is conservative; we may see short spikes to 120 for 2–3 minutes on weekday mornings. Important that fallback behavior is predictable.
- For the sanitized prompt set we'll redact PHI and deliver 12 representative prompts by Tuesday COB.
- Cost sensitivity: we prefer minimizing variable per-token spend with a willingness to commit to reserved capacity if it reduces unit cost by ~25% over hosted.

Also, question on model choices — you mentioned INT4 quantization. If quality loss is >2% on our precision metric, we can't accept it for the primary path. Can you add quantization evaluation to the POC?

I'll review the attachments and come back with any edits to the MAP.

— Sanjay
Director, ML Systems — Verdant Health


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@verdanthealth.com>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@verdanthealth.com>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Latency vs cost tradeoffs — post-call deep dive & POC scope

Thanks Ethan & Sanjay — adding a few operational items so the POC runbook is clear:

- Instrumentation: we will expose per-token and end-to-end latency, KV cache hit ratio, and p50/p95/p99 for both model variants. We'll provide trace IDs for any request that exceeds p95.
- Rollout plan for the POC: week 0: baseline profiling (no caching), week 1: caching enabled + small model routing, week 2: dedicated upsize test (if reserved commit agreed), week 3: production-mirroring and acceptance.
- Security/compliance: Redwood will store logs in a customer-dedicated bucket with SSE-KMS. We'll sign an addendum for 90-day prompt retention.

Notes on quantization eval (per Sanjay's ask):
- We'll run side-by-side eval across INT8/INT4 on your sample prompts and return per-example precision and hallucination flags. Acceptance threshold set to <=2% degradation on precision for primary path.

Action items (who/when)
- Verdant (Sanjay) — deliver sanitized prompt set by Tue EOD (24 May).
- Redwood (Ethan/Grace) — run quant/eval and synthetic concurrency run; deliver CSV and MAP by Thu (27 May).
- Mutual decision call on reserved capacity economics — Fri 28 May.

Grace O'Connor
Customer Success, Redwood Inference

Hi Sam / Priya —

Thanks again for the interest in Redwood. Quick note to propose a pair of demo slots that would let us do a smooth US SDR -> EU AE handoff so you get both a product intro and region-specific pricing/architecture conversation in the same session. I know daylight savings is in effect in a few regions so I included both local times.

Option A (preferred): Tue, 30 May — 4:00 PM PDT / 7:00 PM EDT / Wed, 31 May — 00:00 BST (late for EU)
Option B (alternative): Wed, 31 May — 9:00 AM PDT / 12:00 PM EDT / 17:00 BST (working evening for EU)

If neither works, tell me two windows that work for your team next week and I’ll flip the calendar. Naomi, tagging you — I’m proposing I do a 10–15 minute account context + discovery then hand over to you for the product deep-dive and next steps. Subject: Re: Twi-light handoff — proposed demo slots & follow-up ownership

Two small asks for the handoff:
- Can we shift the run-of-show so the first 10–12 minutes are SDR-led discovery and context, then I take over for the product demo and a 10-minute Q&A? That helps keep the EU pricing/region questions together.
- If the customer wants a follow-up deep-dive on deployment (VPC/KMS specifics), I’ll need a short follow-up meeting in their local BST window (09:00–16:00 BST).

Irene — if you confirm Option B, I’ll add a short pre-demo alignment note to the invite (5–10 mins) so we’re clear on who covers which topics.

—Ethan

source.subject: Post-demo: audit-log extract — delivery options, schema, and proofing
messages:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Grant <alyssa.grant@sigmahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: audit-log extract — delivery options, schema, and proofing

Hi Alyssa — thanks again for the demo time today. Quick recap and concrete asks so we can scope the post-demo follow-up and confirm timelines: 

1) Scope we discussed
- Audit log extract covering "who/what/when" for model inference requests and admin actions (access to keys, role changes).
- Initial window: 90 days for the POC; Sigma may request longer retention for production.

2) Clarifying questions for your security team (can you confirm):
- Do you need raw request text included, or only metadata + redacted snippet?
- Preferred identifier approach: pseudonymous hashed user_id vs full user_id (we generally recommend one-way SHA256 with salt).
- Export delivery preference: S3 (customer-managed KMS), SFTP, or secure SharePoint link?

3) Export format we can provide (examples) — please indicate preference:
- CSV (newline-delimited) + gzip, UTF-8, UTC timestamps
- Parquet (schema'd) for large volumes
- JSONL for event-forwarding pipelines

4) Acceptance criteria for the POC export
- Column-level PII flags and explicit field list
- Timezone normalized to UTC; timestamps in ISO8601
- Hashing algorithm and salt handling documented

Can you send: a sample of your preferred schema (column list) and any must-have/forbidden fields? If you can attach the security artifact (PII field list) that would speed us up.

Proposed next steps:
- You share the schema + example extract or spec (small sample, ~100 rows)
- We provide a short export spec and example CSV (schema-preview)
- Technical handoff call (30 min) to confirm delivery authentication and KMS details — propose next Wed 10–11am PT if that works for you.

source.subject: [EXTERNAL] Acme - sudden p99 tail on embeddings after catalog prune
messages:
From: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Hi Marissa,

We're seeing a sharp increase in p99 latency on the /v1/embeddings endpoint starting ~08:45 PDT after the model catalog prune that was rolled out this morning. p95 moved a bit, but p99 has gone from ~420ms to ~2.3s and some requests are timing out (504). Attached is a screenshot of our Grafana panel and a small trace we captured.

This is impacting semantic search for two high-traffic flows. Can you confirm whether the catalog prune included any model variant removals or new routing defaults? We need an ETA on mitigation — if this requires a rollback please let us know the expected window.

Thanks,
Leo
Lead Data Engineer
Acme Analytics

(attachment: acme_p99_dashboard.png)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Hi Leo — thanks for the alert and the dashboard. We're digging in now. Quick answers up front:

- We did a catalog prune at 08:20 PDT that removed two older 8-bit quantized variants and set a new routing preference to the latest 4-bit quant model for the default embeddings route. No model promotions were performed beyond that.
- I'll loop in SRE and inference to get traces and check routing.

Neha / Avery — can you pick this up? Priority: confirm if routing fell back to a slower variant or if there's a cache/kv-cache cold-start behavior causing the long tails.

Leo — we'll follow up with a mitigation plan within 60 minutes.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior AM, Accounts (Enterprise)
Redwood Inference

---
attachment: trace_snippet_20270514.txt
From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Marissa / team — quick technical note from infra: initial traces show a pattern consistent with KV-cache cold-starts on the promoted 4-bit model variants. The prune removed the warm 8-bit replicas that previously absorbed tail traffic. Two observations:

- At high concurrency the scheduler appears to be routing long-tail traffic to pods that had empty KV caches; first requests show tokenization + KV restore that pushes single-request latency into ~1.8–2.4s range.
- We also saw an uptick in model load events for the promoted variant between 08:30–08:40 which aligns with the customer pulses.

Immediate mitigations we can do now (I will start executing):
1) Temporarily set routing policy for Acme's default route to prefer the older 8-bit variant (roll back routing preference) — this should recover p99 within one pod warmup window (~5–10m).
2) Enable proactive warming for the promoted variant in the background (non-blocking) to reduce cold starts.
3) Re-hydrate KV cache from snapshot for hot pods serving Acme traffic.

I'll coordinate with SRE to implement 1) now and report back. If routing rollback is approved by Marissa we'll proceed.

-Neha
Staff Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:28 AM Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Leo — thanks for the alert and the dashboard. We're digging in now. Quick answers up front:
> ...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:55:00 -0700
Subject: ACTION: Approve routing rollback for Acme default embeddings route

Team — I approve Neha's proposed immediate mitigation #1: roll Acme back to the older 8-bit variant as a routing preference until we fully warm the promoted 4-bit variant. Please execute now and confirm when p99 is back under 600ms.

Also: Ethan, please prepare a short exec note I can send to Acme leadership summarizing cause, mitigation, and next steps (temp rollback, warming plan, plan to revisit catalog pruning process).

Marissa

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:45 AM Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Marissa / team — quick technical note from infra: ...
From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Status: rollback applied — p99 recovered, follow-ups

Update — rollback applied at 09:58 PDT: routing preference reverted for Acme to the 8-bit variant. Observed results:

- p95: steady ~210–240ms
- p99: recovered to ~480–560ms within two warmup minutes
- No additional 504s observed post-09:59

Follow-ups scheduled:
- Warming job for promoted variant started (non-blocking) — ETA to reach warm cache state for Acme: ~25–40m
- Post-warm A/B test for traffic split to validate cost/latency tradeoffs
- Product follow-up to update catalog pruning checklist to include traffic affinity and incremental routing changes

Leo — apologies for the disruption. We'll send a short exec summary and a follow-up plan once the warmed measurements are in (copied Ethan to draft).

-Neha

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:55 AM Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — I approve Neha's proposed immediate mitigation #1: roll Acme back...
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
To: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 18:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Exec note: summary of cause and next steps (Acme)

Leo — following up with the concise note for your leadership: feel free to forward.

Subject: Incident summary — embeddings p99 spike following catalog prune (May 14)

Summary: A catalog prune deployed on May 14 removed legacy 8-bit replicas and changed the default routing preference to a newer 4-bit model variant. This led to KV-cache cold-starts for a subset of pods serving Acme traffic, producing p99 latency spikes and intermittent 504s for ~40 minutes.

Immediate mitigation: We reverted Acme's routing preference to the 8-bit variant at 09:58 PDT, which restored p99 within two minutes. We have also started a background warming job for the promoted variant.

Next steps (owner):
- Product: update catalog prune checklist to require canary traffic checks for high-affinity customers (Owner: Avery Johnson).
- Infra: implement targeted pre-warming for promoted variants and add a KV-cache hydration mechanism (Owner: Neha Kapoor).
- AM: follow-up with Acme on acceptable timing for a controlled switch to the promoted variant and share cost/latency tradeoffs (Owner: Marissa Cole).

We regret the disruption. We're documenting measures to avoid recurrence and will share measurements after the warm run.

Best,
Ethan Park
Director, Product Ops
Redwood Inference

Acme Analytics

(attachment: acme_p99_dashboard.png)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Hi Leo — thanks for the alert and the dashboard. We're digging in now. Quick answers up front:

- We did a catalog prune at 08:20 PDT that removed two older 8-bit quantized variants and set a new routing preference to the latest 4-bit quant model for the default embeddings route. No model promotions were performed beyond that.
- I'll loop in SRE and inference to get traces and check routing.

Neha / Avery — can you pick this up? Priority: confirm if routing fell back to a slower variant or if there's a cache/kv-cache cold-start behavior causing the long tails.

Leo — we'll follow up with a mitigation plan within 60 minutes.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior AM, Accounts (Enterprise)
Redwood Inference

---
attachment: trace_snippet_20270514.txt


From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Marissa / team — quick technical note from infra: initial traces show a pattern consistent with KV-cache cold-starts on the promoted 4-bit model variants. The prune removed the warm 8-bit replicas that previously absorbed tail traffic. Two observations:

- At high concurrency the scheduler appears to be routing long-tail traffic to pods that had empty KV caches; first requests show tokenization + KV restore that pushes single-request latency into ~1.8–2.4s range.
- We also saw an uptick in model load events for the promoted variant between 08:30–08:40 which aligns with the customer pulses.

Immediate mitigations we can do now (I will start executing):
1) Temporarily set routing policy for Acme's default route to prefer the older 8-bit variant (roll back routing preference) — this should recover p99 within one pod warmup window (~5–10m).
2) Enable proactive warming for the promoted variant in the background (non-blocking) to reduce cold starts.
3) Re-hydrate KV cache from snapshot for hot pods serving Acme traffic.

I'll coordinate with SRE to implement 1) now and report back. If routing rollback is approved by Marissa we'll proceed.

-Neha
Staff Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:28 AM Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Leo — thanks for the alert and the dashboard. We're digging in now. Quick answers up front:
> ...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:55:00 -0700
Subject: ACTION: Approve routing rollback for Acme default embeddings route

Team — I approve Neha's proposed immediate mitigation #1: roll Acme back to the older 8-bit variant as a routing preference until we fully warm the promoted 4-bit variant. Please execute now and confirm when p99 is back under 600ms.

Also: Ethan, please prepare a short exec note I can send to Acme leadership summarizing cause, mitigation, and next steps (temp rollback, warming plan, plan to revisit catalog pruning process).

Marissa

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:45 AM Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Marissa / team — quick technical note from infra: ...


From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Status: rollback applied — p99 recovered, follow-ups

Update — rollback applied at 09:58 PDT: routing preference reverted for Acme to the 8-bit variant. Observed results:

- p95: steady ~210–240ms
- p99: recovered to ~480–560ms within two warmup minutes
- No additional 504s observed post-09:59

Follow-ups scheduled:
- Warming job for promoted variant started (non-blocking) — ETA to reach warm cache state for Acme: ~25–40m
- Post-warm A/B test for traffic split to validate cost/latency tradeoffs
- Product follow-up to update catalog pruning checklist to include traffic affinity and incremental routing changes

Leo — apologies for the disruption. We'll send a short exec summary and a follow-up plan once the warmed measurements are in (copied Ethan to draft).

-Neha

On Fri, May 14, 2027 at 09:55 AM Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — I approve Neha's proposed immediate mitigation #1: roll Acme back...


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
To: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 18:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Exec note: summary of cause and next steps (Acme)

Leo — following up with the concise note for your leadership: feel free to forward.

Subject: Incident summary — embeddings p99 spike following catalog prune (May 14)

Summary: A catalog prune deployed on May 14 removed legacy 8-bit replicas and changed the default routing preference to a newer 4-bit model variant. This led to KV-cache cold-starts for a subset of pods serving Acme traffic, producing p99 latency spikes and intermittent 504s for ~40 minutes.

Immediate mitigation: We reverted Acme's routing preference to the 8-bit variant at 09:58 PDT, which restored p99 within two minutes. We have also started a background warming job for the promoted variant.

Next steps (owner):
- Product: update catalog prune checklist to require canary traffic checks for high-affinity customers (Owner: Avery Johnson).
- Infra: implement targeted pre-warming for promoted variants and add a KV-cache hydration mechanism (Owner: Neha Kapoor).
- AM: follow-up with Acme on acceptable timing for a controlled switch to the promoted variant and share cost/latency tradeoffs (Owner: Marissa Cole).

We regret the disruption. We're documenting measures to avoid recurrence and will share measurements after the warm run.

Best,
Ethan Park
Director, Product Ops
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Leo Smith <leo.smith@acme-analytics.com>

Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: embeddings p99 spike after your catalog change (prod impact)

Hi Marissa,

We're seeing a sharp increase in p99 latency on the /v1/embeddings endpoint starting ~08:45 PDT after the model catalog prune that was rolled out this morning. p95 moved a bit, but p99 has gone from ~420ms to ~2.3s and some requests are timing out (504). Attached is a screenshot of our Grafana panel and a small trace we captured.

This is impacting semantic search for two high-traffic flows. Can you confirm whether the catalog prune included any model variant removals or new routing defaults? We need an ETA on mitigation — if this requires a rollback please let us know the expected window.

Thanks,
Leo
Lead Data Engineer
Acme Analytics
