source.subject: Senior Infra candidate — geo band clarity & expedited sign-by
messages:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Hi team,

Quick heads-up on Alex Drummond (Senior Infra). He surfaced a competing offer from BigCloud this morning — base is ~10% higher for EU location and they included an aggressive sign-on. Alex says he prefers Redwood but needs clarity on two things before he can hold:

- Whether we can apply a geo-based uplift for EU hires (his ask: +10% to base)
- Whether we can confirm an expedited sign-by (he's asking for a firm window: 48 hours from now)

I've attached a small benchmark table (comp-benchmark-may2027.xlsx) showing market comps for EU-based infra hires. Candidate is sensitive to timing; wants to close quickly.

Can we get a quick read on headcount availability and whether we can move approval fast enough to meet his window? If we need a short-term bridge (sign-in-principle + final docs later) that's also an option.

Thanks,

--
Laura Bennett
Technical Recruiter, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
Attachment: comp-benchmark-may2027.xlsx
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Laura — thanks for the heads-up.

My read: Alex is strong on the systems side and will materially improve time-to-market for some infra projects this quarter. I think we can consider a location uplift for EU hires; operationally we need a quick confirmation from headcount/procurement and finance on whether the budget supports a 10% uplift plus a modest sign-on.

Proposed short plan:
- I can offer a provisional package: base +10% (geo uplift) + $12,000 sign-on, contingent on fast pathway sign-off.
- Karthik: can you confirm whether there is spare headcount in the FY27 infra bucket and whether procurement can fast-track PROC-4521? If procurement can sign off within 24h we should be able to meet the 48h ask.
- If procurement needs more time, we can send a "sign-in-principle" email to Alex to hold him for 72h while we finish docs.

Attached: draft offer (offer-letter-v1.pdf) — it's the standard SR Infra template with the uplift and sign-on placeholders populated. If legal/finance need changes, we can iterate quickly.

I'll stand by for Karthik's confirmation and can hop on a 15-min call if helpful.

Ethan
Ethan Park
Hiring Manager, Platform
ethan.park@redwood.com
Attachment: offer-letter-v1.pdf
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Ethan/Laura —

Thanks for the context. I reviewed the infra headcount and spoke briefly with procurement — we have one open FY27 headcount that was earmarked for a later quarter but can be moved up with approval. I approve a provisional geo uplift of +10% and a discretionary sign-on of up to $15,000 for this candidate given the competing offer, provided Finance signs off on the one-time payment.

Next steps (timeline):
1) Finance review (I'll loop in Monica) — Monica, can you confirm by EOD today whether the $15k sign-on fits the FY27 comp budget?
2) Legal — expedite final offer letter; Laura please use the attached updated offer (offer-letter-v2.pdf) which includes the uplift language and the contingent clause.
3) Send candidate a sign-in-principle note immediately to hold him while we finish DocuSign. If DocuSign is live within 24 hours we meet the 48h window.

Procurement note: Jira PROC-4521 contains the expedited approval docs (link above).

Karthik
VP, Engineering
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
Attachment: offer-letter-v2.pdf

On Sun, May 9, 2027 at 09:12, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick heads-up on Alex Drummond (Senior Infra). He surfaced a competing offer from BigCloud this morning — base is ~10% higher for EU location and they included an aggressive sign-on. Alex says he prefers Redwood but needs clarity on two things before he can hold:
>
> - Whether we can apply a geo-based uplift for EU hires (his ask: +10% to base)
> - Whether we can confirm an expedited sign-by (he's asking for a firm window: 48 hours from now)
>
> I've attached a small benchmark table (comp-benchmark-may2027.xlsx) showing market comps for EU-based infra hires. Candidate is sensitive to timing; wants to close quickly.
>
> Can we get a quick read on headcount availability and whether we can move approval fast enough to meet his window? If we need a short-term bridge (sign-in-principle + final docs later) that's also an option.
>
> Thanks,
> Laura
From: Alex Drummond <alex.drummond@gmail.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 14:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Ethan / Laura —

Thanks for the quick coordination and for pushing this through. I appreciate the provisional offer language you shared. I'm very interested in joining Redwood — the team and roadmap are a strong fit.

I have one request: BigCloud has given me a hard deadline tomorrow EOD to accept. If Redwood can provide a sign-in-principle email with the uplift and sign-on confirmed (even if the final DocuSign follows), I can hold off on accepting BigCloud until midnight tomorrow (48 hours from their offer). If we cannot provide that, I will have to accept their offer.

I can also sign an intent-to-join note if that helps — happy to provide anything to make the short timeline feel secure.

Best,
Alex Drummond
+44 7700 900123

On Sun, May 9, 2027 at 11:32, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Hi team,

Quick heads-up on Alex Drummond (Senior Infra). He surfaced a competing offer from BigCloud this morning — base is ~10% higher for EU location and they included an aggressive sign-on. Alex says he prefers Redwood but needs clarity on two things before he can hold:

- Whether we can apply a geo-based uplift for EU hires (his ask: +10% to base)
- Whether we can confirm an expedited sign-by (he's asking for a firm window: 48 hours from now)

I've attached a small benchmark table (comp-benchmark-may2027.xlsx) showing market comps for EU-based infra hires. Candidate is sensitive to timing; wants to close quickly.

Can we get a quick read on headcount availability and whether we can move approval fast enough to meet his window? If we need a short-term bridge (sign-in-principle + final docs later) that's also an option.

Thanks,

--
Laura Bennett
Technical Recruiter, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
Attachment: comp-benchmark-may2027.xlsx


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Laura — thanks for the heads-up.

My read: Alex is strong on the systems side and will materially improve time-to-market for some infra projects this quarter. I think we can consider a location uplift for EU hires; operationally we need a quick confirmation from headcount/procurement and finance on whether the budget supports a 10% uplift plus a modest sign-on.

Proposed short plan:
- I can offer a provisional package: base +10% (geo uplift) + $12,000 sign-on, contingent on fast pathway sign-off.
- Karthik: can you confirm whether there is spare headcount in the FY27 infra bucket and whether procurement can fast-track PROC-4521? If procurement can sign off within 24h we should be able to meet the 48h ask.
- If procurement needs more time, we can send a "sign-in-principle" email to Alex to hold him for 72h while we finish docs.

Attached: draft offer (offer-letter-v1.pdf) — it's the standard SR Infra template with the uplift and sign-on placeholders populated. If legal/finance need changes, we can iterate quickly.

I'll stand by for Karthik's confirmation and can hop on a 15-min call if helpful.

Ethan
Ethan Park
Hiring Manager, Platform
ethan.park@redwood.com
Attachment: offer-letter-v1.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 May 2027 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Ethan/Laura —

Thanks for the context. I reviewed the infra headcount and spoke briefly with procurement — we have one open FY27 headcount that was earmarked for a later quarter but can be moved up with approval. I approve a provisional geo uplift of +10% and a discretionary sign-on of up to $15,000 for this candidate given the competing offer, provided Finance signs off on the one-time payment.

Next steps (timeline):
1) Finance review (I'll loop in Monica) — Monica, can you confirm by EOD today whether the $15k sign-on fits the FY27 comp budget?
2) Legal — expedite final offer letter; Laura please use the attached updated offer (offer-letter-v2.pdf) which includes the uplift language and the contingent clause.
3) Send candidate a sign-in-principle note immediately to hold him while we finish DocuSign. If DocuSign is live within 24 hours we meet the 48h window.

Procurement note: Jira PROC-4521 contains the expedited approval docs (link above).

Karthik
VP, Engineering
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
Attachment: offer-letter-v2.pdf

> Quick heads-up on Alex Drummond (Senior Infra). He surfaced a competing offer from BigCloud this morning — base is ~10% higher for EU location and they included an aggressive sign-on. Alex says he prefers Redwood but needs clarity on two things before he can hold:

> - Whether we can apply a geo-based uplift for EU hires (his ask: +10% to base)
> - Whether we can confirm an expedited sign-by (he's asking for a firm window: 48 hours from now)

> Thanks,
> Laura

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 14:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Candidate update — Alex Drummond (Senior Infra)

Ethan / Laura —

Thanks for the quick coordination and for pushing this through. I appreciate the provisional offer language you shared. I'm very interested in joining Redwood — the team and roadmap are a strong fit.

I have one request: BigCloud has given me a hard deadline tomorrow EOD to accept. If Redwood can provide a sign-in-principle email with the uplift and sign-on confirmed (even if the final DocuSign follows), I can hold off on accepting BigCloud until midnight tomorrow (48 hours from their offer). If we cannot provide that, I will have to accept their offer.

I can also sign an intent-to-join note if that helps — happy to provide anything to make the short timeline feel secure.

Best,
Alex Drummond
+44 7700 900123

source.subject: Panel Relay: Emergency Interviewer Reassignment & Load Smoothing
messages:
From: BrightHire Scheduling <scheduling@brighthire.io>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Candidate: Riley Park - Software Engineer Onsite (Panel needs)

Hi team —

We have an onsite loop for Riley Park (SE II) scheduled for next Wednesday 5/12. Hiring manager requested a 4-person panel: System Design, Product Fit, Coding, and Culture debrief. Riley is flying in and availability window is 10:30–15:00 PT.

Can you confirm coverage for the 11:00 slot (coding) and the 13:00 system-design slot? If someone needs to swap, please propose an alternate who can cover the slot.

Candidate details attached.

[Attachment: Riley_Park_Resume.pdf (pdf)]

Thanks,
Jules from BrightHire
BrightHire Scheduling
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2027 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Candidate: Riley Park - Software Engineer Onsite (Panel needs)

Laura / Soojin —

Thanks — quick note: our interview pool is light that day because a few folks are out for conferences. I can take the coding slot at 11:00 if needed, but I'd prefer to keep it to another engineer with stronger backend experience. Two asks:

- Laura, can you confirm whether Marcus Lin is available to take an 11:00 coding slot? He handled the last coding loop well and has the right rubric.
- Soojin, would you be OK with taking the 13:00 system-design slot? If you can, Marcus can be floated into a product-fit or culture role later.

If either of you are blocked, propose an internal backfill (Marcus is my first choice). I'll build a short runbook once we lock people in.

Thanks — short turnaround on this one.

Ethan
Ethan Park
Recruiting Ops | Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2027 10:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Candidate: Riley Park - Software Engineer Onsite (Panel needs)

Ethan —

11:00 coding: I just checked Marcus's calendar; he has a 10:00 conflict but is free 10:45–12:30 if the interview can start at 10:50 instead of 11:00. He can also do a 60-minute coding session (we typically do 45).

13:00 system-design: I'm overloaded that afternoon and won't be able to swap. Soojin, feel free to take it if that works.

Attaching a quick panel role matrix with suggested rubrics and common probe questions (Marcus + Soojin suggested roles).

[Attachment: panel_role_matrix.xlsx (xlsx)]

Laura Bennett
Senior Recruiting Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Candidate: Riley Park - Software Engineer Onsite (Panel needs)

I can take the 13:00 system-design slot. Quick logistics note: I prefer to do design loops with the whiteboard room booked and a 10 min buffer for candidate check-in. If Marcus can cover coding at 10:50, we should be fine.

One request — Marcus, if you take coding, could you also jot two quick bullet points in the runbook about the starter problem we used last month? That saved the panel time and gave consistent scoring.

If anyone prefers I can swap system-design for product-fit, but 13:00 system-design works best for me.

Soojin Lee
Hiring Partner – Backend Platform
Redwood Inference
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 May 2027 13:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Final: 5/12 Riley Park onsite — assignments + runbook link

Thanks all — final plan below. I've updated the calendar invites and dropped a short runbook in Drive. Please review the runbook before the interview and add the two bullet points if you're leading the coding/problem session.

Plan (5/12 onsite, candidate window 10:30–15:00 PT):
- 10:50–11:35 — Coding — Marcus Lin (covering backend rubric, 45m + 10m buffer)
- 12:15–13:00 — Product Fit — Laura Bennett (30m + buffer)
- 13:00–13:45 — System Design — Soojin Lee (whiteboard room reserved)
- 14:15–14:45 — Culture / Debrief — Ethan Park (30m, wrap & logistics)

Notes / runbook links:
- Runbook: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-interviews-runbooks
- HubSpot candidate record: HS-43217

Attachments: interview-runbook.md (short checklist) attached for offline review.

If any emergencies pop up, we agreed Marcus will be the float fallback for the whole loop. Appreciate the quick turn.

Ethan Park
Recruiting Ops | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: interview-runbook.md (text)]

source.subject: Invoice sweep window exception — escrow entity split & VAT routing
messages:
From: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 09:12:00 +0100
Subject: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — quick follow up from our finance team. We signed the annual commitment last quarter but are implementing an internal escrow split across our UK and NL entities. Before we finalize the PO and wiring, can you confirm:  

- How Redwood intends to schedule the committed-spend sweep windows (monthly / quarterly), and whether we can request non-standard timing for the first two tranches.
- Whether Redwood can issue split invoices to two legal entities (Bayside UK Ltd and Bayside NL BV) for the same commitment, and how VAT will be applied on those invoices.

Our treasury team also asks for a sample invoice and the exact bank details you will use for a split wiring. I’ve attached our internal ledger notes (no PII) for context.

Appreciate a short timeline — we need this confirmed to pull the PO through our S2P system by next Wednesday.

Thanks,
Marisol
Marisol Vega
Head of Finance | Bayside Analytics
marisol.vega@baysideanalytics.com

Attachment: bayside-ledger-notes.csv (CSV stub)

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 10:02:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split

Hi Marisol — thanks for the clear ask. Short answers and a suggested plan below; I’ll loop in billing/tax for the final confirmation.

Proposed plan (can do non-standard timing for first two tranches):
- Sweep cadence: Default monthly sweep on the 1st unless you opt for quarterly. For this customer we can schedule the first two tranches on 2027-05-15 and 2027-06-15 to align with your payroll.
- Split invoices: We support issuing invoices to multiple legal entities associated with a single commitment, but we must map the committed amount to entity-level ledger codes up front.
- VAT treatment: If the services are taxed in the buyer country, we’ll apply VAT to the invoice issued by the corresponding Redwood legal entity. If you want reverse-charge handling (B2B EU), we need each entity’s VAT registration numbers and a signed VAT attestation.

What I’m attaching: a proposed invoice-schedule CSV (shows entity split and dates) and a draft sample invoice for review.

Next steps I need from you by Tue EOD:  
1) Confirm which two legal entities will receive invoices (exact legal names + VAT IDs).
2) Confirm preferred sweep dates for the first two tranches (default above unless you tell us otherwise).
3) PO number and beneficiary details if the wiring will be split.

I’ll open an internal ticket with Billing for bank details and the final invoice routing.

Cheers,
Ethan Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.com
Attachment: proposed-invoice-schedule.csv; invoice-draft-Bayside-RWD-893.pdf

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 09:05:00 +0100
Subject: Fwd: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split — need billing/tax confirm

Kimberly / Priya — forwarding Bayside’s note and our proposed schedule. They want: split invoices to two legal entities (UK + NL), non-standard first two sweep dates (15 May and 15 June), and sample invoice + bank details.

Key questions:  
- Can Redwood invoice two entities for the same annual commitment while preserving a single committed balance and drawdown tracking? If so, how do we reflect that in the ledger (one commit id, two invoice refs)?
- VAT matrix: for UK-issued invoices do we charge UK VAT, and for NL invoices do we apply Dutch VAT or reverse-charge? See attached customer VAT IDs (from their reply).
- Any constraints on split wiring (partial wires to different Redwood bank accounts) — treasury needs the final remit instructions.

I attached: BAY_LEDGER_NOTES.csv (from customer), proposed-invoice-schedule.csv, draft invoice. Can you confirm by EOD Tuesday so I can reply to Marisol and keep their PO timeline?

—Ethan

---- Original Message ----
From: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 09:12:00 +0100
Subject: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>

(quoted above)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 11:18:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Fwd: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split — need billing/tax confirm

Thanks — reviewed. High level notes and operational asks:  

1) Committed balance / ledger: We can keep a single commitment id (RWD-2024-893) and issue multiple invoice documents against that commit. Each invoice will reference the commit id and have its own invoice number. Accounting will net the receipts against the commit balance. Please ensure the invoice schedule CSV has an explicit column for commit id and entity invoice_ref.

2) VAT routing rules (per our tax counsel):
- If Redwood issues an invoice from Redwood EU Ltd (NL) to Bayside NL BV and the buyer is VAT-registered, we apply zero-rated B2B reverse-charge if the service qualifies — buyer must provide VAT number and sign the attestation.
- UK invoices: post-Brexit, services invoiced from our UK legal entity will be subject to UK VAT where place-of-supply rules indicate. If Bayside UK is VAT-registered, we can zero-rate with reverse-charge but we need their VAT certificate.

3) Split wiring / bank accounts: We can accept split wires, but each invoice must include the precise remit instructions and bank account. Treasury prefers a single collection then internal allocation; if Bayside insists on two separate wires we will provide two account refs.

source.subject: Clarify data erasure schedule for logs, backups, and prompt cache
source.thread_text:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@medisys.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Medisys DPO <dpo@medisys.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Question on erasure windows for logs/backups/prompt data

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks for taking this. As part of our SIG review we need to confirm a few specifics about Redwood's deletion practices so we can sign off on the DPA. High level asks: can you confirm the following for Redwood-hosted deployments?

1) Application logs (API access logs, error traces): what is the default retention and what are the available retention windows we can contract?
2) Backups: how long are backups retained, do they include prompt text or user-submitted payloads, and how quickly can a backup be excluded/deleted on a per-customer basis?
3) Prompt/interaction retention: do prompts/query text or transient KV caches persist beyond the active session? If so, what are purge timelines and are there controls to disable prompt storage entirely?
4) Deletion evidence: what artifacts do you provide to prove deletion (audit logs, job ids, timestamps)?

Our security team is especially sensitive to backups containing PHI. Appreciate a concise breakdown and any SLA we can put in the DPA.

Best,
Lena Ortiz
Lead Security Analyst, MediSys Health

---
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@medisys.com>
Cc: Medisys DPO <dpo@medisys.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 11:05:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Question on erasure windows for logs/backups/prompt data

Lena — thanks for the clear list. I pulled in our infra and legal leads (Vivek and Marissa) and summarized answers below. Please let me know if you need this in a formal exhibit for the DPA.

Summary answers (short):
- Application logs: default 90 days; contractual options 30/90/365 days. Logs are retained in customer-tagged buckets.
- Backups: weekly snapshots retained for 90 days by default; longer retention (annual snapshot retention up to 7 years) available for enterprise plans. Backups do not intentionally redact prompt text — they are snapshot-level copies of model state and persisted metadata. We offer an opt-in backup-exclusion flag to omit prompt history from snapshots for new customers; for existing backups we can run targeted purge jobs.
- Prompt/interaction retention: by default, prompts and generated tokens are retained for 30 days to support debugging and quality monitoring; KV cache is ephemeral and only held in memory for the lifetime of the model instance (no disks) unless a customer opts into persistent conversation history.
- Deletion evidence: we provide audit records (job id, start/end timestamps, S3 object keys removed) and a signed deletion certificate for enterprise customers.

I'll paste the more detailed infra notes below.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Enterprise Accounts — Redwood Inference

On Mon, May 3, 2027 at 11:18 AM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Kim — expanding a bit with infra detail for each of Lena's bullets.
>
> 1) Logs:
>    • Default retention: 90d; config via Console or API (policy: 30/90/365).
>    • Storage: logs are stored in per-tenant S3 prefixes audited by object lifecycle policies.
>    • Erasure: lifecycle job removes object versions; we publish job ids and retention policy applied.
>
> 2) Backups:
>    • Snapshot cadence: daily incremental, weekly full snapshot.
>    • Default retention: 90d for incremental chains; weekly fulls kept 90d. Enterprise option: archive fulls to cold storage for up to 7y.
>    • Contents: backups capture model weights, metadata, service-level metadata (request ids, timestamps). By default they do capture persisted prompt history when "persistent_conversation" is enabled for an account.
>    • Per-customer deletion: we can exclude future snapshots via account flag; for existing snapshots we run a controlled purge that removes objects and emits an audit bundle (job id + object list). Turnaround: 3–10 business days depending on snapshot size.
>
> 3) Prompt retention & KV cache:
>    • KV cache: in-memory only, flushed on process restart; not included in backups unless a customer explicitly writes conversation history to persistent storage.
>    • Prompts / conversation history: default 30d; can be configured to 0d (no storage) for new accounts via API or Console toggle.
>    • Purge operations: immediate for active conversation objects (delete API returns job id); aggregated indices and derived analytics may take up to 48 hours to reconcile.
>
> 4) Deletion evidence:
>    • Delivered artifacts: deletion job id, timestamp, list of S3 keys removed, signed manifest, and audit log entries. For SOC/SIG buyers we can provide a redacted audit bundle in advance.
>    • Compliance: SOC 2 controls map and internal runbook attached (linkshared).
>
> Note on PHI: If PHI is present, we recommend the customer enable backup-exclusion and set prompt retention to 0d; we can additionally offer an encrypt-at-rest with customer-managed keys and a short retention SLA in the DPA.
>
> Vivek
> Principal, Infrastructure

---
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@medisys.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Medisys DPO <dpo@medisys.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 13:02:47 -0700
Subject: Re: Question on erasure windows for logs/backups/prompt data

Thanks for the quick summary. A couple of clarifying follow-ups:

- For existing backups that include prompt text, is the 3–10 business day purge timeline guaranteed, or does it depend on size and queueing? We need a worst-case SLA for our procurement team.
- For deletion evidence, can you provide a sample signed deletion certificate and an example of the audit manifest fields (we're comfortable with redacted keys).

If you can get those two items into a short exhibit we can attach to the DPA and move forward.

Best,
Lena

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena@medisys.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Question on erasure windows for logs/backups/prompt data

Lena — looping in ops (Ethan) and attaching our retention matrix and a redacted sample deletion certificate.

Regarding the purge SLA for existing backups: we operate a prioritized purge queue. For enterprise DPA commitments we can contract a 10-business-day maximum for targeted backup purges that remove customer-contained prompt text (this will be added as an exhibit). The 3–10 window Vivek mentioned is typical; we guarantee 10 business days in the contract.

Attached: Retention_RetentionMatrix_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet)
Attached: Backup_Retention_Notes.pdf (redacted deletion certificate sample)

If that meets your procurement needs we'll include the exhibit language and a DocuSign link.

—Marissa
Head of Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@medisys.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Medisys DPO <dpo@medisys.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Question on erasure windows for logs/backups/prompt data

Marissa, Kimberly — that works for us. Please send the exhibit text and the sample certificate; we'll route to procurement and legal. Also confirming MediSys will enable backup-exclusion and set prompt retention to 0d for accounts storing PHI.

Thanks,
Lena
Medisys Health

source.subject: Mutual action plan & environment prep for NovaMed eval
messages:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.inference>
To: Claire Hammond <claire.hammond@novamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mutual action plan & environment prep for NovaMed eval

Claire —

Thanks again for the time today. Per our conversation I pulled together the initial mutual action plan for the 4-week evaluation and a short checklist we need from your side to get the POC environment ready. High-level asks:

- Org setup: a non-prod project/org where we can create a Redwood service account and run a small dedicated cluster (we only need IAM privileges for the service account; we will not modify your production resources).
- API keys: we will create scoped API keys for the evaluation. Please confirm an owner email for token rotation notifications.
- IP allowlist: list of static egress IPs your team will open (we can provide these once peering is ready).
- VPC peering: preferred cloud (GCP or Azure), VPC/Project IDs, and a contact for peering (network ops).

Proposed timeline (open to adjustments):
- Week 0 (this week): confirm org/project, network contact, and sign MAA/SOW stub
- Week 1: service account & API key provisioning; initial IP allowlist test
- Week 2: VPC peering + routing tests
- Week 3–4: workload run, perf checks, cost estimates, and handoff notes

I attached a one-page MAP and a small network template you can share with your networking team. Can you confirm the network contact and whether you prefer GCP or Azure for peering? Also, please share the CIDR blocks we should expect.

Best —
Jasmine Liu
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jasmine.liu@redwood.inference]
[Attachment: MAP_NovaMed_v1.pdf (application/pdf)]
[Attachment: redwood-network-template.yaml (text/yaml)]

From: Claire Hammond <claire.hammond@novamed.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.inference>
Cc: ops@novamed.com
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 12:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mutual action plan & environment prep for NovaMed eval

Jasmine —

Thanks — this looks good and aligns with our expectations. A couple items from our side:

- We prefer GCP for the peering.
- Our network CIDRs for the eval project are 10.48.0.0/16 (dev-eval) and 10.49.0.0/24 (services).
- Network contact: Marco Diaz (marco.diaz@novamed.com, +1 415-555-0182). He will need the peering config and any IPs to allow.
- For service accounts, please provide the email you will use for the connector so our security team can pre-approve it.
- We will also need a short security questionnaire (SOC2 scope fields) and a redline-friendly SOW/MAA for our legal team.

I attached our preliminary network diagram and I'll loop Marco separately when you have the peering details.

Claire Hammond
Director, Platform Engineering | NovaMed
[Attachment: novamed_network_draft.vsdx (application/vnd.ms-visio)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: NovaMed MAP — task split

Looping in internal owners so we can move fast:

- Ethan Park will own VPC peering on our side and provide the peering request template (target: 2027-05-01 for peering completion).
- I will provision the scoped evaluation API keys once Jasmine confirms the owner email (target: 2027-04-29).
- Marissa — can you schedule a 30-min kickoff with Claire and Marco this Thursday to walk the network template and Gov/Compliance asks?
- Jasmine — please push the MAA/SOW stub to HubSpot so legal can start review.

Keep notes in the HubSpot deal (DEAL-4582). If we hit any blockers on peering, we'll fall back to IP-allowlist-only for the initial tests.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.inference>
To: Claire Hammond <claire.hammond@novamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 02 May 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mutual action plan & environment prep for NovaMed eval

Claire —

Great — thanks for the quick turnaround and Marco's contact. Confirming next steps and the concrete values your network team will need from us:

1) Service account (for peering and connector): redwood-connector@redwood-inference.iam.gserviceaccount.com
2) Static egress IPs to allow (for initial IP-allowlist fallback): 34.86.12.45, 34.86.12.46, 34.86.12.47
3) Peering details we need from you: project-id (the eval project), VPC network name, and the route priority you want us to influence.
4) Target dates:
   - API key provisioning: 2027-04-29 (complete)
   - VPC peering request submitted by us: 2027-04-28
   - Expected peering acceptance by NovaMed (target): 2027-05-01

Attached is an updated MAP (v1) with those target dates plus our redwood-network-template.yaml for Marco to import into his ticket. I also added a short SOC2 scope guide for your security team at the bottom of the MAP.

If Marco prefers, Ethan can join the kickoff to walk through the peering flow and routes. Let me know a time this week and I'll send a calendar invite.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this live.

Jasmine Liu
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jasmine.liu@redwood.inference]
[Attachment: MAP_NovaMed_v1.pdf (application/pdf)]
[Attachment: redwood-network-template.yaml (text/yaml)]

source.subject: Renewal negotiation: Ardent Labs — commit, credits & invoice terms
messages:
From: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion — Ardent Labs renewal + extra capacity

Hi Ethan,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We’re lining up the renewal for our ML inference usage (current term ends July 31) and we’re looking at two things at once: a) an uplift in committed capacity to support our upcoming feature rollouts, and b) tighter invoice terms on a couple of one-off credits we used during our pilot.

Quick background: Q1 usage spiked ~2.4x vs our committed baseline. We’d like to move to a 12-month commit with a staged uplift (month 1–3: +20%, month 4–12: +50%) and we want to lock price through the end of the year if possible. Procurement will also ask for a 30-day invoice deferral on the first renewal invoice because of their internal timing.

Could you send a short written proposal — pricing delta for the commit tiers, how credits would be applied, and whether Redwood can support a temporary price-hold (60–90 days)? I attached our Q1 usage export so you have the exact numbers.

Thanks,
Clare Hunt
Head of Cloud Engineering
Ardent Labs

Attachment: Ardent_usage_Q1_2027.csv (csv, 68KB)

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Ardent Labs renewal + extra capacity

Hi Clare — thanks for the clear ask and for the usage file. Quick summary up front: we can support a 12-month commit with the staged uplift you outlined, offer a temporary price-hold window, and accommodate invoice deferral with a small one-time processing fee. Details below and I’ve attached a draft SOW you can share with procurement.

Proposal (high level):
- Commit: 12 months; baseline = your current commit. Uplift schedule: months 1–3 +20% (soft guarantee), months 4–12 +50% (contracted commit).
- Pricing: for the uplifted commit we can offer a blended rate that is ~10% below on-demand for the additional units (applies only to committed tokens/embeds).
- Price-hold: 90-day price-hold on the new blended rate starting from the renewal effective date. After 90 days we’ll re-evaluate based on model/infra cost indexes; we’ll provide 30 days’ notice if rates change.
- Credits: we’ll apply a one-time credit equal to 1 month of baseline committed spend to offset pilot overages.
- Invoice terms: we can accept a 30-day deferral on the first renewal invoice. There will be a single one-time administrative fee of $4,000 to cover extended payment processing; alternatively, we can reduce discount by 1% to waive the fee — your choice.
- Compliance: we can provide SOC 2 attestation and logs export for procurement; Ben can coordinate the items they’ll request.

I attached: Ardent_SOW_Renewal_draft_v1.pdf (SOW draft).

Next steps:
1) Tell me whether procurement takes the $4k admin fee or the 1% discount reduction.
2) If OK, I’ll circulate a marked SOW and a DocuSign offer link for signature, and finance will confirm the invoice deferral window.

—Ethan
Ethan Park
Renewals Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Ardent_SOW_Renewal_draft_v1.pdf (pdf, 240KB)

From: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@ardentlabs.com
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 16:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Ardent Labs renewal + extra capacity

Ethan,

Thanks — that looks promising. A couple of clarifications from procurement:
- The 30-day invoice deferral is a hard ask for the first invoice (our fiscal calendar), we prefer not to accept a discount reduction.
- Procurement will also want an explicit commitment on how usage overages are billed during the staged uplift (do you bill on true usage if they exceed the month-by-month uplift?).
- For credits: we appreciate the one-month baseline credit. Can that be split across Q3 invoices rather than one lump sum?

Procurement: please confirm who at Redwood will receive the formal PO once internal approvals are complete.

—Clare
Clare Hunt
Head of Cloud Engineering, Ardent Labs

On Wed, Apr 28, 2027 at 09:12 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Clare — thanks for the clear ask and for the usage file. Quick summary up front: we can support a 12-month commit with the staged uplift you outlined, offer a temporary price-hold window, and accommodate invoice deferral with a small one-time processing fee. Details below and I’ve attached a draft SOW you can share with procurement.
>
> (see attached)

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 10:00:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Renewal discussion — Ardent Labs renewal + extra capacity

Team — forwarding Clare’s procurement reply. Need quick alignment before I send the final offer:

Key asks from Ardent: 30-day deferral (first invoice); staged uplift billing clarity; split credit over Q3.

My proposed counter (to present tonight):
- Keep the 12-month staged commit as proposed.
- Pricing: hold blended uplift rate at 10% off on-demand for 90 days.
- Overage handling: during months 1–3 we treat the uplift band (+20%) as a soft guarantee — if their usage exceeds the uplift in a given month we bill true usage at on-demand for the overage. Months 4–12 overages billed against committed excess (i.e., no punitive uplift).
- Credits: split the one-month baseline credit over three invoices (Q3 months) as a line-item credit.
- Invoice deferral: accept the 30-day deferral for the first invoice but waive the $4k admin fee only if finance signs off to treat it as a one-time exception; otherwise apply the 1% discount reduction.

Lauren/Ben — can finance approve waiving the $4k as a one-time exception if we lock the 12-month commit at the proposed uplift? This helps avoid impacting the customer relationship.

Marissa — if this looks OK I’ll send Clare the counter and include a DocuSign link; let me know if you want to handle the contract handoff.

Thanks,
Ethan

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 16:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Ardent Labs renewal + extra capacity
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@ardentlabs.com

> Ethan,
>
> Thanks — that looks promising. A couple of clarifications from procurement: ...

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>, procurement@ardentlabs.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Final renewal offer: Ardent Labs (ARD-2027-07) — terms & next steps

Hi Clare / Ardent procurement —

Appreciate the quick turnaround. Per our internal alignment, here is the final offer we can sign to today:

1) Term & Commit
- 12-month term starting 2027-08-01. Baseline = current monthly commit. Staged uplift: months 1–3 +20% (soft guarantee), months 4–12 +50% (contracted).

2) Pricing & Price-hold
- Blended uplift rate for the additional committed units = 10% below on-demand. Redwood will honor a 90-day price-hold on the blended rate from the effective date; Redwood will provide 30 days’ notice before any rate adjustment after that window.

3) Overage billing
- Months 1–3 (staging): if actual usage exceeds that month’s uplift, overages billed at on-demand rates for the excess.
- Months 4–12: overages billed against committed excess at the blended uplift rate (no punitive multiplier).

4) Credits
- One-month baseline committed spend credited and split evenly across the first three invoices after renewal (appears as a line-item credit).

5) Invoice terms
- Redwood accepts Ardent’s request for a 30-day deferral on the first renewal invoice. Finance has approved waiving the $4,000 admin fee as a one-time exception conditional on signed agreement by 2027-05-07.

6) Compliance items
- SOC 2 attestation and a logs export bundle will be provided to procurement once PO is received. Ben will be the Redwood contact for evidence requests (ben.carter@redwood.ai).

Next steps:
- If Ardent accepts, we’ll send a DocuSign package (SOW + order form) to procurement and Marissa will coordinate handoff to on-boarding. Link: https://app.docusign.com/prepare/ARD-2027-07
- Please confirm acceptance by 2027-05-07 to lock the waived fee.

Thanks again — I’ll escalate internally if you need an earlier effective date.

—Ethan Park
Renewals Lead, Redwood Inference
Contact: ethan.park@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments referenced: ARD-docsign-offer-link.txt (contains the prepared DocuSign package id), Ardent_SOW_Renewal_draft_v1.pdf, Ardent_usage_Q1_2027.csv

- Price-hold: 90-day price-hold on the new blended rate starting from the renewal effective date. After 90 days we’ll re-evaluate based on model/infra cost indexes; we’ll provide 30 days’ notice if rates change.
- Credits: we’ll apply a one-time credit equal to 1 month of baseline committed spend to offset pilot overages.
- Invoice terms: we can accept a 30-day deferral on the first renewal invoice. There will be a single one-time administrative fee of $4,000 to cover extended payment processing; alternatively, we can reduce discount by 1% to waive the fee — your choice.
- Compliance: we can provide SOC 2 attestation and logs export for procurement; Ben can coordinate the items they’ll request.

I attached: Ardent_SOW_Renewal_draft_v1.pdf (SOW draft).

Next steps:
1) Tell me whether procurement takes the $4k admin fee or the 1% discount reduction.
2) If OK, I’ll circulate a marked SOW and a DocuSign offer link for signature, and finance will confirm the invoice deferral window.

—Ethan
Ethan Park

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Clare Hunt <clare.hunt@ardentlabs.com>, procurement@ardentlabs.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Final renewal offer: Ardent Labs (ARD-2027-07) — terms & next steps

Hi Clare / Ardent procurement —

Appreciate the quick turnaround. Per our internal alignment, here is the final offer we can sign to today:

1) Term & Commit
- 12-month term starting 2027-08-01. Baseline = current monthly commit. Staged uplift: months 1–3 +20% (soft guarantee), months 4–12 +50% (contracted).

2) Pricing & Price-hold
- Blended uplift rate for the additional committed units = 10% below on-demand. Redwood will honor a 90-day price-hold on the blended rate from the effective date; Redwood will provide 30 days’ notice before any rate adjustment after that window.

3) Overage billing
- Months 1–3 (staging): if actual usage exceeds that month’s uplift, overages billed at on-demand rates for the excess.
- Months 4–12: overages billed against committed excess at the blended uplift rate (no punitive multiplier).

4) Credits
- One-month baseline committed spend credited and split evenly across the first three invoices after renewal (appears as a line-item credit).

5) Invoice terms
- Redwood accepts Ardent’s request for a 30-day deferral on the first renewal invoice. Finance has approved waiving the $4,000 admin fee as a one-time exception conditional on signed agreement by 2027-05-07.

6) Compliance items
- SOC 2 attestation and a logs export bundle will be provided to procurement once PO is received. Ben will be the Redwood contact for evidence requests (ben.carter@redwood.ai).

Next steps:
- If Ardent accepts, we’ll send a DocuSign package (SOW + order form) to procurement and Marissa will coordinate handoff to on-boarding. Link: https://app.docusign.com/prepare/ARD-2027-07
- Please confirm acceptance by 2027-05-07 to lock the waived fee.

Thanks again — I’ll escalate internally if you need an earlier effective date.

—Ethan Park
Renewals Lead, Redwood Inference
Contact: ethan.park@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments referenced: ARD-docsign-offer-link.txt (contains the prepared DocuSign package id), Ardent_SOW_Renewal_draft_v1.pdf, Ardent_usage_Q1_2027.csv

source.subject: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks
messages:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Andi Moreno <andi@atlasfintech.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Team — kicking off a short thread to align before Wednesday's QBR with Atlas FinTech. High level: Atlas will want concrete runway options and the unit-econ story for the next two quarters (GPU spend, utilization targets, and what software levers can buy us).

Please review the attached runway model (qbr_capacity_model.xlsx) and the short notes on batching/KV-cache experiments (batching_kvcache_notes.pdf). I need the following by EOD Tuesday so Lauren and I can pull the leadership deck: 

- A delta table showing 3 runway scenarios (Conservative / Balanced / Aggressive) with GPU spend, expected avg utilization, and monthly token throughput.
- A one-paragraph line for each scenario on how batching and KV cache contribute (e.g., 30% lower per-token cost via 2x batching and 60% KV hit).
- Any immediate procurement asks (reserve nodes / committed capacity) and a quick cost/benefit note.

Andi — including you here so you're aware of the ask and the timelines. We may share a slimmed-down two-slide summary during the QBR.

Attachments: qbr_capacity_model.xlsx, batching_kvcache_notes.pdf

Thanks,
Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Director, Infrastructure Economics
Redwood Inference
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Kimberly — thanks for the clear asks. Quick clarifications before we lock numbers: 

- For utilization, are we reporting average GPU occupancy (payload + kernels) or effective token-processing utilization? I'd prefer the latter for the QBR (it maps to per-token cost).
- For scenarios, include a short risk line: (1) supply risk if spot pools shrink, (2) degradation risk if KV hit falls below 40%.

I'll take the runway scenario templates and insert the leadership-friendly slides. Expect my draft slide + a 3-line exec summary by 10:30 Tuesday.

One ask to Karthik: in the Balanced scenario, can you give a concrete example of the batching profile (batch size distribution, median latency impact) and the KV-hit assumptions you used? I'll include that in the footnote.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
VP, Product & Operations
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Apr 26, 2027 at 09:15 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking off a short thread to align before Wednesday's QBR with Atlas FinTech...
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 14:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Folks — taking the Balanced scenario and adding numbers. Key assumptions up front: baseline (today) per-token infra cost = $0.0009 (hosted mix), current avg GPU occupancy = 62%, current KV-hit = 38%.

Balanced scenario (30-day avg):
- GPU spend: $560k / month
- Effective utilization (token-processing): 72%
- Monthly token throughput: 14.5B tokens
- Software levers: 1.4x average batching (median batch size -> 8 from 6), KV-hit -> 52% via small prefix caching, 10% of requests served entirely from cache (prefix short-circuit).
- Net per-token infra cost reduction vs baseline: ~26% (driven 16% from batching, 10% from KV-cache).

Conservative scenario (supply risk): higher spot volatility, assume only 1.1x batching and 45% KV-hit -> GPU spend $640k, utilization 66%, tokens 12.5B. Aggressive scenario: 2x batching, 65% KV-hit -> GPU spend $420k, utilization 78%, tokens 18B.

Attached heatmap (kv-hit-heatmap.png) shows sensitivity of unit-cost to KV-hit vs batch multiplier. I'll drop a short table into Kimberly's workbook with the three scenarios and the 6-line risk annotations.

Attachment: kv-hit-heatmap.png

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Head of Serving Orchestration
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Apr 26, 2027 at 11:02 AM Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com> wrote:
> Kimberly — thanks for the clear asks...
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Thank you — this is the level of detail I want for the leadership deck. A couple of operational asks and one leadership decision: 

Operational asks (for the sprint):
- Karthik: schedule a 48h canary for the Balanced config on a dedicated slot pool (target start Wednesday night). Capture tail-latency and error-rate delta vs baseline.
- Ops: prepare a short procurement note for two options: A) 3-month reserved nodes for critical pools (fast to provision) B) 12-month committed capacity discount (requires finance sign-off). Include expected $/token delta.

Leadership decision (we need this by Tuesday EOD): choose whether to pursue Option A (short runway insurance) or Option B (discounted commitment) if Atlas commits to expansion. If we pick A, I approve a $150k contingency for reserves; if B, we'll route to finance for signature.

Finally, for external messaging: Kimberly, cc'd Andi, we'll present only the Balanced scenario + the Conservative risk line; keep the aggressive scenario as internal-only.

—Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
VP, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Apr 26, 2027 at 02:28 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> Folks — taking the Balanced scenario and adding numbers...
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Andi Moreno <andi@atlasfintech.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Looping back — can we lock the Balanced slide set and one backup slide (Conservative) by EOD tomorrow? I've scheduled a 30-min sync Tue 09:00 PT to walk through the deck; invite sent.

Andi — we'll only share the Balanced + Conservative summary in the customer-facing slides. Atlas: please forward any additional questions ahead of the QBR and we'll try to incorporate.

Thanks,
Ethan
Ethan Park
Director, Solutions
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 27, 2027 at 09:05 AM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thank you — this is the level of detail I want for the leadership deck...

text:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Andi Moreno <andi@atlasfintech.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Team — kicking off a short thread to align before Wednesday's QBR with Atlas FinTech. High level: Atlas will want concrete runway options and the unit-econ story for the next two quarters (GPU spend, utilization targets, and what software levers can buy us).

Please review the attached runway model (qbr_capacity_model.xlsx) and the short notes on batching/KV-cache experiments (batching_kvcache_notes.pdf). I need the following by EOD Tuesday so Lauren and I can pull the leadership deck: 

- A delta table showing 3 runway scenarios (Conservative / Balanced / Aggressive) with GPU spend, expected avg utilization, and monthly token throughput.
- A one-paragraph line for each scenario on how batching and KV cache contribute (e.g., 30% lower per-token cost via 2x batching and 60% KV hit).
- Any immediate procurement asks (reserve nodes / committed capacity) and a quick cost/benefit note.

Andi — including you here so you're aware of the ask and the timelines. We may share a slimmed-down two-slide summary during the QBR.

Attachments: qbr_capacity_model.xlsx, batching_kvcache_notes.pdf

Thanks,
Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Director, Infrastructure Economics
Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Kimberly — thanks for the clear asks. Quick clarifications before we lock numbers: 

- For utilization, are we reporting average GPU occupancy (payload + kernels) or effective token-processing utilization? I'd prefer the latter for the QBR (it maps to per-token cost).
- For scenarios, include a short risk line: (1) supply risk if spot pools shrink, (2) degradation risk if KV hit falls below 40%.

I'll take the runway scenario templates and insert the leadership-friendly slides. Expect my draft slide + a 3-line exec summary by 10:30 Tuesday.

One ask to Karthik: in the Balanced scenario, can you give a concrete example of the batching profile (batch size distribution, median latency impact) and the KV-hit assumptions you used? I'll include that in the footnote.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
VP, Product & Operations
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Apr 26, 2027 at 09:15 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking off a short thread to align before Wednesday's QBR with Atlas FinTech...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 14:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR: capacity runway & batching/KV-cache impact — leadership asks

Folks — taking the Balanced scenario and adding numbers. Key assumptions up front: baseline (today) per-token infra cost = $0.0009 (hosted mix), current avg GPU occupancy = 62%, current KV-hit = 38%.

Balanced scenario (30-day avg):
- GPU spend: $560k / month
- Effective utilization (token-processing): 72%
- Monthly token throughput: 14.5B tokens
- Software levers: 1.4x average batching (median batch size -> 8 from 6), KV-hit -> 52% via small prefix caching, 10% of requests served entirely from cache (prefix short-circuit).
- Net per-token infra cost reduction vs baseline: ~26% (driven 16% from batching, 10% from KV-cache).

Conservative scenario (supply risk): higher spot volatility, assume only 1.1x batching and 45% KV-hit -> GPU spend $640k, utilization 66%, tokens 12.5B. Aggressive scenario: 2x batching, 65% KV-hit -> GPU spend $420k, utilization 78%, tokens 18B.

Attached heatmap (kv-hit-heatmap.png) shows sensitivity of unit-cost to KV-hit vs batch multiplier. I'll drop a short table into Kimberly's workbook with the three scenarios and the 6-line risk annotations.

Attachment: kv-hit-heatmap.png

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Head of Serving Orchestration
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Redwood x Integral - Technical Onboarding & Interop Gatecheck
messages:
From: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>; Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Hi Rishi / Monica,

Thanks again for the product intro last week — Integral is excited to move toward a scoped technical validation with Redwood. Our engineering lead (Vera) and I sketched a short "gatecheck" plan so we can validate integration surface, performance, and operational fit before committing to a dedicated listing in our marketplace.

High-level asks we have for the gatecheck:
- Reference architecture review focused on model routing and deployment variants (hosted vs. dedicated).
- Smoke tests for end-to-end inference latency at representative payloads.
- Verification of auth flows (API keys + optional SSO) and data residency controls.
- A short runbook for failure/fallback behavior and circuit-breaker semantics.

Attached: integral-integration-surface.pdf, api-capabilities.xlsx (stubs). Can you send a Redwood gatecheck template and suggest target SLOs you expect for this workload?

Ideal timeline: start validation next week (week of Apr 19). We can provide a staging environment and sample payloads.

Thanks,
Lucas Brenner
Head of Platform Partnerships
Integral AI

Attachments: integral-integration-surface.pdf (diagram), api-capabilities.xlsx (sample payloads)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
To: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Lucas —

Appreciate the clear scope. I'm happy to run this validation with you. Quick responses below and a proposed kickoff agenda attached.

What we'll provide (initial):
- Redwood gatecheck template (see attached) with acceptance criteria and test matrix.
- An engineer point-of-contact for the validation (Ethan Park).
- A short reference design doc that maps Integral's endpoints to our routing/fallback options.

Suggested kickoff agenda (30–45 min):
1) Introductions + success criteria (10 min)
2) Walk the integration surface (15 min)
3) Access and test data requirements (10 min)
4) Next steps, timeline, owners (5 min)

To proceed we need: staging endpoint + credentials (or a time-limited test token), sample payloads, and confirmation on which Redwood deployment mode you want us to validate first (hosted API or dedicated pool).

I've attached the gatecheck template (redwood-gatecheck-template.docx). Proposed kickoff: Tue Apr 20 at 10:00 AM PT — does that work for the Integral team?

Best,
Rishi Malhotra
Senior Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-gatecheck-template.docx

From: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vera Chung <vera@integral.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Rishi — thanks, that agenda looks good. Quick answers + access: 

- Staging environment: https://staging.integral.ai/redwood-integration (API keys will be created for "redwood-gatecheck" user).
- Primary contact: Vera Chung (cc'd) — she'll provision keys and a sandbox dataset.
- We want the first validation run against Redwood Hosted API variants (we'll evaluate Dedicated later).

Performance targets (what we expect to validate):
- Functional: end-to-end correctness for 95% of test prompts.
- Latency SLO: p95 < 150ms, p99 < 400ms for small-medium prompts (<= 512 tokens).
- Throughput: sustained 50 concurrent requests at representative payloads for 10 minutes without >1% error rate.

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/INT-4512 (for reference).

We can do Tue Apr 20 10:00 AM PT — I'll have Vera share keys before the call.

Lucas
Head of Platform Partnerships
Integral AI

---
On Sun, Apr 18, 2027 at 11:03 AM Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Lucas —
> 
> Appreciate the clear scope. I'm happy to run this validation with you. Quick responses below and a proposed kickoff agenda attached.
> 
> [see attached]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.ai>
To: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>; Vera Chung <vera@integral.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>; Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Hi all — thanks for confirming the kickoff time. A few items from Redwood's side to make the validation crisp:

Acceptance criteria (proposal):
- Integration surface: API contract validated (request/response schema) with 0 critical mismatches.
- Security: API key auth works, token rotation tested, and SSO onboarding documented if applicable.
- Performance: p95 and p99 latency targets as Lucas specified.
- Reliability: documented fallback behavior when model variants are unavailable.
- Observability: request tracing, per-token cost visibility, and error logs surfaced for the test window.

Test matrix (quick):
- Smoke: 10 simple prompts (sanity + schema)
- Regression: 20 prompts including edge cases and hallucination-sensitive queries
- Load: ramp to 50 concurrent workers for 10 minutes (monitor error rate and p95/p99)
- Failover: simulate model variant eviction and confirm fallback route within 5s

Owners: Ethan will be Redwood's engineer-on-call for day-to-day. He'll reach out to Vera for keys and sample data.

Ethan — can you confirm you'll be available Tue 20 at 10:00 AM PT and share your cell briefly for quick sync?

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Partner Solutions | Redwood Inference

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
To: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>; Vera Chung <vera@integral.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Technical Onboarding & Interop Gatecheck — Next Steps

Thanks everyone for a productive kickoff this morning. Summary and next steps: 

Summary of outcomes:
- Access: Vera provisioned a time-limited API key for redwood-gatecheck and a small sandbox dataset.
- Test plan: We agreed to the smoke, regression, load, and failover tests described in Aisha's matrix.
- Targets: p95 < 150ms, p99 < 400ms, <1% error at 50 concurrent sustained.

Owners and timeline: 
- Redwood engineering lead (Ethan Park) — running tests and collecting traces (owner for test execution).
- Integral engineering (Vera Chung) — environment support and dataset provisioning.
- Partnership owners (Rishi / Lucas) — overall alignment and acceptance.

Immediate next steps (by Apr 26):
1) Ethan to run smoke + regression tests and upload raw traces to the shared drive.
2) Redwood to run a single 10-minute load test (50 concurrent) and capture p95/p99 and error rate.
3) Both sides to review runbook gaps and document fallback behavior.

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.xlsx (MVP milestones + owners).
DocuSign: we agreed to finalize a mutual MSA after successful validation — DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=ABC123

If anything above looks off, flag it here; otherwise we'll close the validation loop once test artifacts are uploaded to the shared folder.

Best,
Rishi Malhotra
Senior Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 08:30 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi all — thanks for confirming the kickoff time. A few items from Redwood's side to make the validation crisp:...

Attachment: mutual-action-plan.xlsx

Senior Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-gatecheck-template.docx

From: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vera Chung <vera@integral.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Rishi — thanks, that agenda looks good. Quick answers + access: 

- Staging environment: https://staging.integral.ai/redwood-integration (API keys will be created for "redwood-gatecheck" user).
- Primary contact: Vera Chung (cc'd) — she'll provision keys and a sandbox dataset.
- We want the first validation run against Redwood Hosted API variants (we'll evaluate Dedicated later).

Performance targets (what we expect to validate):
- Functional: end-to-end correctness for 95% of test prompts.
- Latency SLO: p95 < 150ms, p99 < 400ms for small-medium prompts (<= 512 tokens).
- Throughput: sustained 50 concurrent requests at representative payloads for 10 minutes without >1% error rate.

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/INT-4512 (for reference).

We can do Tue Apr 20 10:00 AM PT — I'll have Vera share keys before the call.

Lucas
Head of Platform Partnerships
Integral AI

> Lucas —
> 
> Appreciate the clear scope. I'm happy to run this validation with you. Quick responses below and a proposed kickoff agenda attached.
> 
> [see attached]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.ai>
To: Lucas Brenner <lucas@integral.ai>; Vera Chung <vera@integral.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>; Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Tech Onboarding: Gatecheck Request

Hi all — thanks for confirming the kickoff time. A few items from Redwood's side to make the validation crisp:

Acceptance criteria (proposal):
- Integration surface: API contract validated (request/response schema) with 0 critical mismatches.
- Security: API key auth works, token rotation tested, and SSO onboarding documented if applicable.
- Performance: p95 and p99 latency targets as Lucas specified.
- Reliability: documented fallback behavior when model variants are unavailable.
- Observability: request tracing, per-token cost visibility, and error logs surfaced for the test window.

Test matrix (quick):
- Smoke: 10 simple prompts (sanity + schema)
- Regression: 20 prompts including edge cases and hallucination-sensitive queries
- Load: ramp to 50 concurrent workers for 10 minutes (monitor error rate and p95/p99)
- Failover: simulate model variant eviction and confirm fallback route within 5s

Owners: Ethan will be Redwood's engineer-on-call for day-to-day. He'll reach out to Vera for keys and sample data.

Ethan — can you confirm you'll be available Tue 20 at 10:00 AM PT and share your cell briefly for quick sync?

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Partner Solutions | Redwood Inference

Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood x Integral - Technical Onboarding & Interop Gatecheck — Next Steps

Thanks everyone for a productive kickoff this morning. Summary and next steps: 

Summary of outcomes:
- Access: Vera provisioned a time-limited API key for redwood-gatecheck and a small sandbox dataset.
- Test plan: We agreed to the smoke, regression, load, and failover tests described in Aisha's matrix.
- Targets: p95 < 150ms, p99 < 400ms, <1% error at 50 concurrent sustained.

Owners and timeline: 
- Redwood engineering lead (Ethan Park) — running tests and collecting traces (owner for test execution).
- Integral engineering (Vera Chung) — environment support and dataset provisioning.
- Partnership owners (Rishi / Lucas) — overall alignment and acceptance.

Immediate next steps (by Apr 26):
1) Ethan to run smoke + regression tests and upload raw traces to the shared drive.
2) Redwood to run a single 10-minute load test (50 concurrent) and capture p95/p99 and error rate.
3) Both sides to review runbook gaps and document fallback behavior.

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.xlsx (MVP milestones + owners).
DocuSign: we agreed to finalize a mutual MSA after successful validation — DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=ABC123

If anything above looks off, flag it here; otherwise we'll close the validation loop once test artifacts are uploaded to the shared folder.

source.subject: Unit cost drivers & immediate next steps (post-demo)
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Ayesha Rahman <ayesha@luma-analytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 11 Apr 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Unit cost drivers & immediate next steps (post-demo)

Hi Ayesha — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap from our side so we’re aligned and can get you a tight pilot proposal.

What you said: (pain points)
- Heavy cost pressure on user-facing features: inference spend is ~60% of infra budget for the recommender microservice.
- High variance in prompt length across cohorts (avg 220 tokens, 95th ~950) causing unpredictable unit costs.
- Current stack: internal Docker-based inference on g4 instances + a small managed endpoint for experiments; no KV cache today.

Success criteria you called out:
- 50% reduction in per-request token cost without impacting 95th percentile latency SLA (target: <= 450ms for p95).
- Clear ops runbook for rolling back quantized variants if we see quality regression.
- Pilot with 2 models (chat+ranking) operating on 1m monthly queries.

What we propose as next steps:
1) Pilot scope doc (attached spreadsheet) — we’ll fill estimated unit-econ with your sample token distribution.
2) Short tech call with your infra lead (30–45m) to map out cache placement, expected batching windows, and network topology.
3) SOW draft (attached) + procurement timeline — target kickoff in 3 weeks if approved.

Open questions we’ll answer before the tech call:
- Will you allow 8-bit/4-bit quantized model variants for the pilot? (you indicated willingness to try 8-bit for non-critical paths)
- Are there any regulatory data residency constraints for production?

Attachments: redwood-inference-cost-metrics.xlsx (pilot cost template), sow-draft-v1.pdf (SOW skeleton).

If that matches your memory, can you confirm: (a) willingness to run quantized variants for the pilot, and (b) two names to include on the infra tech call? We’ll lock a time early next week.

Thanks —
Ethan
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
https://redwood.ai

From: Ayesha Rahman <ayesha@luma-analytics.com>
