source.subject: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps
messages:
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-08T16:00:00Z
Subject: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Hi Tara —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we can land the integration workstream: 

Recap of needs (from discovery):
- API access for server-side backend services (ingest, embeddings, reranker pipelines)
- Short-lived credentials for multi-tenant production pipelines (rotation + audit)
- Support for Okta / OIDC SSO for developer accounts and SAML for admin console
- Routing rules: low-latency route for live inference; cost-efficient fallback for batch/async jobs
- Structured output + tool-calling footprint for your document enrichment flow

Redwood recommended approach (high level):
- Primary: Service accounts + workload identity federation (no long-lived plaintext API keys). Issue short-lived JWTs bound to a service account, minted via your IdP or via Redwood federation.
- Secondary (fallback): Scoped API keys for non-sensitive lab or dev workloads (rotate every 30–90 days).
- Key rotation: automated JWK rotation + KMS-backed key material. We can surface rotation events in the console and push to your audit stream.
- Routing: tag service accounts with routing policy IDs (e.g., live/fast vs batch/low-cost). This lets requests be routed to different model variants automatically.
- Embeddings/reranker: per-request metadata tags so we can route heavy embedding windows to Dedicated pools and keep reranking on-host to minimize token egress.

Attachments: I attached a short "redwood-credentials-playbook.pdf" with detailed flows and a sample Mutual Action Plan.

Concrete next steps I propose:
1) Your security team to send the short questionnaire we shared (I attached an editable xlsx).
2) Schedule a 60-minute engineering deep-dive next week (I suggest Mon/Tue mornings PST).
3) We’ll provide a sample Terraform snippet for service account creation + a workload-identity diagram (attached).

If that looks good I can loop in our solutions engineer (Monica) to run the deep-dive and land the M A P.

Best,
Vanessa Ortiz
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: redwood-credentials-playbook.pdf (pdf, 320KB)

From: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Finance Security <security@avafinance.com>
Date: 2027-04-09T14:12:00Z
Subject: Re: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Vanessa — thanks for the clear summary. A couple of clarifying questions from security/infra so our SREs can scope the work: 

1) Rotation windows: do you recommend 30 days or 90 days for scoped API keys if we need them? What about token TTL for the short-lived JWTs (1h? 15m?) and refresh best practices?
2) We use Okta and would prefer OIDC / SAML integration. Does Redwood support workload identity federation directly to Okta (no service account keys stored in our terraform state)?
3) Audit & evidence: can we ship audit logs from Redwood to our SIEM (Splunk) or will we need to pull them from the Console? Are log exports real-time?
4) Embedding cost question: if we route embeddings to a Dedicated pool, does that materially change per-embedding pricing or is it just a billing allocation?

If you can answer those items we’ll decide whether to run the deep-dive or send a smaller set of engineering folks first. Also looping in our security lead (copied).

—Tara
Tara Chen
Head of Platform Engineering | Ava Finance
tara.chen@avafinance.com

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-10T09:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Hi Tara — great questions. I’m the Solutions Engineer who will run the deep-dive if you want to schedule one. Answers below with implementation notes.

1) Rotation windows
- Scoped API keys: we recommend a 30–60 day rotation for keys used in production admin workflows. Keys used only for temporary testing can be longer but we discourage >90 days for production.
- Short-lived JWTs: typical TTL is 1 hour; recommended client-side refresh window is 10–15 minutes before expiry. For highly sensitive flows we use 15m tokens with a rotating refresh token pattern.

2) Okta / workload identity federation
- Yes: Redwood supports OIDC federation and can mint service-account-bound tokens via Okta without storing long-lived keys in your terraform state. Pattern: create a "federated identity" in Redwood console pointing to your Okta app; Redwood validates IdP assertions and issues short-lived credentials scoped to a service account.
- We can provide a step-by-step guide and a Terraform module that wires the Okta OIDC trust with minimal manual steps.

3) Audit & evidence
- Audit logs: we offer two delivery mechanisms: push to your SIEM via secure syslog/HTTPS streaming (near real-time with <1m typical latency) or pull from Console exports (daily snapshots). For audits we can provide signed event bundles for a given time range.
- We’ll share the endpoint and example config during the deep-dive.

4) Embedding cost implications
- Billing model: Dedicated pool usage is billed to the account that owns the Dedicated capacity. Per-embedding cost is a function of the model and compute used; routing embeddings to Dedicated does not inherently increase per-unit token cost but changes the allocation of spend and can lower egress if embeddings are computed inside your VPC/Dedicated environment.
- Suggested approach: route large batch embed jobs to Dedicated and keep low-latency, small-window embeddings on Hosted with caching enabled.

Sample excerpt (service-account TF snippet):
-----BEGIN SAMPLE-----
resource "redwood_service_account" "ava_ingest" {
  name        = "ava-ingest-sa"
  description = "Service account for Ava Finance ingestion pipeline"
  scopes      = ["inference:generate","embeddings:create"]
  routing_policy = "batch-dedicated"
}

resource "redwood_federated_identity" "ava_okta" {
  provider = "okta"
  issuer   = "https://ava.okta.com"
  client_id = var.ava_client_id
  mapping = {"ava-ingest-sa" = "ava-ingest"}
}
-----END SAMPLE-----

Attachments: I’m attaching a sample TF file and a diagram that shows the OIDC flow + refresh cycle. For the deep-dive we can walk through the terraform module and the Console steps.

Proposed next step from me: 60m deep-dive with your infra + security teams. I have availability Tue 10–12 PST and Thu 08–11 PST. Vanessa — if either of those windows works for Ava, please confirm and I’ll put a calendar invite with a short agenda.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: sample-service-account-terraform.tf (text, 4KB)
Attachment: workload-identity-flow.drawio (drawio, 120KB)

From: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Finance Security <security@avafinance.com>
Date: 2027-04-12T09:23:00Z
Subject: Re: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Thanks Monica — that all sounds good. We’ll take Tue 2027-04-13 10:00 PST for the deep-dive (I'll send a calendar invite).

Security: we'll start with the short questionnaire and share our Okta app details ahead of time.

Tara

1) Rotation windows: do you recommend 30 days or 90 days for scoped API keys if we need them? What about token TTL for the short-lived JWTs (1h? 15m?) and refresh best practices?

3) Audit & evidence: can we ship audit logs from Redwood to our SIEM (Splunk) or will we need to pull them from the Console? Are log exports real-time?
4) Embedding cost question: if we route embeddings to a Dedicated pool, does that materially change per-embedding pricing or is it just a billing allocation?

If you can answer those items we’ll decide whether to run the deep-dive or send a smaller set of engineering folks first. Also looping in our security lead (copied).

—Tara
Tara Chen
Head of Platform Engineering | Ava Finance
tara.chen@avafinance.com


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-10T09:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Hi Tara — great questions. I’m the Solutions Engineer who will run the deep-dive if you want to schedule one. Answers below with implementation notes.

1) Rotation windows
- Scoped API keys: we recommend a 30–60 day rotation for keys used in production admin workflows. Keys used only for temporary testing can be longer but we discourage >90 days for production.
- Short-lived JWTs: typical TTL is 1 hour; recommended client-side refresh window is 10–15 minutes before expiry. For highly sensitive flows we use 15m tokens with a rotating refresh token pattern.

2) Okta / workload identity federation
- Yes: Redwood supports OIDC federation and can mint service-account-bound tokens via Okta without storing long-lived keys in your terraform state. Pattern: create a \"federated identity\" in Redwood console pointing to your Okta app; Redwood validates IdP assertions and issues short-lived credentials scoped to a service account.
- We can provide a step-by-step guide and a Terraform module that wires the Okta OIDC trust with minimal manual steps.

3) Audit & evidence
- Audit logs: we offer two delivery mechanisms: push to your SIEM via secure syslog/HTTPS streaming (near real-time with <1m typical latency) or pull from Console exports (daily snapshots). For audits we can provide signed event bundles for a given time range.
- We’ll share the endpoint and example config during the deep-dive.

4) Embedding cost implications
- Billing model: Dedicated pool usage is billed to the account that owns the Dedicated capacity. Per-embedding cost is a function of the model and compute used; routing embeddings to Dedicated does not inherently increase per-unit token cost but changes the allocation of spend and can lower egress if embeddings are computed inside your VPC/Dedicated environment.
- Suggested approach: route large batch embed jobs to Dedicated and keep low-latency, small-window embeddings on Hosted with caching enabled.

Sample excerpt (service-account TF snippet):
-----BEGIN SAMPLE-----
resource \"redwood_service_account\" \"ava_ingest\" {
  name        = \"ava-ingest-sa\"
  description = \"Service account for Ava Finance ingestion pipeline\"
  scopes      = [\"inference:generate\",\"embeddings:create\"]
  routing_policy = \"batch-dedicated\"
}

resource \"redwood_federated_identity\" \"ava_okta\" {
  provider = \"okta\"
  issuer   = \"https://ava.okta.com\"
  client_id = var.ava_client_id
  mapping = {\"ava-ingest-sa\" = \"ava-ingest\"}
}
-----END SAMPLE-----

Attachments: I’m attaching a sample TF file and a diagram that shows the OIDC flow + refresh cycle. For the deep-dive we can walk through the terraform module and the Console steps.

Proposed next step from me: 60m deep-dive with your infra + security teams. I have availability Tue 10–12 PST and Thu 08–11 PST. Vanessa — if either of those windows works for Ava, please confirm and I’ll put a calendar invite with a short agenda.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: sample-service-account-terraform.tf (text, 4KB)
Attachment: workload-identity-flow.drawio (drawio, 120KB)


From: Tara Chen <tara.chen@avafinance.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Finance Security <security@avafinance.com>
Date: 2027-04-12T09:23:00Z
Subject: Re: Credentials onboarding & integration checklist — next steps

Thanks Monica — that all sounds good. We’ll take Tue 2027-04-13 10:00 PST for the deep-dive (I'll send a calendar invite).

Security: we'll start with the short questionnaire and share our Okta app details ahead of time.

Tara

source.subject: p95/p99 spike from request backlog — Medilens production impact
messages:
From: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@medilens.ai>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@medilens.ai
Date: 2027-04-10T08:12:00Z
Subject: Production latency spike — p95/p99 regression since 08:00 UTC

Naomi,

We started seeing a sharp increase in API latency at ~08:00 UTC. Our p95 has climbed from ~140ms to ~1.1s and p99 to ~3.4s. Error rates have ticked up (5xxs) and some inference calls are timing out. This is impacting our document ingestion pipeline — downstream processing is delayed and retries are piling up.

We've disabled some non-critical features, but saw little improvement. Can you confirm whether anything changed on Redwood's side (deploys, routing, batching defaults)? We're on the hosted API in us-west with model pinned to /redwood/gpt-neox-8k/v2.

Attaching the brief logs we pulled. Please advise immediate mitigations; this is affecting a production job.

- Sarah
Sarah Lee
Head of Platform Ops, Medilens

Attachments: medilens-logs-0800-0830.json (log excerpt)

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@medilens.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-10T08:25:00Z
Subject: Re: Production latency spike — p95/p99 regression since 08:00 UTC

Sarah, thanks for flagging — we see the same signal in our global ingestion dashboards. We're triaging now. Quick notes:
- Suspending new canary rollouts immediately (no deploys at 08:00).
- Engineering (cc'ed) taking lead on root cause.
- Short-term: we'll attempt tenant-level routing to a lower-latency pool and reduce max_batch_wait on Medilens traffic.

We will send an initial RCA and mitigation ETA within 30 minutes. We're treating this as Sev2 (production impact).

Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@medilens.ai>
Date: 2027-04-10T08:48:00Z
Subject: Re: Production latency spike — p95/p99 regression since 08:00 UTC

Quick findings from the runtime:

1) Symptom summary (08:00–08:30 UTC):
   - p50 ~ 110ms (unchanged)
   - p95 increased from ~140ms -> ~1.12s
   - p99 increased from ~480ms -> ~3.6s
   - 5xx rate increased from 0.02% -> 0.9% (spike in 504 timeouts in our LB logs)

2) Root cause hypothesis: batching/queue-depth amplification. Around 07:58 UTC we observed a transient change in sequence length distribution for Medilens (more long-context requests) combined with a short-lived surge in concurrent connections from a single client job. The runtime's continuous-batcher extended batching windows to reach target batch sizes, increasing queue depth by 6–8x for Medilens traffic. That produced pronounced tail latency (higher p95/p99) and some request expirations.

3) Why did this affect only Medilens?
   - They are pinned to a multi-tenant GPU pool with slightly higher baseline load; our smart-routing didn't migrate them because the pool wasn't marked under threshold.
   - Their KV-cache had low hit-rate for this workload (prefixes changed), so inference cost per request jumped, slowing throughput.

4) Immediate mitigations we applied (08:40 UTC):
   - Reduced max_batch_wait_ms for Medilens route to 4ms (from 20ms) — reduces batching window and queue depth.
   - Enabled tenant-specific priority routing to an underutilized pool in us-west for the next 90 minutes.
   - Triggered KV-cache warmup for common prefixes on their model instance.

5) Next steps (ETA):
   - Short-term: observe metrics for 30 minutes (ETA 09:10 UTC).
   - Mid-term: create a dedicated reserved slot for Medilens to avoid multi-tenant amplification (proposal in PR). ETA 2 business days to provision for test; 5–7 days for production route.
   - Postmortem: full RCA with timeline and perf artifacts.

I've attached a qdepth graph and a 30s stack trace showing batcher hold times.

—Ethan
Ethan Park
SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachments: qdepth-metrics-20270410.png, stack-trace-output.txt

From: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@medilens.ai>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-10T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Production latency spike — p95/p99 regression since 08:00 UTC

Thanks Ethan — the change you made already looks promising. p95 is down to ~210ms after routing and batch window reduction, p99 trending toward 650ms. We'll keep some non-critical jobs paused for the next hour while you validate.

Two asks:
1) Can you provide an ETA for the dedicated routing or a mitigation that won't require us to move traffic to a different model? Our compliance team needs confirmation this won't re-occur during their next ingest window (monitored nightly).
2) For credits/compensation: this outage caused a 6-hour processing delay; let us know what remedies Redwood can offer.

Appreciate the quick response.

- Sarah

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@medilens.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-10T14:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Production latency spike — p95/p99 regression since 08:00 UTC — summary & next steps

Sarah — final update / exec summary for the incident today:  

What happened
- At ~07:58 UTC a combination of an unexpected shift in request length distribution and a short concurrent-connection surge from a Medilens job increased queue depth in our multi-tenant batcher. The batcher increased wait time to hit batch-size targets which amplified tail latency. Some requests timed out. 

Impact
- p95 regressed to ~1.12s (from ~140ms) and p99 to ~3.6s for ~35 minutes. Retry traffic slightly increased 5xx visible in LB. Medilens observed ~6 hours of downstream processing delay due to the spike and retries.

Actions taken (complete)
- Reduced tenant batch waiting threshold (max_batch_wait_ms -> 4ms).
- Tenant-priority routing to an underutilized pool in us-west for immediate relief.
- KV-cache warmup for common inference prefixes.
- Monitoring and alerting rules adjusted to fire on correlated queue-depth + sequence-length anomalies.

Planned actions (timeline)
- Immediate (today): keep tenant on priority pool for 24 hours; monitor metrics and rollback if regressions. Owner: Ethan.
- 48 hours: provision a short-lived dedicated slot for Medilens (test route + load test). Owner: Sean.
- 5–7 days: production dedicated routing or capacity reservation, plus a configuration flag to cap per-tenant batching windows under surge. Owner: Naomi/Infra.
- Postmortem and delivery of credits proposal: our CS/finance team will follow up within 48 hours.

We will provide exportable artifacts (qdepth graphs, traces, the stack trace) and a short RCA doc. For immediate compensation/credits request, I've looped in Sean from Sales to coordinate.

Sean — please advise on compensation guidelines for an incident of this scope; we should propose a goodwill credit and prioritized capacity provisioning.

Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

Attachments: incident-summary-20270410.pdf (pending), qdepth-metrics-20270410.png

source.subject: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze
source.body:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Raymond <alex@equinox.ai>, Elyse Navarro <elyse@bigretail.co>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze

Hi Alex / Elyse,

Thanks again for sending over the initial artifact. We kicked off an internal intake with infra and runtime yesterday and I wanted to loop in the product and eng owners for a quick alignment. Two asks from our side to avoid last-minute regressions during the onboarding window:

1) Can Equinox provide a short compatibility checklist (hardware kernel targets, expected max-sequence, KV cache footprint for 8k context) and an explicit list of variants that are API-compatible with the 8B client we serve today?

2) Requesting a temporary model "freeze" window while we run our E2E integration tests and perf validation. Proposed window: 2027-04-12 00:00 UTC — 2027-04-16 23:59 UTC. During the window we ask that no new public variants be pushed to the artifact registry for the Equinox-XL tag used by Elyse's environment.

Monica / Dev — can you confirm the exact telemetry and regression checks we will run so I can communicate requirements to the Equinox team?

Elyse — we'll coordinate the validation tests with you once we have the checklist. Ideally we want alignment on the fallback policy if we see a perf or tokenization regression.

Attaching the initial compatibility checklist template we will use; Alex mentioned a binary artifact will follow.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

---

From: Alex Raymond <alex@equinox.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elyse Navarro <elyse@bigretail.co>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 13:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear asks. We're attaching the v0.9 artifact (equinox-xl-v0.9.tar.gz) and a short compatibility matrix that answers the kernel/sequence/KV questions. Quick notes:

- v0.9 is the release we'd like you to validate. There are internal variants for int8/int4 quant that are functionally equivalent, but they have different memory layout; we'll flag which ones are binary-compatible with your runtime in the matrix.
- We can hold the public tag update to 2027-04-17 00:00 UTC to support your freeze window.

I'll also add a small README into the artifact with expected perf numbers on our test infra. Let me know if you need a sanitized trace (we can provide).

Alex
Principal Engineer, Equinox AI
alex@equinox.ai

Attachment: equinox-xl-v0.9.tar.gz (model artifact)

---

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 6 Apr 2027 09:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze

Ben — tagging in the test plan. High level overview of what we'll run during the freeze window:

- Functional: tokenization/ID mapping checks using the Equinox sample prompts (coverage: English + es + zh)
- Perf: p50/p95 latency under our continuous-batch load profile (batch sizes 1, 4, 16) on A100-80GB and RTX-8000 staging nodes
- Memory: KV growth and peak resident set over 8k context with synthetic traces
- Accuracy smoke: 100 canonical prompts, BLEU/ROUGE-like checks for deterministic tasks and soft checks for sampling tasks; flag any >5% deviation on deterministic prompts
- Telemetry: token-level latency, OOM events, kernel fallback counts, quant-decoding mismatches

We will instrument the above and post results to the Drive folder. If anything fails we'll run a rollback to the last verified variant and open JIRA-INF-3421 with failure details.

I attached the compatibility checklist template and a short rollout runbook (how we switch traffic and rollback).

—Monica
Senior Product Manager, Model Ops
monica.patel@redwood.ai

Attachments: compatibility-checklist.xlsx, rollout-runbook.md

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Alex Raymond <alex@equinox.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze

Quick technical clarifications from runtime:

- Please confirm whether Equinox v0.9 includes a custom fused attention kernel. If yes, we need the kernel selection hints (or a fallback path).
- For quant variants: confirm whether the int4 variant uses per-channel scales or per-tensor; per-channel will require a minor change to our loader.
- We will run the KV checkpoint test (writes/reads) against a 6-hour synthetic replay to validate cache correctness.

If Alex can confirm the kernel/quant details by EOD Tuesday (2027-04-07), we'll be able to finalize the test harness and sign-off the freeze window.

Dev
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
dev.patel@redwood.ai

---

From: Alex Raymond <alex@equinox.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elyse Navarro <elyse@bigretail.co>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 16:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Equinox-XL integration request — compat checklist + temporary model freeze

Hi all — confirming answers inline:

- Fused kernel: v0.9 does include an optional fused attention kernel. We've included a fallback CPU/kernel implementation in the artifact. We'll add kernel selection hints to the README by EOD.
- Int4 quant: uses per-channel scales. We understand that requires a loader change. I'll include a small patch snippet in the Drive folder so your loader team can preview.

source.subject: Mistral Health renewal — proposed commit shift & term amendment
messages:
From: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion + updated 12mo forecast

Hi Ethan,

We’re kicking off our renewal planning for Mistral Health and I wanted to flag a few items up front so we can make the next quarter tidy: 1) our usage is likely to increase ~25% in Q3 due to a docs ingestion project; 2) procurement needs a clear ceiling/price-hold for first 6 months; and 3) we’re open to re-structuring the commit if there’s a credit/discount trade that makes sense.

Can you send over a short set of options (3 paths) that show: commit levels, net effective rates, any upfront credits, and what a 6-month price-hold would look like? Also include any exclusions (e.g., Private deployments) and how bursting over commit would be handled.

We’ll pull in Ben Owens and our procurement inbox to review. Happy to do a 30-min sync next week.

Thanks,
Sandra Kim
Head of ML Platform
Mistral Health
attachments: forecast_Q3_2027.csv (spreadsheet stub)

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>, Ben Owens <benjamin.owens@mistralhealth.com>
Cc: procurement@mistralhealth.com
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion + updated 12mo forecast

Thanks Sandra — appreciate the heads up. Attached is a short package with three alternative renewal paths and a commit calculator so you can toggle the uplift. High level:

Option A — Moderate uplift (recommended)- 12-month commit + 25% uplift vs current baseline- 10% off list rates year 1 (applied as billing credits across first 3 months)- 6-month price-hold on base token price; burst bill at standard overage rates- No coverage for Private/on-prem consumption (must be quoted separately)

Option B — Aggressive commit (best unit economics)- 18-month commit + 40% uplift- 18% effective discount in year 1 (one-time prepaid credit applied monthly)- Price-hold for 9 months- Includes limited burst pool (configured as reserved burst capacity)

Option C — Low-commit safety net- 12-month with only 10% uplift- 5% discount + $30k credit bucket to offset overages- 6-month price-hold limited to hosted API only

Notes: all options assume current Mistral model variants. We’ve excluded Private deployments from the price-hold because of different hosting cost profiles and auditing needs. I’ve attached a redlineable term sheet (v3) and the commit calculator.

If any of the numbers look promising, I can schedule a 30-min call with Marissa and Vivek to quickly align on what concessions we can approve from Regional Sales vs. what needs a CS/Finance escalation. Would Thursday 10am PT work for you?

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf; Redwood-Commit-Calculator.xlsx

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Mistral renewal — proposed commit shift & term amendment (need quick decision)

Marissa/Vivek — forwarding Sandra’s ask and the options I sent. Quick summary and ask:

Context: Mistral is a mid-market healthcare customer with predictable growth and strict procurement windows. They want a 6-month price-hold and are willing to increase commit if we provide credits/discounts. Usage forecast attached suggests a Q3 spike of ~25%.

What I’m proposing we can approve at regional level (no legal change):

1) Offer Option A as the baseline (12m, +25% uplift) with 10% year-1 discount applied as a $90k credit bucket spread over first 90 days.
2) Include a firm 6-month price-hold only for hosted API, with explicit exclusion clause for Private/on-prem – we’ll add an audit right if they push.
3) Add a simple burst policy: overage billed at published overage rate unless they auto-scale into burst pool; any sustained >120% of commit for 2 months triggers review.

What needs escalation to Finance/CS:
- Option B (18% effective discount + 9-month price-hold) — needs finance approval because of prepaid credit mechanics.

Approval ask: Can you sign off on Option A as a delegated concession (up to $100k total credits) and confirm the language we should use for the Private deployment carve-out? If yes I’ll send amended term sheet and a DocuSign early next week.

Quoted from Sandra’s original: "> procurement needs a clear ceiling/price-hold for first 6 months; and we’re open to re-structuring the commit if there’s a credit/discount trade that makes sense."

Thanks — quick thumbs up would be great.

—Ethan

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>, Ben Owens <benjamin.owens@mistralhealth.com>, procurement@mistralhealth.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Apr 2027 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: Final — amended term sheet & next steps (DocuSign link)

Sandra / Ben — thanks for the call last week. Per our conversation we are aligned on the Option A path: 12-month commitment with a 25% uplift and a $90k credit applied across the first 90 days (appears as billing credits). Key points summarized below: 

- Commit: 25% uplift vs current baseline for 12 months- Credits: $90,000 applied as bill credits across months 1–3- Price-hold: 6 months on hosted API pricing; Private/on-prem excluded and billed per a separate quote- Burst: overage at published overage rates; sustained >120% of commit for two consecutive months will trigger a capacity review and proposed restructure- Term sheet attached (Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf) and commit calculator included

I’ve attached the redline and sent the DocuSign envelope here: https://docusign.com/envelope/ABC123456789 — please review and sign by Friday to lock the price-hold window. If procurement needs a revised PO flow we can include that as an appendix (procurement@mistralhealth.com).

If you’d like the Option B economics pulled for internal review, we can do that, but it will need finance sign-off.

Thanks again — looking forward to continuing the partnership.

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf; Redwood-Commit-Calculator.xlsx

1) Offer Option A as the baseline (12m, +25% uplift) with 10% year-1 discount applied as a $90k credit bucket spread over first 90 days.
2) Include a firm 6-month price-hold only for hosted API, with explicit exclusion clause for Private/on-prem – we’ll add an audit right if they push.
3) Add a simple burst policy: overage billed at published overage rate unless they auto-scale into burst pool; any sustained >120% of commit for 2 months triggers review.

What needs escalation to Finance/CS:
- Option B (18% effective discount + 9-month price-hold) — needs finance approval because of prepaid credit mechanics.

Approval ask: Can you sign off on Option A as a delegated concession (up to $100k total credits) and confirm the language we should use for the Private deployment carve-out? If yes I’ll send amended term sheet and a DocuSign early next week.

Quoted from Sandra’s original: "> procurement needs a clear ceiling/price-hold for first 6 months; and we’re open to re-structuring the commit if there’s a credit/discount trade that makes sense."

Thanks — quick thumbs up would be great.

—Ethan

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>, Ben Owens <benjamin.owens@mistralhealth.com>, procurement@mistralhealth.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Apr 2027 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: Final — amended term sheet & next steps (DocuSign link)

Sandra / Ben — thanks for the call last week. Per our conversation we are aligned on the Option A path: 12-month commitment with a 25% uplift and a $90k credit applied across the first 90 days (appears as billing credits). Key points summarized below: 

- Commit: 25% uplift vs current baseline for 12 months- Credits: $90,000 applied as bill credits across months 1–3- Price-hold: 6 months on hosted API pricing; Private/on-prem excluded and billed per a separate quote- Burst: overage at published overage rates; sustained >120% of commit for two consecutive months will trigger a capacity review and proposed restructure- Term sheet attached (Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf) and commit calculator included

I’ve attached the redline and sent the DocuSign envelope here: https://docusign.com/envelope/ABC123456789 — please review and sign by Friday to lock the price-hold window. If procurement needs a revised PO flow we can include that as an appendix (procurement@mistralhealth.com).

If you’d like the Option B economics pulled for internal review, we can do that, but it will need finance sign-off.

Thanks again — looking forward to continuing the partnership.

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf; Redwood-Commit-Calculator.xlsx
text:
From: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion + updated 12mo forecast

Hi Ethan,

We’re kicking off our renewal planning for Mistral Health and I wanted to flag a few items up front so we can make the next quarter tidy: 1) our usage is likely to increase ~25% in Q3 due to a docs ingestion project; 2) procurement needs a clear ceiling/price-hold for first 6 months; and 3) we’re open to re-structuring the commit if there’s a credit/discount trade that makes sense.

Can you send over a short set of options (3 paths) that show: commit levels, net effective rates, any upfront credits, and what a 6-month price-hold would look like? Also include any exclusions (e.g., Private deployments) and how bursting over commit would be handled.

We’ll pull in Ben Owens and our procurement inbox to review. Happy to do a 30-min sync next week.

Thanks,
Sandra Kim
Head of ML Platform
Mistral Health
attachments: forecast_Q3_2027.csv (spreadsheet stub)

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Sandra Kim <sandra.kim@mistralhealth.com>, Ben Owens <benjamin.owens@mistralhealth.com>
Cc: procurement@mistralhealth.com
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion + updated 12mo forecast

Thanks Sandra — appreciate the heads up. Attached is a short package with three alternative renewal paths and a commit calculator so you can toggle the uplift. High level:

Option A — Moderate uplift (recommended)- 12-month commit + 25% uplift vs current baseline- 10% off list rates year 1 (applied as billing credits across first 3 months)- 6-month price-hold on base token price; burst bill at standard overage rates- No coverage for Private/on-prem consumption (must be quoted separately)

Option B — Aggressive commit (best unit economics)- 18-month commit + 40% uplift- 18% effective discount in year 1 (one-time prepaid credit applied monthly)- Price-hold for 9 months- Includes limited burst pool (configured as reserved burst capacity)

Option C — Low-commit safety net- 12-month with only 10% uplift- 5% discount + $30k credit bucket to offset overages- 6-month price-hold limited to hosted API only

Notes: all options assume current Mistral model variants. We’ve excluded Private deployments from the price-hold because of different hosting cost profiles and auditing needs. I’ve attached a redlineable term sheet (v3) and the commit calculator.

If any of the numbers look promising, I can schedule a 30-min call with Marissa and Vivek to quickly align on what concessions we can approve from Regional Sales vs. what needs a CS/Finance escalation. Would Thursday 10am PT work for you?

Best,
Ethan Park
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: Mistral_Renewal_Term_Sheet_v3.pdf; Redwood-Commit-Calculator.xlsx

source.subject: Pilot evaluation framework and next steps — Veridian Analytics
messages:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@veridian.ai>
Cc: Veridian Procurement <procurement@veridian.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot evaluation framework and next steps — Veridian Analytics

Hi Samira —

Thanks again for your time earlier. Quick recap from our side and a proposed lightweight pilot that will let your engineering team validate latency, cost, and integration effort over a 3-week sprint.

Goal (what success looks like)
- Ingest ~100k historical support interactions and run embedding + semantic search for a sample UI.
- Achieve median query latency < 250ms for 95% of queries at target concurrency (~20 QPS).
- Demonstrate cost per 1k tokens within target band you mentioned (~$0.35–$0.50).
- Show reproducible evaluation results and basic security controls (VPC peering + audit log access).

Proposed scope & timeline (high level)
Week 0 (prep) — access, dataset slice, config (2–3 days)
Week 1 — ingest + index, baseline embeddings, smoke tests
Week 2 — load testing, latency tuning, cost profiling, QC on output quality (prompt sets we discussed)
Week 3 — wrap, results doc, recommendations and optional A/B test against your current setup

Ownership
- Diego Fernandez (Sales): project owner, timeline and commercial coordination
- Monica Patel (Solutions Eng): integration, ingestion, and test harness
- Veridian: provide dataset slice, product contact for API integration (TBD)

Attachments: veridian_pilot_plan_v1.pdf (pilot outline), redwood_trial_config.yaml (example config for trial cluster)

Immediate asks / next steps
1) Can you confirm the two contacts from the Veridian product team who will be involved and whether the dataset can be shared via S3 link (or if we should use an SFTP)?
2) Do procurement folks need a draft SOW or a short commercial note to start internal review?
3) Proposed kickoff: Tue 2027-04-06 at 10:00 PT — does that work?

I left a few optional knobs in the attached plan (quantization profile vs. full fp16) — Monica can propose the concrete trial config once we lock the dataset and region.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Diego Fernandez | Redwood Inference
<diego@redwood.com>

---
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@veridian.ai>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Cc: Veridian Procurement <procurement@veridian.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot evaluation framework and next steps — Veridian Analytics

Diego —

Appreciate the clear plan and timeline. A few quick items from our side:

- I'll loop in Ravi (product) and Lina (platform) for the dataset/API work — Ravi will be the product lead.
- For data transfer, S3 link is ideal; we control the bucket. We need to confirm encryption-at-rest and a short data handling note for procurement.
- Procurement would prefer a short commercial note (summary of services + single paragraph on billing) rather than a full SOW to start. Attaching procurement contact (procurement@veridian.ai) to this thread.

Questions:
1) For the latency target: can you confirm whether the 250ms is cold-start or steady-state? We care about both but want to understand what to expect for our UI.
2) Any constraints on using our EU region for test data? We prefer US for this pilot but may need to switch later.

Tue 4/6 10am PT works for kickoff — please send calendar invite.

Thanks —
Samira Khan
Director, Product
Veridian Analytics
samira.k@veridian.ai

---
(Attachment: none)
From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@veridian.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Veridian Procurement <procurement@veridian.ai>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot evaluation framework and next steps — Veridian Analytics

Thanks Samira — quick answers inline and an updated ask list.

Latency: the 250ms target is steady-state p95 for token generation/response once the model is warmed (i.e., after initial KV cache warmup). Cold-starts will be higher depending on model size and session length; Monica will include expected cold/start numbers in the trial config.

Region: happy to run in US for the pilot. If you want EU later, we can replicate the setup but it'll add ~2 business days for infra provisioning.

Procurement: noted — I will attach a 1-page commercial summary to the calendar invite.

Updated ownership + tech contacts
- Diego Fernandez (Sales) — kickoff, calendar and commercial summary
- Monica Patel (SE) — technical lead for ingestion, config, and test harness (monica@redwood.com)
- Ethan Park (Customer Success) — trial success criteria tracking and post-pilot recommendations (ethan@redwood.com)

Attachments: veridian_pilot_plan_v1.pdf, redwood_trial_config.yaml

Next steps (before Tue kickoff)
- Veridian: provide S3 link to a 100k-row dataset slice and product contact (Ravi).
- Redwood: send calendar invite w/ 1-page commercial summary and preliminary test script.

Monica and Ethan — can you confirm you can run the ingestion test and produce an initial latency profile by EOD Wed? If not, flag blockers.

Diego

---
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>, Samira Khan <samira.k@veridian.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Apr 2027 14:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot evaluation framework and next steps — Veridian Analytics

Hi all —

I'm good to run ingestion and an initial latency profile. Quick tech checklist so Samira/Ravi know what we'll need on the kickoff:

Pre-kickoff checklist (tech)
- S3 read-only IAM role (temporary) or presigned URL for the dataset slice
- Example queries / prompt set (10–20) for quality checks
- Target concurrency/peak QPS numbers (you mentioned ~20 QPS)
- Any blocked IP ranges for the trial (if you have strict allowlists)

I'll run the ingestion on a small dev cluster and then scale to the trial config in redwood_trial_config.yaml during Week 1. Expect an initial p95 latency snapshot within 24 hours of ingestion.

Diego — I'll join the kickoff on Tue and send a short tech-setup doc to the Drive link ahead of the meeting.

Best,
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
(Attachment referenced: tech-setup-checklist.md — will be uploaded to the Drive link)

source.subject: StratusBank governance annex — technical response and eval workflow
messages:
From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@stratusbank.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@stratusbank.com>, procure@stratusbank.com
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: StratusBank RFP — governance annex and technical questions

Hi Ethan,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As part of our procurement for the digital customer engagement platform, our security and procurement teams have a governance annex that we need Redwood to complete. I attached our annex (StratusBank_Governance_Annex_20270402.docx). Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide a technical annex and evidence matrix by EOD Monday (4/5)? If not, please propose a timeline. We also need an initial statement on whether you consider the targeted use high-risk under EU AI Act so we can flag for legal review.

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: StratusBank RFP — governance annex and technical questions

Team — forwarding StratusBank annex (attached). Laura asked for a technical annex + evidence matrix by EOD Monday (4/5). Quick asks:

- Marissa: Can you draft the technical response focusing on NIST AI RMF mapping and our eval/regression workflow. Please include operational metrics we monitor (latency, token safety flags, semantic drift measures, hallucination sampling) and the proposed regression harness specifics (integration points, sampling strategy, thresholds).
- Neha: Pull SOC 2 and KMS/KPIs evidence (SOC2_report_2026.pdf, key rotation policy, KMS certification notes). Add any callouts for EU data residency/private deployment options.
- Vivek: Provide the recommended cadence for evals and canary rollout criteria (percentages, rollback thresholds) and a short paragraph on SLA impacts if the customer chooses Dedicated vs Hosted.

I'll consolidate and send final to Laura. Please send drafts/comments inline by EOD Sunday night so I can assemble Monday morning.

Thanks — Ethan
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 3 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: StratusBank RFP — governance annex and technical questions

Ethan — draft answers below (please review). I'll attach a concise technical annex PDF and an evidence_matrix.csv when we consolidate.

1) NIST AI RMF mapping (high level)
- Identify: We maintain an inventory of served models (catalog with model_id, version, quantization profile, training origin tags). Inventory is accessible via our Console API and exported for audits.
- Protect: Access controls via RBAC, per-route ACLs, VPC Private deployments, and KMS-backed keys for model artifacts and encryption at rest. Transport encryption TLS 1.3.
- Detect: Runtime telemetry (per-request token scoring, safety trigger flags, rate anomaly detection) aggregated into traces and alerts. We retain trace-level logs per customer per their retention settings and can provide log extracts for audits.
- Respond: Automated fallback routing to lower-capacity or alternative model variants when safety thresholds or latency SLAs are breached. Incident runbooks and post-mortem templates; we provide incident reports for Dedicated/Private customers as part of SLA.
- Recover: Version pinning and rollback tooling (canary abort/automatic rollback) plus snapshots of KV cache state for continuity during rollbacks.

2) EU AI Act positioning
- High-risk determination: Based on the RFP description (loan-eligibility assistance used to triage explicit customer outcomes), the deployment is likely to be considered "high-risk" under the EU AI Act if used to make or materially influence significant decisions. Our recommended positioning:
  - Treat the integration as high-risk for controls design and evidence production.
  - Offer Private (VPC) deployment and dedicated capacity to satisfy data residency and accountability requirements.
  - Provide documentation required by Article 13 (technical documentation) and the post-market monitoring plan.
  - Implement explainability aids: structured output templates, confidence metadata, and request-level provenance to support auditability.

3) Evaluation and regression approach (operational plan)
- Baseline selection: We use a locked baseline model for each customer integration; baselines are snapshot versions with stable tokenization and metrics.
- Continuous evaluation: Production-facing telemetry is sampled (default 1% of traffic, configurable) and routed to an offline evaluation harness. Sampling is stratified by route, language, and user segment to preserve representativeness.
- Metrics: Unit tests + black-box metrics: response correctness (task-specific F1/ROUGE where applicable), safety violation rate (triggered safety filters per 10k responses), hallucination sampling (human-annotated rate on sampled outputs), latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99), and token-cost delta vs baseline.
- Regression harness: Automated jobs run nightly for fast-turnaround (smoke + safety checks) and weekly for full-cadence regression (larger sample sets with human annotation). Fail-states auto-block promotions to staging/production.
- Rollout policy: Canary -> phased rollout -> full rollout. Canary default: 5% traffic for 24 hours, expand to 20% for 48 hours with automated evaluation gates. Rollback triggers: safety violations above configured threshold (default 0.1% on sampled traffic), degradation in task performance beyond configurable delta, or unexpected latency regressions that breach p95 SLA by >25%.
- Auditing & evidence: We produce regression reports with sampling provenance, annotation results, and diffed metrics. Customers can request additional eval sets or provide gold-labeled prompts for targeted regression tests.

Notes: We'll include example metric dashboards and an exported eval report in the evidence matrix. For EU Act compliance we'll surface the post-market monitoring cadence (monthly summary + on-demand deeper sampling after major model changes).

Marissa
Principal Product, Governance & Evals
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 4 Apr 2027 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: StratusBank RFP — governance annex and technical questions

Adding supporting artifacts and a couple of compliance clarifications:
- Attached: SOC2_report_2026.pdf (redacted), evidence_matrix.csv (mapping of RFP questions to artifacts), KMS_certification_summary.pdf (key rotation and HSM notes).
- Data residency: For EU customers or controlled-data workloads, we recommend Private deployment. For US-only deployments, we can offer Dedicated capacity in a US region with retained audit logs per agreed retention window.
- EU AI Act: We recommend listing the deployment as high-risk in the annex and documenting mitigations (documentation, human oversight controls, testing and post-market monitoring). Legal will need to review final wording; I can prepare a short legal-friendly paragraph.

I'll attach the artifacts to the consolidated response.

Neha
Head of Compliance, Redwood Inference
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@stratusbank.com>, Tom Ellis <t.ellis@stratusbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00

Thanks for the patience — attached is our consolidated technical annex and evidence matrix (Redwood_StratusBank_Governance_Annex_Response_20270405.pdf, evidence_matrix.csv, operational_evals.md). Highlights:
- We recommend treating the loan-eligibility assistance integration as high-risk under the EU AI Act; enclosed are our design controls and post-market monitoring commitments.
- NIST AI RMF mapping included (identify/protect/detect/respond/recover) with pointers to artifacts.
- Evaluation & regression workflow: stratified sampling, nightly smoke checks, weekly full regression with human annotation, and canary rollout defaults (5% for 24h -> 20% for 48h). Rollback triggers are safety-violation thresholds and performance deltas as described in the annex.

Next steps:
- Proposed technical review call: Mon 12 Apr 2027 at 10:00am PT to walk Annex and evidence.
- If you confirm the high-risk designation, we'll provide the post-market monitoring schedule and a templated Article 13 technical documentation package for your legal team.

messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-02T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick feasibility ask after FinBound call — private deployment options

Team,

Follow-up from this morning's discovery with FinBound (Pooja Shah). High level: they want a pilot that runs sensitive payment data inside their VPC or on-prem colocation (cannot route raw traffic through hosted shared infra). They asked for two things before the procurement team will sign an MSA:

- A short feasibility summary showing architecture options (VPC peering / Transit / on-prem connector),
- A clear estimate of ops + engineering staffing required for a 3-month pilot, and any SLA exceptions we'd need to grant.

Key constraints from FinBound:
- No shared-hosted compute for production PII processing during pilot.
- KMS/HSM key material must be customer-controlled in their cloud HSM (Azure Key Vault/HSM).
- Audit logs must be retained for 12 months in their S3-like storage.
- Pilot must complete within a 90-day window; procurement expects a cost and resource plan by EOD Friday 9th.

Ask: can each of you reply with the following by EOD Thursday?
- Karthik: short tech approach + minimum infra (GPU count, network layout) and blockers. Attach sketch if helpful.
- Marissa: pricing baseline for a 3-month pilot + proposed commercial flex (pilot credits / SLA carve-outs).
- Neha: security evidence list and any additional gating controls (HSM/KMS, contract language we should surface).
- Ethan: SRE/ops headcount estimate and on-call rotations for the pilot.

I attached a quick whiteboard snapshot I sketched during the call (architecture_sketch.png). I'll consolidate and share to Pooja once we have inputs.

Thanks — Jonas
Jonas Weber
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com

Attachments: architecture_sketch.png (image stub)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-02T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick feasibility ask after FinBound call — private deployment options

Thanks Jonas — quick technical read:

Summary (short):
- Both VPC-hosted dedicated (customer VPC peering + private ENIs) and on-prem via a secure connector are feasible.
- Tradeoffs: VPC option is faster (2–4 weeks to stand up pilot infra), on-prem adds network/ops complexity (4–8 weeks) and a small SD-WAN or site-to-site VPN requirement.

Minimum infra (pilot, single region, modest throughput target):
- Serving fleet: 4x A100-class (or equivalent) instances for primary model serving (2x primary, 2x warm/failover) + 1x smaller instance for control-plane tasks.
- Storage: encrypted object store for model artifacts + KV cache persistence (customer-provided S3).
- Networking: Transit VPC preferred if multi-subnet; otherwise VPC peering + strict NACLs. For on-prem we recommend an eBPF-based connector with mutual TLS to our control plane.

Key blockers / items requiring product exceptions:
- HSM/KMS integration: we can support customer-managed keys, but we need an HSM integration plan (customer must expose a supported KMIP or cloud KMS endpoint).
- Kernel/quantization variants: some models we recommend quantize to reduce GPU footprint — may change perf profile; we should capture perf/regression test scope.
- Auditability: We can route logs to customer S3, but retention and log-shipping SLA need to be agreed.

Estimated time to ready (engineering + infra config only):
- VPC pilot: 2–4 weeks for infra + 1 week for integration testing.
- On-prem pilot: 4–8 weeks depending on network approvals and rack access.

I'll attach a one-page sketch (architecture_sketch.png) and can run a micro-benchmark on equivalent hardware if we get a list of model(s) / seq lengths.

Karthik
Staff Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood

Attachments: architecture_sketch.png (image stub)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-02T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick feasibility ask after FinBound call — private deployment options

Following up on cost / commercial:

Commercial baseline for a 3-month pilot (ballpark):
- VPC-hosted dedicated (customer VPC, reserved pilot cluster): ~$120k–$160k for infra + support (includes reserved GPU capacity with burst).
- On-prem connector (we ship control plane + edge connector): ~$90k–$140k for software + remote ops support (does not include customer rack/power).

Pilot concessions we can offer to accelerate procurement: 
- Pilot credit: 40% credit on the pilot invoice if a production contract (12+ months) is signed within 90 days.
- SLA: for pilot we propose an SLO of 95% availability with a documented escalation path to SRE; full 99.9% SLA kept for production only. Any stricter SLA for pilot will require dedicated standby coverage (additional cost).

Deal details: Deal ID FIN-2027-003. We should plan a short one-pager for procurement that lays out: scope, responsibilities (customer vs Redwood), pilot timeline, acceptance criteria, and evidence list (SOC2 + network diagram). I can draft that once tech/resourcing is confirmed.

Marissa
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-03T09:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick feasibility ask after FinBound call — private deployment options

Security checklist (high level) — attaching the formal checklist (security_checklist.pdf):

Must-have items before pilot kickoff:
- SOC 2 Type II report (latest) or equivalent attestation.
- Customer-managed KMS/HSM access pattern: confirm supported endpoints + test credential exchange.
- Network segmentation proof: firewall rules, egress restrictions, and IP allowlist for control-plane traffic.
- Audit logs: shipping to customer storage with 12-month retention; confirm format and retention policy.
- IAM controls: least-privilege service accounts and documented key rotation schedule.

Timeline: security review typically takes ~3 weeks from complete documentation; accelerated review possible but requires dedicated security reviewer (we can provide a prelim checklist this week).

Open questions for FinBound to answer (please include in the one-pager):
- Do they have existing HSM endpoint/KMIP support? Which cloud provider?
- Which team owns on-prem network approvals and do they allow outbound TLS to our control-plane endpoints?

Neha
Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference
neha@redwood.com

Attachments: security_checklist.pdf (document stub)

To: Pooja Shah <pooja@finbound.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, FinBound IT Procurement <itprocurement@finbound.io>

- Proposed pilot baseline: 4 A100-class serving units + control-plane node; secure log routing to your object store; customer-managed KMS.
- Commercial: Marissa will propose the detailed pilot quote (Deal ID FIN-2027-003). We can offer a pilot credit if you sign production within 90 days.
- Security: Neha attached the checklist. We will need SOC2 attestation and confirmation of the HSM/KMS endpoint. Security review ~3 weeks.

Requested from FinBound (please confirm by EOD next Tuesday):
1) KMS/HSM provider and endpoint details (or confirm cloud provider).
2) Network contact for on-prem approvals and whether outbound TLS to our control-plane is allowed.
3) Acceptance criteria for the pilot (sample prompt/payloads or perf targets).

Attached: architecture_sketch.png, security_checklist.pdf, pilot_scope_onepager.docx (draft one-pager — internal). We'll turn the one-pager into a customer-facing attachment after we get the above answers.

If this aligns, Marissa will follow with the pilot quote and I will schedule a short technical sync (30 minutes) with Karthik and your network team.

source.subject: Weekly: batch capacity scenario playback
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood-inference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>, jane.doe@acme.com
Cc: finops@acme.com, cloudops@cloudscale.com
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly: batch capacity scenario playback

Team / Acme folks —

Attaching the latest pipeline export and two sizing scenarios for Dedicated GPU capacity tied to Acme's forecasted usage. Goal for this thread: converge on the scenario we present to Acme on Tuesday so Sales/Infra/Finance are aligned before the exec call.

High level:
- Attached: 20270403_acme_pipeline_forecast.csv (pipeline by close month) and gpu_sizing_scenarios_v3.pdf (three scenarios: conservative, baseline, aggressive).
- Key ask for Infra (Karthik): confirm which scenario matches our available Dedicated slots for May/June and if burst credits cover the aggressive case.
- Key ask for FinOps (Monica): provide monthly cost delta for baseline vs conservative and a quick note on crediting flexibility.
- Sales (Ben): set expectation for Acme on a preferred default and fallback.

Please reply with short bullets by EOD so I can fold into the slide deck. If we need a quick sync, suggest 30m at 1pm PT — I can host.

Thanks,
Ethan
Ethan Park
Senior Sales Lead, Redwood Inference
attachments: [20270403_acme_pipeline_forecast.csv (CSV), gpu_sizing_scenarios_v3.pdf (PDF)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood-inference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>, jane.doe@acme.com
Cc: cloudops@cloudscale.com
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly: batch capacity scenario playback

Ethan —

Thanks for the packet. Quick infra read:
- Conservative: fits comfortably within existing Dedicated pools (we can map to Pool-A & Pool-C). No additional procurement required.
- Baseline: requires reserving an additional 8 GPUs in Pool-B for early June. That is doable with a 2-week lead but reduces burst headroom.
- Aggressive: exceeds reserve capacity for June by ~22 GPUs. We can bridge with burst credits for up to ~12 GPUs for short windows, but sustained demand would need a short-term Dedicated add-on or shifting some load to lower-cost quantized variants.

Questions / notes:
1) Which months in the forecast are the high-concurrency peaks? I see a close in late May for 40% of volume — if that's concentrated, burst helps; if spread, we need reserves.
2) If Acme is willing to accept a 5-10% latency degradation, we can route some inference to quantized instances and reduce required GPUs by ~15%.

I can confirm exact pool mappings if you share the final chosen scenario by EOD Monday.

-Karthik

source.subject: Gridly: surge 504s and timeouts - suspected cache/batching deadlock (urgent)
source.body:
From: Carlos Ramirez <carlos.ramirez@gridly.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 22:14:00 -0700
Subject: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Monica,

We're seeing a sustained spike in 504s and client timeouts across our main product endpoint (text-generate) between 21:45 and 22:05 PDT. Error rate peaked at ~14% for 30s and stayed above 3% for ~10 minutes. Latency p50 jumped from 120ms to 2.4s and p95 to 6.1s. Our users experienced heavy retrying which worsened the queue/backlog.

Attached is a short log snippet and the request ids. Can you advise immediately? This is impacting multiple enterprise customers who are in the middle of scheduled processing.

-Carlos
Principal SRE, Gridly

--
attachments: incident_snapshot_20270331.log (text)

---- Reply (Monica Patel -> Gridly + internal) ----
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Ramirez <carlos.ramirez@gridly.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 22:29:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Carlos—thank you for the heads-up and the logs. We’re investigating on call now. Initial ask: can you paste a couple of example request-ids from the spike? That will help us correlate with our service traces.

Ethan/Hannah/Rishi — looping you in. ETA on triage? My suspicion from initial traces: abnormal queueing + a KV-cache eviction/coordination issue that amplifies latency under bursty traffic (we rolled a batching tweak last week). If true, short-term mitigation might be: 1) disable the new batch coalescer variant, or 2) force the route to the fallback model pool.

I’m keeping Gridly updated; they’ll need timely follow-up and a postmortem.

-Monica
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---- Internal thread (Ethan Park -> internal) ----
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 22:34:45 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Monica/Carlos — I pulled the traces for request ids CR-7721 / CR-7723 from the logs Carlos attached. Observations:
- There are long stalls at the batching coordinator when prefix batches are being assembled (workers wait on a cache read that blocks on a lock).
- KV-cache miss amplification: under high concurrency the eviction routine is triggering synchronous metadata updates which serialize many requests.
- The coalescer change deployed 3 days ago introduced an optimistic lock around the cache metadata (to avoid duplicate writes). That lock path appears to deadlock with the eviction path under certain timing, producing ~500-700ms stalls per request then cascading queue growth.

Immediate mitigations I recommend:
1) Revert the coalescer optimistic-lock change on the canary cluster (rollback flag already prepared).
2) Temporarily reduce max batch size from 64 -> 16 to reduce contention.
3) Enable short-circuit fallback: route new requests to the prior-serving variant for Gridly only.

I can put the rollback in motion with ops help. If we need exec-level coordination for a cross-customer rollback window, Rishi can advise.

Ethan
Senior Solutions Engineer

---- Internal (Rishi Malhotra -> exec + ops) ----
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:08:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

All—good triage notes. We need to move fast: Gridly is an active pilot (D-8421) and visible to our partnerships team. Hannah, please coordinate with infra on an immediate rollback of the canary config and a per-customer routing change for Gridly. Monica, keep Carlos and their CTO updated every 30 minutes.

If rollback isn't safe within 30m, escalate to me and I’ll engage product/engineering leadership for a controlled brief maintenance window (we can offer an SLA credit if needed). Also request impact metrics (tokens, qps) for the spike so we can quantify credit.

Rishi
VP, Sales Operations

---- Internal (Hannah Schmitt -> Ops + Gridly update) ----
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: infra-ops@redwood.com; Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:24:33 -0700
Subject: Action: rollback coalescer change + routing override for Gridly (INF-7293)

Team—taking action now per triage: 1) rolling back coalescer optimistic-lock change in canary and scheduling rollout to prod after verification; 2) setting routing override so Gridly traffic hits the pre-change model pool for the next 2 hours; 3) reducing default batch size for Gridly to 16.

I'll post updates in the Jira ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-7293. Expect the rollback to complete in ~12 minutes; I will confirm when done.

Hannah
Product Ops Manager

---- Final update to Gridly (Monica Patel -> Carlos Ramirez) ----
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Ramirez <carlos.ramirez@gridly.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:42:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Carlos — quick update: we rolled back the recent coalescer change on canary and applied an immediate routing override for Gridly. We've also reduced your per-tenant batch size temporarily. Early metrics show error rate down to ~0.6% and p95 latency back under 700ms. We're continuing to monitor through the afternoon and will provide a full postmortem.

If you need an incident bridge for the rest of the day, I can set one up. Also attaching a short SLA credit request doc we prepared in case you want to start internal approvals.

Thanks for the fast signal — that helped us find the deadlock path quickly.

-Monica
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--
attachments: sla-credit-request.docx (docx)

---- Notes + next steps ----
- Created Jira INF-7293 (tracking bug in coalescer + eviction paths).
- Postmortem to include: timeline, root cause (optimistic-lock + eviction serialization), mitigation steps (rollback + batch-size cap + cache metadata async changes), and customer impact (tokens affected + SLA credit calc).
- Monica to share postmortem with Gridly and partnerships.

End of thread.
