Attached is a short log snippet and the request ids. Can you advise immediately? This is impacting multiple enterprise customers who are in the middle of scheduled processing.

-Carlos
Principal SRE, Gridly

--
attachments: incident_snapshot_20270331.log (text)

---- Reply (Monica Patel -> Gridly + internal) ----
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Ramirez <carlos.ramirez@gridly.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 22:29:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Carlos—thank you for the heads-up and the logs. We’re investigating on call now. Initial ask: can you paste a couple of example request-ids from the spike? That will help us correlate with our service traces.

Ethan/Hannah/Rishi — looping you in. ETA on triage? My suspicion from initial traces: abnormal queueing + a KV-cache eviction/coordination issue that amplifies latency under bursty traffic (we rolled a batching tweak last week). If true, short-term mitigation might be: 1) disable the new batch coalescer variant, or 2) force the route to the fallback model pool.

I’m keeping Gridly updated; they’ll need timely follow-up and a postmortem.

-Monica
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---- Internal thread (Ethan Park -> internal) ----
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 22:34:45 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Monica/Carlos — I pulled the traces for request ids CR-7721 / CR-7723 from the logs Carlos attached. Observations:
- There are long stalls at the batching coordinator when prefix batches are being assembled (workers wait on a cache read that blocks on a lock).
- KV-cache miss amplification: under high concurrency the eviction routine is triggering synchronous metadata updates which serialize many requests.
- The coalescer change deployed 3 days ago introduced an optimistic lock around the cache metadata (to avoid duplicate writes). That lock path appears to deadlock with the eviction path under certain timing, producing ~500-700ms stalls per request then cascading queue growth.

Immediate mitigations I recommend:
1) Revert the coalescer optimistic-lock change on the canary cluster (rollback flag already prepared).
2) Temporarily reduce max batch size from 64 -> 16 to reduce contention.
3) Enable short-circuit fallback: route new requests to the prior-serving variant for Gridly only.

I can put the rollback in motion with ops help. If we need exec-level coordination for a cross-customer rollback window, Rishi can advise.

Ethan
Senior Solutions Engineer

---- Internal (Rishi Malhotra -> exec + ops) ----
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:08:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

All—good triage notes. We need to move fast: Gridly is an active pilot (D-8421) and visible to our partnerships team. Hannah, please coordinate with infra on an immediate rollback of the canary config and a per-customer routing change for Gridly. Monica, keep Carlos and their CTO updated every 30 minutes.

If rollback isn't safe within 30m, escalate to me and I’ll engage product/engineering leadership for a controlled brief maintenance window (we can offer an SLA credit if needed). Also request impact metrics (tokens, qps) for the spike so we can quantify credit.

Rishi
VP, Sales Operations

---- Internal (Hannah Schmitt -> Ops + Gridly update) ----
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: infra-ops@redwood.com; Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:24:33 -0700
Subject: Action: rollback coalescer change + routing override for Gridly (INF-7293)

Team—taking action now per triage: 1) rolling back coalescer optimistic-lock change in canary and scheduling rollout to prod after verification; 2) setting routing override so Gridly traffic hits the pre-change model pool for the next 2 hours; 3) reducing default batch size for Gridly to 16.

I'll post updates in the Jira ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-7293. Expect the rollback to complete in ~12 minutes; I will confirm when done.

Hannah
Product Ops Manager

---- Final update to Gridly (Monica Patel -> Carlos Ramirez) ----
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Ramirez <carlos.ramirez@gridly.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 00:42:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 504s - major latency spike during peak window

Carlos — quick update: we rolled back the recent coalescer change on canary and applied an immediate routing override for Gridly. We've also reduced your per-tenant batch size temporarily. Early metrics show error rate down to ~0.6% and p95 latency back under 700ms. We're continuing to monitor through the afternoon and will provide a full postmortem.

If you need an incident bridge for the rest of the day, I can set one up. Also attaching a short SLA credit request doc we prepared in case you want to start internal approvals.

Thanks for the fast signal — that helped us find the deadlock path quickly.

-Monica
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--
attachments: sla-credit-request.docx (docx)

---- Notes + next steps ----
- Created Jira INF-7293 (tracking bug in coalescer + eviction paths).
- Postmortem to include: timeline, root cause (optimistic-lock + eviction serialization), mitigation steps (rollback + batch-size cap + cache metadata async changes), and customer impact (tokens affected + SLA credit calc).
- Monica to share postmortem with Gridly and partnerships.

End of thread.

source.subject: Renewal audit: Lighthouse performance attestation and term conversation
messages:
From: Alex Liu <alex@lighthouse.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@lighthouse.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal audit: performance attestation and next steps

Ethan —

Thanks for the work during the pilot. We finished an internal pass on the PoC logs and there are a few items that raised concerns for our SRE and ML infra teams:

- 95th percentile latency spikes during sustained traffic (see sample attached metrics)
- Inconsistent throughput when sequence length variance increases
- Two tokenization edge-cases that caused retries in our pipeline

We value Redwood's feature set but CloudPulse has approached us with a side-by-side benchmark showing consistently lower p95 and a lower unit cost for our baseline throughput. Procurement asked me to request formal attestation from your side: a short benchmark run that mirrors our production mix (10–20k reqs/min, mixed seq lengths) and a written summary we can share with our finance and platform teams.

Can you provide: (1) a 48–72 hour benchmark window we can inspect, (2) a log summary, and (3) any concessions (SLA or term) you’re willing to discuss if results match your published profiles? We need to circle back by next week to keep timelines aligned with our renewal.

Appreciate a clear timeline — procurement has asked for something by Apr 2.

Best,
Alex Liu
Head of Platform Partnerships
Lighthouse AI

Attachment: lighthouse_poc_log_summary.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)
From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Liu <alex@lighthouse.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal audit: performance attestation and next steps

Alex —

Thanks for the note and the attached summary. I’m looping in Karthik (SE lead) and Marissa (Customer Success) so we can mobilize quickly. Short answer: yes — we can run a mirrored benchmark and provide a concise attestation plus a targeted proposal if the results show parity with our claims.

Proposed immediate plan:
1) Redwood runs a 48-hour mirror of your production mix starting Friday 4/2 (we’ll coordinate traffic window and IPs).
2) We’ll share live dashboards and a post-run attestation doc within 24 hours of completion.
3) If metrics meet the agreed thresholds, we’ll deliver a written term proposal (SLA + uplift pricing) for your procurement review.

Can you confirm the preferred window (we suggested Fri/Sat to avoid weekday noise) and the primary contact for our infra team to coordinate network/ingress?

— Ethan
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Lighthouse benchmark & save plan (draft)

Team —

Draft playbook for the Lighthouse mirror and potential save offer. I’m preparing a one-page save letter and a technical runbook. Key checkpoints below; I’ll attach the working doc after this note.

Runbook checklist:
- Confirm traffic profile CSV from Alex (sequence length distribution, request mix)
- Allocate dedicated GPU pool with 2x baseline capacity for the 48-hour run
- Enable KV cache and prefix caching to replicate production cache warmup
- Capture token-level latency + p95, p99, and tail distribution (store raw traces)
- Run a short adversarial batch to reproduce tokenization edge-case

Commercial levers to offer if performance aligns:
- 12 month committed term with 20% reserved capacity uplift on current baseline
- Step-down unit pricing tied to committed volume (10% discount at 6M tokens/mo commit)
- A temporary SLA concession: 99.9% p95 latency over committed traffic for 3 months post-renewal (technical fine print to follow)
- Offer a 30-day parallel proof window with no additional charges

Attachments: save_plan_v1.docx (DOCX, 240KB)

I’ll be on the call Friday at 08:30 PT to align with Alex, Karthik — please add anything urgent to the doc before then.

— Marissa
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference
From: Alex Liu <alex@lighthouse.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@lighthouse.ai>
Date: Fri, 1 Apr 2027 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal audit: performance attestation and next steps

Ethan / team —

Thanks for the rapid turnaround and the proposal outline. We ran a quick side-by-side internally with CloudPulse during the week; their published setup delivered ~12% better p95 under sustained traffic for the specific model family we use. I’m attaching the evaluation spreadsheet CloudPulse provided so you can see the comparison they shared with us.

Two asks from procurement and platform leadership:
1) Formalized counter-offer by Apr 2 that matches or meaningfully narrows the p95 gap and explains the remediation approach for the tokenization retries.
2) If Redwood cannot match the perf delta, we need a migration mitigation plan (pricing for a one-time data export/parallel run and an engineering SLT commitment window) so we can assess total cost of migration.

Time is tight on the renewal calendar. If you can get a crisp written counter by end of day tomorrow, we’ll present to procurement on Monday.

Best,
Alex
Head of Platform Partnerships
Lighthouse AI

Attachment: cloudpulse_eval.xlsx (XLSX, 95KB)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Liu <alex@lighthouse.ai>, Procurement Team <procure@lighthouse.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 1 Apr 2027 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal audit: performance attestation and next steps — Redwood counter

Alex —

Appreciate the direct signal and the CloudPulse file. We committed to a short-but-transparent path and here is our counter in writing. High level summary first, then the operational guarantees and migration contingency.

Summary offer:
- Term: 12 months with a committed reserved baseline (matches existing committed throughput) and a 20% reserved capacity uplift for burst tolerance.
- Pricing: 10% discount applied to token rates on the committed baseline; overage at standard rates with a volume-tiering step at 6M tokens/mo.
- Performance SLO: 99.9% of requests served at p95 latency <= your baseline target for committed traffic over a 30-day live evaluation window (we’ll run the 48-hour mirror this weekend and then a 30-day live parallel on a mirrored shard).
- Technical remediation: dedicated tokenization patch and validation harness deployed during the mirror; we will run a targeted adversarial tokenization test and commit to a follow-up patch rollout within 7 days if we reproduce the retries.
- Migration contingency: if results do not meet the SLO within the agreed evaluation window, Redwood will provide a one-time migration support package including a parallel export runner (up to 72 hours), engineering handover, and three priority SE support days at no extra charge.

Operational notes / next steps:
1) We will start the agreed 48-hour mirror on Sat 4/3 at 00:00 PT — infra team will share ingress IPs and a dashboard link to your SRE.
2) Post-run attestation doc and raw traces delivered within 24 hours of run completion.
3) Legal will issue a one-page amendment for the temporary SLA concession for signature when you confirm acceptance.

Links: live dashboard (https://drive.redwoodinc/reports/lighthouse/poc-results), HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/45219/DL-2024-1729)

We believe this narrows the gap while giving Lighthouse instrumentation and an exit path if necessary. Happy to jump on a 30-minute call now if you want to walk through specifics before procurement reviews.

— Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference
